  Fix: Add `customize.toml` declaring `activation_steps_prepend/append`, `persistent_facts` (with the BMad default glob), and the three high-value path scalars: `test_report_template_path`, `output_formats_path`, `scoring_rules_path` (each empty-default-falls-through-to-bundled per skf-brief-skill's pattern); add a §3 customization resolver step to `## On Activation`.

- **[A2] Reference files missing language headers** | severity: high | src/skf-test-skill/references/*.md
  Issue: Per principles file ("Each carved-out file uses `{communication_language}`") and confirmed by grep, zero of the eight reference files in this skill carry a `{communication_language}` header — compare skf-brief-skill/references/gather-intent.md:13 (`<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->`). On context compaction (SKILL.md drops mid-flow), a stage loaded standalone has no signal to honor the language. Inline workflow-rules at SKILL.md:34 doesn't survive a compaction that drops SKILL.md.
  Fix: Stamp each `references/*.md` with the canonical header at the top of the file (after frontmatter): `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. Test report content in {document_output_language}. -->`.

- **[A3] `## Conventions` block deviates from the canonical form** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md:12-18
  Issue: The skill adds a second `references/` bullet describing `nextStepFile` chaining and conflates two concerns. The canonical block from principles-file ("from `bmad-prfaq/SKILL.md`") is five lines; references-folder description belongs to a workflow-conventions doc, not the path-resolution block. skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:16-22 matches the canonical exactly.
  Fix: Trim to the canonical 5-bullet block and lift the `references/` chaining description into the existing `## Workflow Rules` section or `## Stages` preamble.

- **[A4] Carved-out reference files repeat heavy boilerplate** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/references/init.md:18-35, detect-mode.md:9-19, coverage-check.md:14-22, coherence-check.md:15-23, external-validators.md:12-20, score.md:15-23, report.md:25-34
  Issue: Every step file repeats `STEP GOAL`, `## Rules`, `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` with a "CRITICAL" warning, and a `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` footer. This is the workflow-rules-table-restated-in-every-step pattern: skf-brief-skill's references run lean. Cumulative cost across 7 step files is ~150 lines of identical scaffolding the LLM already knows from SKILL.md's `## Workflow Rules`.
  Fix: Drop `## Rules`/`## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headers and the CRITICAL footer. Keep step goal + work. The chain note is one sentence at the end, not a 7-line subsection.

- **[A5] "Auto-Proceed" subsections duplicate the chain instruction** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/references/init.md:223-233, detect-mode.md:89-100, coverage-check.md:331-342, coherence-check.md:223-234, external-validators.md:188-198, score.md:250-261, report.md:253-285
  Issue: Each step ends with both an "Auto-Proceed" subsection (`#### Menu Handling Logic` + `#### EXECUTION RULES`) and a `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE`. Both restate "update frontmatter then load `{nextStepFile}`." On a workflow with frontmatter `nextStepFile: 'X.md'` already declared, two restatements are clutter.
  Fix: Collapse into one closing line: `Update stepsCompleted, then load and execute {nextStepFile}.` Drop "Menu Handling Logic" / "EXECUTION RULES" / "CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE" headers entirely from non-interactive auto-proceed steps.

- **[A6] `health-check.md` carries a full step file for a 2-line probe** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/references/health-check.md
  Issue: 25 lines, frontmatter, `## STEP GOAL`, `## Rules`, `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` to (1) probe two paths and (2) execute the first that exists. report.md:255-264 already declares the same probe order and HALT diagnostic — the dispatcher in report.md §7 could chain directly to the resolved file without a passthrough step.
  Fix: Either inline the probe into report.md §7 (resolve `{nextStepFile}` to the actual shared path before chain), or shrink health-check.md to ≤6 lines.

- **[A7] `## Workflow Rules` includes a no-op directive** | severity: low | src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md:32
  Issue: "If any instruction references a subprocess or tool you lack, achieve the outcome in your main context thread" — the LLM does this by default; the directive's only effect is to suggest main-thread fallback is quality-equivalent to ast-grep, which it's not.
  Fix: Drop the bullet; tier-aware downgrade logic already lives in detect-mode.md and coverage-check.md.

- **[A8] References codes (B1/B2/.../M5/S8) appear without a glossary** | severity: low | src/skf-test-skill/references/{init,score,report,coherence-check,scoring-rules}.md
  Issue: `per B2`, `(B4)`, `(H4)`, `(S6)`, `M5`, `S9`, `H1` appear inline with no anchor explaining where they're defined. These look like requirement IDs from an external spec but the spec isn't referenced.
  Fix: Either drop the codes inline or add a brief `references/spec-ids.md` glossary mapping each ID to its origin.

### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)

- **[D1] Markdown-fence balance, language-tag scan, and table column drift could be one script** | severity: high | src/skf-test-skill/references/coherence-check.md:44-86
  Issue: §2.2/§2.3/§2.6 hand the LLM bash recipes and a Python snippet to count fences, detect bare opening fences, and check table column alignment. These are pure deterministic operations — given identical SKILL.md, always produces identical findings. LLM cost ~500-800 tokens per invocation to "run" the recipe.
  Fix: Author `scripts/scan-skill-md-structure.py` returning JSON `{unbalanced_fences, bare_opening_fences[], table_drift[]}`. Step prompt loads JSON and converts to findings.

- **[D2] Required-section presence check is grep-fast** | severity: high | src/skf-test-skill/references/coherence-check.md:36-42
  Issue: §2.1 has the LLM run `grep -n "^## {section}" SKILL.md` for each of 3 synonym sets. With 4-6 synonyms per set, that's 12-18 grep invocations LLM-orchestrated. A script returns the satisfied/unsatisfied verdict in one call.
  Fix: Extend `scan-skill-md-structure.py` (D1) with a `--required-sections` mode emitting `{description: {satisfied, matched_synonym}, usage: {...}, api_surface: {...}}`.

- **[D3] Exports-in-Usage cross-grep is per-export sequential** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/references/coherence-check.md:65-67
  Issue: §2.4 walks `exports[].name` and runs `grep -c "{export.name}" SKILL.md` per export. For a skill with 50+ exports this is 50 LLM-orchestrated grep calls. The subagent has SKILL.md content already (from §1); a single subagent invocation with the exports list returns all misses at once.
  Fix: Fold into the existing inventory subagent (§1) — return `exports_used_in_usage_section: [{name, count}]` alongside the inventory.

- **[D4] compute-score.py has no unit tests** | severity: high | src/skf-test-skill/scripts/compute-score.py
  Issue: `scripts/tests/` does not exist. compute-score.py centralizes the floor logic that drives correctness (false-PASS catastrophic per scoring-rules.md:188). Without tests, future tweaks silently risk breaking equivalence-class arithmetic. M3 fixture matrix at scoring-rules.md:113-141 *declares* fixtures (`test/fixtures/compute-score-contract.json`) that don't exist alongside the script.
  Fix: Create `scripts/tests/test_compute_score.py` seeded from the M3 matrix's 4 equivalence classes (A/B/C/D) + INCONCLUSIVE floor cases + threshold cap. Add an entry to top-level `package.json` `test:python`.

- **[D5] compute-score.py lacks argparse / --help** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/scripts/compute-score.py:289-298
  Issue: Script uses raw `sys.argv[1]` with a one-line Usage print. Per repo's agentic-design convention, every script self-documents via argparse so an agent can `python3 compute-score.py --help` to discover the input schema.
  Fix: Replace the sys.argv block with argparse: `--json-input` (positional or stdin), `--help` showing the input schema and an example.

- **[D6] Subagent prompts return JSON without consistent "ONLY return X"** | severity: medium | src/skf-test-skill/references/coverage-check.md:46-72; references/coherence-check.md:118-145
  Issue: §1 of coverage-check has the discipline ("ONLY return this compact JSON inventory — no prose"). Later sections (§1b cross-check, contextual coherence §3-§4) delegate to subagents without re-asserting it. Principles-file failure mode: "Subagent prompt without explicit return format → verbose prose."
  Fix: Audit every subagent invocation; add `Return ONLY <schema>. No prose, no commentary, no markdown.` to each.

- **[D7] External-validator probes run sequentially when they're independent** | severity: low | src/skf-test-skill/references/external-validators.md:53-130
  Issue: §2 (skill-check) and §3 (tessl) are independent shells. Currently told to run in order; running both probes + both validations in a single parallel-Bash batch would halve wall-clock.
  Fix: Add a note at §2 entry: "Run sections 2 and 3 in parallel — issue both probes and both validations in a single multi-call message; outputs are independent."

### Customization Surface (customize.toml)

- **[C1] No customize.toml — opt in** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/
  Issue: No customize.toml file exists. Workflow hardcodes three asset/template paths and has zero `persistent_facts` surface despite being a quality grader where org-specific severity rules are common.
  Fix: Ship a customize.toml mirroring skf-brief-skill's shape: `activation_steps_prepend/append = []`, `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`, three path scalars — `test_report_template_path`, `output_formats_path`, `scoring_rules_path` (each empty-string default). Wire resolver in SKILL.md's `## On Activation` (insert as step 3).

- **[C2] Threshold is hardcoded; org-customizable override is natural** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/references/scoring-rules.md:5, score.md:31
  Issue: `Pass threshold: 80%` is locked. Different orgs run different bars. The scoring script already accepts `threshold` in JSON input. Workflow text mentions "custom threshold override from workflow input" (score.md:31) without exposing the override path.
  Fix: Add `default_threshold = 80` scalar to customize.toml. score.md §1 reads `{workflow.default_threshold}` and passes it into compute-score JSON. CLI override still wins.

- **[C3] `on_complete` hook would let pipelines snap onto results** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/
  Issue: Workflow ends by writing result-JSON files and dispatching health-check. Downstream automator wanting slack/dashboard/CI hook would have to fork.
  Fix: Add optional `on_complete = ""` scalar; report.md §4c invokes the command with `--result-path=...` after atomic write.

### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)

- **[E1] No "Exit Codes" / "Result Contract (Headless)" block in SKILL.md** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md:50-67
  Issue: The Invocation Contract table mentions `headless_mode` and "All gates auto-resolve" but doesn't enumerate exit codes the way skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-105 does. Headless exit codes ARE defined inside the workflow (report.md:243-251: 0/2/3/4) — they should be surfaced at the contract level so an automator never has to read step files.
  Fix: Add `## Exit Codes` and `## Result Contract (Headless)` sections to SKILL.md per skf-brief-skill's template; promote the report.md:243-251 exit-code table + the `skf-test-skill-result-{run_id}.json` schema sketch up to SKILL.md.

- **[E2] No `--allow-workspace-drift` exposure at SKILL.md** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md:50-58
  Issue: The flag governs an important workflow-level branch (`pass-with-drift` verdict, refusing to recommend export) but is introduced only at init.md:36. A user or automator scanning SKILL.md has no signal it exists.
  Fix: Add a `**Flags**` row to the Invocation Contract table at SKILL.md:50: `--allow-workspace-drift` — bypass workspace-HEAD-vs-pinned guard; PASS verdicts become `pass-with-drift` and refuse export-skill chain.

- **[E3] No first-time `forge-tier.yaml` missing recovery** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/references/init.md:128-132
  Issue: When `{sidecarFile}` is missing, the step HALTs with "Please run the **setup** workflow first." For an automator running test-skill in a fresh CI shell, that's a dead-end.
  Fix: Document an opt-in `--tier=<Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep>` flag at init.md §1 that bypasses sidecar requirement, setting `detected_tier` directly. Sidecar missing + no `--tier` → keep current HALT.

- **[E4] Discovery testing's `--discovery-catalog=all` recipe is underspecified** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md:89-96
  Issue: §4b.0 already handles `catalog_size < 2` by skipping. But the `--discovery-catalog=all` escape hatch at line 96 doesn't say what counts as "the broader pool".
  Fix: Two-line elaboration: "When the flag is set, source candidates from `{project-root}/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` AND `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/**/agent.md` (their first-line description). Pool size = union of both globs."

- **[E5] No `--skip-health-check` / `--no-discovery` for fast CI runs** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md:85-138, 253-285
  Issue: Discovery testing and the health-check dispatch are both mandatory. For a CI matrix running test-skill across 50 skills, both dominate wall-clock. An automator running test-skill purely for the score doesn't need either.
  Fix: Two optional flags: `--no-discovery` (skip §4b, log Info note); `--no-health-check` (skip §7 dispatch, exit at §6b). Default behavior unchanged.

- **[E6] Result contract JSON schema isn't pinned in a shared schema file** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md:156-162
  Issue: The contract's shape is described in prose. A downstream consumer wanting to type the JSON has to scrape the prose. The skill references `{outputContractSchema}` at line 10 pointing to `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md` — but test-skill's payload-specific schema isn't visibly carved out.
  Fix: Either reference the existing shared schema explicitly (with a snippet showing test-skill's specific fields) or carve `assets/result-contract.schema.json` and have report.md §4c reference it.

## Deterministic Summary

- **path-standards:** 4 findings, all high. Two `_bmad/skf/` paths in report.md:8, 96 lack `{project-root}` prefix (existing prose says "NOT relative to this step file" but the scanner correctly flags the bare reference). Two `../` literals in coherence-check.md:122, 147 — inside doc examples of file paths the workflow scans (a legitimate use of `../` as the *subject* of detection); rephrasing to `parent-directory-prefix` would clear both without changing semantics.
- **scripts:** 3 findings (1 high, 2 medium). `scripts/tests/` missing entirely; `compute-score.py` lacks argparse + has no unit tests. Single Python script, 1 file, 0 lint issues.
- **workflow-integrity:** 0 issues, all 7 H2 sections present, valid frontmatter, simple-workflow classification matches inline-stages-table reality. (Note: stage_summary.total_stages reads `0` — the prepass keys on `## Stages` direct-child stages, not `references/`-folder enumeration; behavior is correct.)
- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md 67 lines / ~865 tokens — well under the ~130-line ceiling. References vary: `report.md` 290 lines / 3995 tokens and `coverage-check.md` 347 lines / 5323 tokens are the heaviest; both in single-purpose ≤5000 token range but lean against it. Zero waste patterns detected, zero back-references — strong.
- **execution-deps:** 0 issues — no inferable hard/soft dependency graph because chaining is encoded through frontmatter `nextStepFile`, which the prepass doesn't parse. Chain is linear (init → detect-mode → coverage → coherence → external → score → report → health-check) and correctly serializable.

## Strengths (preserve)

- Three-state PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE gate with a centralized minimum-evidence floor (scoring-rules.md:175-188) — exactly the failsafe principles-file calls out for a grader skill.
- The deterministic scoring script (scripts/compute-score.py) implements the floor centrally, with a clear "Never override the script's `result` value" instruction at score.md:110.
- M3 equivalence-class matrix at scoring-rules.md:103-150 — redistribution math captured as data, not prose, with 26 cells reducing to 4 classes. Pinning fixtures by class is the right testing strategy.
- Subagent inventory contract at coverage-check.md:78-94 — parent-side schema validation + ground-truth spot-check is exactly the "ONLY return X + verify" discipline principles-file demands. (Apply consistently — see D6.)
- Source-Access-Protocol five-state waterfall (source-access-protocol.md:74-103) — handles the local/provenance-map/metadata/remote/docs-only continuum without confidence inflation; `analysis_confidence` propagated downstream.
- Run-id + flock (init.md:184-186) — per-run identifier + per-skill test lock makes the workflow safe under CI concurrency.
- `pass-with-drift` (score.md:158-170, report.md:158, 228-229, 247) — the test result records that the verdict cannot be trusted for export, and next-workflow is forced to `update-skill`. The kind of design-rationale-as-mechanism principles-file rewards.

## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)

1. **Ship customize.toml + reference-file language headers (A1, A2, C1, C2)** — effort: low. Unblocks org adoption (forks today, configurable tomorrow), brings the skill in line with the Excellent peer's shape, adds the persistent_facts surface where org-specific severity policies will naturally live. Resolves 4 findings.

2. **Carve `scripts/scan-skill-md-structure.py` + add compute-score.py tests (D1, D2, D3, D4, D5)** — effort: medium. Replaces ~5 LLM-orchestrated grep/awk recipes per coverage+coherence run with a single script call (token savings: several thousand per invocation at large skills) and pins the scoring math against the M3 fixture matrix. Resolves 5 findings.

3. **Tighten reference-file boilerplate + lift contract to SKILL.md (A4, A5, A6, A7, E1, E2)** — effort: medium. Cuts ~150 lines of repeated step-file ceremony, drops the redundant `health-check.md` passthrough, surfaces exit codes + `--allow-workspace-drift` at the SKILL.md contract level where automators look. Resolves 6 findings.

content.content: Updated task #2 status
Updated task #3 status

1	# Quality Findings: skf-quick-skill
2	
3	**Grade:** Good
4	**Narrative:** This skill is structurally well-architected and intelligence-placement-aware — sophisticated probe-order helper resolution, ref-aware source reading, explicit parallel-fetch directives, a stable Exit Codes map with paired HARD-HALT envelope contracts, a Batch Mode with per-target boundary events, and a Repo-Shape Sniff that gracefully degrades non-library targets. The primary gap to Excellent is one runtime path bug (`{forger_root}` does not resolve — `preferences.yaml` will never be read), a missing `customize.toml` despite at least three lift-worthy paths (template, registries data, batch output), and an exit-code/envelope hole on the ecosystem-check [I] Install / [A] Abort branches.
5	
6	## Broken (critical/high)
7	
8	- **{forger_root} variable does not resolve** | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116
9	  Issue: `{forger_root}` is not defined anywhere in the module (`module.yaml` declares `sidecar_path`, not `forger_root`; no other skill references `forger_root`). The line `Read \`{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml\` and \`{forger_root}/preferences.yaml\` in parallel` therefore reads a literal `{forger_root}/preferences.yaml` path that doesn't exist, so `headless_mode` resolution falls through to the default `false`. Headless invocations via `preferences.yaml` will silently fail; only `--headless`/`-H` arg-based activation works.
10	  Fix: Replace `{forger_root}` with `{sidecar_path}` (matches `skf-create-skill/references/load-brief.md:4` and `skf-forger/SKILL.md:54` pattern). Also extend On-Activation §1 config read to include `sidecar_path` in the resolved fields list (currently absent).
11	
12	- **Ecosystem-check [I] Install and [A] Abort exit silently — no exit code, no result envelope** | src/skf-quick-skill/references/ecosystem-check.md:62-63
13	  Issue: Both terminal branches "Display install instructions, then end workflow" and "Display 'Compilation cancelled.', then end workflow" omit any exit code or HARD HALT envelope. Headless automators that hit an ecosystem match and the gate auto-resolves to `[P]` proceed are fine, but human-driven [I]/[A] paths return with no envelope on stderr, no `quick-skill-result-latest.json` on disk (since `{skill_package}` is unknown at this phase), and exit code 0 implying success — contradicting the SKILL.md "Exit Codes" promise that "Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable, documented code."
14	  Fix: Add an exit code for `ecosystem-cancelled` (or fold into `user-cancelled` at a new code 2 / 6 — pick one), and emit the HARD HALT stderr envelope per SKILL.md "Result Contract on HARD HALT" with `phase: "ecosystem-check"`, `skill_package: null`, before the workflow ends. Update the Exit Codes table accordingly.
15	
16	## Findings by Dimension
17	
18	### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)
19	
20	- **[A1] Language-header style inconsistent with Excellent peer** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/references/*.md (top of each file)
21	  Issue: Every stage opens with a visible body line `Communicate with the user in \`{communication_language}\`.` while the Excellent peer (`skf-brief-skill/references/analyze-target.md:9`) uses an invisible `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->` HTML comment that doesn't render to the user but still seeds the LLM. Stages that produce written artifacts also need `{document_output_language}` (compile, write-and-validate, finalize handle this; others should too where appropriate).
22	  Fix: Replace the body-line directives with HTML-comment headers; add `{document_output_language}` mention to the comment in stages that emit user-facing prose.
23	
24	- **[A2] Ecosystem-check is over-ceremonied for the work it does** | severity: medium | src/skf-quick-skill/references/ecosystem-check.md
25	  Issue: A trivial advisory query/match/no-match check (88 lines) carries two `Menu Handling Logic` blocks + two `EXECUTION RULES` blocks + a `CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` for what amounts to "if match, ask; otherwise proceed." The Menu Handling Logic for §4 ("After no-match or skip determination, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}") and its EXECUTION RULES are pure ceremony around a one-line auto-proceed.
26	  Fix: Collapse §3 and §4 into a single decision branch; drop the §4 Menu Handling Logic + EXECUTION RULES blocks; trim from 88 → ~50 lines. Resolves an "earn its keep" violation called out in the principles file ("multiple files that could be a single instruction" / "defensive padding").
27	
28	- **[A3] SKILL.md Result Contract schema table duplicates partial content already in shared/references/output-contract-schema.md** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:100-112
29	  Issue: The 13-line schema table is faithfully Quick-Skill-specific but a near-duplicate of what `src/shared/references/output-contract-schema.md` already documents. Since SKILL.md already runs 233 lines (within ceiling but at the top of the multi-branch range), one option is to point at the shared doc plus a Quick-Skill-specific delta paragraph for the `error.code` enum and `skill_package: null` semantics.
30	  Fix: Either keep as-is (load-bearing for a HARD-HALT-heavy workflow) or extract `error.*` field rows to a `references/result-contract.md` carved-out. Recommend keep-as-is unless SKILL.md line-budget pressure increases — the schema is referenced from 7 stage HARD HALT branches and survives compaction better when inline. Mark as a deferred decision rather than a fix.
31	
32	- **[A4] Batch Mode lives at SKILL.md scope but is a discrete sub-protocol** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:137-232
33	  Issue: ~95 lines of Batch Mode + its boundary-event JSON sub-schema sit at SKILL.md scope. Single-target users (the common case) pay weight for this on every activation. Carving Batch Mode to `references/batch-mode.md` and replacing the SKILL.md section with a 3-4-line summary + path pointer would cut SKILL.md from 233 → ~140 lines.
34	  Fix: Carve `references/batch-mode.md` with input format, execution loop, summary contract, headless events, and exit code; leave a short pointer block in SKILL.md. Survives compaction because On Activation step 4 explicitly loads the file when `--batch` is set.
35	
36	- **[A5] Workflow Rules over-rules — duplication with implicit conventions** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:24-39
37	  Issue: Lines 28-29 ("Read each step file completely before taking any action" / "Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly — do not skip, reorder, or optimize" / "Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps") are LLM-native defaults already covered by the chain-via-`nextStepFile` mechanism; they don't prevent a failure mode the model would otherwise hit. The principles file calls these out as "Numbered procedural steps for things the LLM does naturally."
38	  Fix: Drop the three load/order rules; keep only "Never fabricate content — all data must come from source extraction or user input" (load-bearing for hallucination prevention), `{communication_language}`, and the two headless-mode lines (load-bearing for headless contract).
39	
40	### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)
41	
42	- **[D1] Customize-resolver activation step is missing** | severity: medium | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116-135 (On Activation)
43	  Issue: The Excellent peer (`skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:46-67`) runs `python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py --skill {skill-root} --key workflow` on activation and applies a path-scalar fallback so stage files reference `{<purpose>_path}` directly. Quick-skill skips this — there is no `customize.toml` and no resolver call. Since C1 (below) proposes lifting the skill-template asset, registries data, and batch output to scalars, the resolver step needs to land alongside.
44	  Fix: Add a customization resolver step (mirror `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:46-67`) once a `customize.toml` is introduced — this is gated on C1 below. Token cost: zero (one batched activation script call replaces in-prompt path defaulting).
45	
46	- **[D2] Prepass flags 3 sequential tool-call steps in SKILL.md — all are genuine data dependencies** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116, 133, 135
47	  Issue: `execution-deps-prepass.json` flags 3 sequential tool-call steps. Inspection: step 1 already correctly batches config + preferences ("one batched tool-call message — they are independent files"), but step 4 (`--batch` → read batch file) and step 5 (load `resolve-target.md`) are unavoidable serial dependencies on prior values. The prepass count is true but the steps are genuinely sequential — no parallelisation opportunity. The meaningful win is the parallel-fetch directive in `quick-extract.md:26` (README + manifest + entry-point batched as one message).
48	  Fix: Leave activation as-is. Tracked here so future reviewers don't re-raise the prepass false positive.
49	
50	- **[D3] §1 README read + §2 source-files fetch in quick-extract aren't quite as parallel as the directive claims** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/references/quick-extract.md:26, 28-29, 64-79
51	  Issue: The parallel-fetch directive at line 26 instructs README + manifest + entry-point as one batched message, but §1 also covers extraction from README content (description, features, usage, installation) — meaning the LLM reads §1 content *after* the parallel fetch returns. That ordering is fine for execution but the directive's claim "one batched tool-call message rather than three sequential round trips" needs to be matched by a sub-step that explicitly states "fetch in one message, then parse the responses." Today's prose reads as though parsing-extraction happens inside the fetch step.
52	  Fix: Rewrite §1 + §2 into a single "Fetch in parallel, then parse" subsection that lists the URLs to fetch in one message and then walks the extraction in §1.x / §2.x.
53	
54	- **[D4] Multi-module Maven / Gradle loop is serial in prose despite a parallel sub-module fetch directive** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/references/quick-extract.md:79, 107
55	  Issue: Line 79 says "loop §2+§3 per module. Sub-module fetches are safe to issue as one batched tool-call message — N module fetches collapse to O(1) wall-clock time." Line 107 then says "fetch each sub-module's manifest + entry-point files (§2) and re-invoke the helper per module (§3), aggregating exports[] across all module envelopes." The "re-invoke the helper per module" is serial — each module's helper invocation needs its own subprocess. If `skf-extract-public-api.py` accepts arbitrarily many `entries` (line 94), the per-module loop could collapse to one helper call with all sub-module entries (loses the per-module package_name, gains throughput).
56	  Fix: Verify whether `skf-extract-public-api.py` supports per-module aggregation in a single call; if yes, document the collapse. If no, keep the per-module helper loop but call out that the wall-clock saving is fetch-side, not helper-invocation-side.
57	
58	### Customization Surface (customize.toml)
59	
60	- **[C1] No customize.toml at all — at least three lift-worthy paths** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/ (missing customize.toml)
61	  Issue: Quick-skill ships no `customize.toml`. Three immediate lift candidates exist:
62	  - `assets/skill-template.md` → `skill_template_path` (referenced from compile.md:3, 27)
63	  - `references/registry-resolution.md` → `registry_resolution_path` (referenced from resolve-target.md:3, 84 — fallback when shared resolver script is absent)
64	  - `{skills_output_folder}/_batch/` → `batch_output_path` (orgs may want batch summaries to land in a different folder, e.g., `~/skf-batch/`)
65	  Plus the BMad-default `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]` for orgs that want house-style enforcement. The principles file calls this out: workflow has 2+ hardcoded asset paths and no `customize.toml` at all → high-opportunity to opt in.
66	  Fix: Create `customize.toml` mirroring `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` shape (header, `[workflow]` block, BMad-default `persistent_facts`, three optional path scalars). Add the resolver step to On Activation per D1. Update compile.md / resolve-target.md / batch-summary write logic to reference `{skillTemplatePath}` / `{registryResolutionPath}` / `{batchOutputPath}`. Add the activation-time path-scalar fallback (empty-string override falls through to bundled default).
67	
68	- **[C2] Six CLI override flags would benefit from documentation persistence** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:124-131
69	  Issue: The override flags (`--description`, `--exports`, `--skip-snippet`, `--no-active-pointer`, `--batch`, `--fail-fast`) are CLI-only — orgs can't pre-set defaults across all runs. A team that always wants `--no-active-pointer` (because they orchestrate pointer flips downstream) must remember to pass the flag every time.
70	  Fix: Optional — `[workflow.defaults]` block in customize.toml carrying the default flag values, merged into `{overrides}` at activation. Defer; the override surface is reasonable as-is and the flags are op-specific.
71	
72	### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)
73	
74	- **[E1] No cancel-line affordance on interactive gates** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/references/resolve-target.md:33-40, ecosystem-check.md:50-55, compile.md:148-150
75	  Issue: Brief-skill's gather-intent.md (line 96, 102) tells users they can type `cancel` / `exit` / `[X]` / `q` / `:q` at any prompt to exit cleanly with a `user-cancelled` exit code. Quick-skill's prompts have menu choices ([P]/[I]/[A], [C]/[E]/[S]/[Q]) but no universal cancel — a user who wanted to abort during the §1 Resolve Target prompt has no documented escape; the prompt waits indefinitely. The closest is [Q] at the compile step (exit code 6).
76	  Fix: Document a `cancel` / `exit` / `:q` universal cancel-line affordance in SKILL.md `Workflow Rules` and the §1 Resolve Target prompt at minimum. Wire to exit code 6 (compile-cancelled) or add a new exit code 2 (user-cancelled, matching brief-skill's exit code 6 mapping). Resolves the brief-skill peer-feature parity gap.
77	
78	- **[E2] Activation lacks a forge_data_folder / skills_output_folder write probe** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116-135
79	  Issue: Brief-skill `gather-intent.md:34-40` runs a single-byte write-and-remove probe to `{forge_data_folder}` at the top of step 1 — catches read-only-mount / disk-full / permission-denied paths before the user invests 5–15 minutes in interactive scoping. Quick-skill skips this; on a quick interactive run the user invests 1-2 minutes on resolve+ecosystem before write-failure in step 5. A 5-second pre-flight write probe would shift the failure earlier with no UX cost.
80	  Fix: Add a pre-flight write probe to `{skills_output_folder}` (since that's where step 5 §1 writes) inside On Activation, mirror the brief-skill pattern. HALT with exit code 4 (write-failure) and the standard envelope if it fails.
81	
82	- **[E3] Open-floor opening hint missing — straight to "Provide a target"** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/references/resolve-target.md:30-40
83	  Issue: The principles file's "Open-floor opening" pattern is reserved for conversational skills with scoping decisions to make. Quick-skill is intentionally non-conversational ("speed is the priority"), so a strict open-floor would dilute its identity. But a minimal hint — "If you have a language hint or scope hint up front, include them now to skip the multi-language gate later" — would help expert users who know their target multi-language repo will trip the §4 gate.
84	  Fix: Defer or add a one-line hint to the §1 Resolve Target prompt. Low priority — Quick-Skill's value prop is "no brief needed," not "guided discovery."
85	
86	- **[E4] Progress event slug list duplicates the Stages table** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:34-39
87	  Issue: The bullet list of progress-event slugs (`resolve-target`, `ecosystem-check`, ...) at line 39 duplicates the Stages table at line 43-51. If the table is the source of truth, the slug list ages.
88	  Fix: Replace line 39 with "see the Stages table — `<slug>` is the kebab portion of the filename" (one-line, single source of truth).
89	
90	- **[E5] No mention of recovery suggestion in HARD HALT envelopes** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:101-112
91	  Issue: The completion summary in finalize.md:67-73 suggests `test-skill` and `export-skill` after success, but the HARD HALT envelopes carry no analogue — automators reading `quick-skill-result-latest.json` see `{status: "error", ...}` but no suggested-recovery field. Brief-skill emits an `error_code` enum that downstream tools can branch on; quick-skill emits `error.code` too, but no suggestion ladder ("re-run with --language-hint", "try /skf-create-skill from a brief").
92	  Fix: Optional. Add an `error.suggestion` field carrying a one-line recovery hint per error-code class. Low ROI but a delight-tier improvement for chained-pipeline UX.
93	
94	## Deterministic Summary
95	
96	- **path-standards:** 0 findings across 10 files (no double-prefix, no bare bmad, no absolute paths, no relative-prefix). Baseline-Excellent on path hygiene.
97	- **scripts:** 0 findings — no `scripts/` directory exists locally; all deterministic helpers live in `src/shared/scripts/` and are probe-order-resolved from stage frontmatter. Healthy — no per-skill duplication.
98	- **workflow-integrity:** 0 issues. 15 sections detected in SKILL.md; stage-summary shows zero stages parsed (the prepass parser doesn't recognize the `nextStepFile` frontmatter chain pattern as "stages" — prepass limitation, not a defect). Workflow type correctly classified as `simple-workflow`.
99	- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md at 233 lines / 3689 tokens, 6 tables, 7 fenced blocks, 15 sections — within "Multi-branch SKILL.md: up to ~250 lines" target. Two largest references: `compile.md` (172/2269) and `quick-extract.md` (180/2946). No waste patterns, no back-references. Pre-pass health: passes.
100	- **execution-deps:** 1 medium issue — "3 sequential tool-call steps." Inspection shows genuine data dependencies (config-read → batch-file-read → first-stage-load); not a parallelisation miss. See D2.
101	
102	## Strengths (preserve)
103	
104	- **Probe-order helper resolution** with installed-first / dev-fallback ordering — `packageResolverProbeOrder`, `publicApiExtractorProbeOrder`, `quickMetadataRendererProbeOrder`, `frontmatterValidatorProbeOrder`, `outputValidatorProbeOrder`, `atomicWriteProbeOrder` — every fragile-operation invocation is exact, with one right way (principles "When Procedure IS Value" section).
105	- **Ref-aware source reading** in quick-extract.md:24 — `?ref={source_ref}` propagates the user-specified `@version` through every fetch so the metadata claim matches the extracted source. Catches a quiet provenance bug that bare GitHub-content reads would silently hide.
106	- **Repo-Shape Sniff** in quick-extract.md:40-62 — graceful-degradation classifier for awesome-list / docs-site / examples-only repos, with a soft-warn gate and headless-mode auto-proceed that surfaces the signal in the result contract. Catches "wrap a library" misapplications before they produce a degenerate SKILL.md.
107	- **Paired success+error result contracts** — every HARD HALT path emits an `SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: {...}` stderr envelope with a stable exit code, and post-step-5 HALTs additionally write the `-latest.json` to disk. Headless automators never see a missing `-latest.json` after a failed run.
108	- **Batch Mode with per-target boundary events + batch summary contract** — the `{"batch":<n>,"target":...}` boundary events and the `partial`/`failed`/`success` resolution rule are well-designed for CI consumers and the future `skf-batch-skills` meta-workflow.
109	- **Headless coercion logic** — `--batch` implies `--headless` with explicit log entry "headless: coerced by --batch"; pre-empts the "headless flag silently overrode my intent" footgun.
110	- **Atomic active-pointer flip via shared helper** — explicit ban on `rm + ln -s` with two named failure modes, Windows junction fallback, locked via `flock` on `active.skf-lock`. Production-grade infrastructure for what looks like a "just symlink it" operation.
111	- **Zero-Exports Soft Gate (rescue mode)** at quick-extract.md:130-151 — catches the degenerate-SKILL.md case before write, offers retry-with-hints / proceed / abort, headless-mode auto-proceed surfaces the signal. Facilitative pattern the principles file calls "capture-don't-interrupt."
112	
113	## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)
114	
115	1. **Fix `{forger_root}` → `{sidecar_path}` and add ecosystem-check exit envelopes** — resolves 2 broken-section findings, effort: low. This is the only real runtime bug and the only contract gap; both fixes are tiny.
116	
117	2. **Introduce customize.toml with three path scalars + resolver activation step** — resolves [C1] + [D1], effort: low-medium. Mirror the `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` shape, lift `skillTemplatePath` / `registryResolutionPath` / `batchOutputPath`, ship BMad-default `persistent_facts`. Adds the customize-resolver step to On Activation. This is the "Foundation" half of the Foundation+Tightening pattern that earned Excellent in #306.
118	
119	3. **Tightening pass** — resolves [A1] (HTML-comment language headers), [A2] (collapse ecosystem-check ceremony), [A5] (drop redundant workflow rules), [E1] (cancel-line affordance), [E2] (write-probe), [E4] (slug list dedup), effort: medium. This is the "Tightening" half of the pattern from #323. Together with #1 and #2, brings quick-skill to par with the post-elevation Core skills.
120

# Quality Findings: skf-quick-skill

**Grade:** Good
**Narrative:** This skill is structurally well-architected and intelligence-placement-aware — sophisticated probe-order helper resolution, ref-aware source reading, explicit parallel-fetch directives, a stable Exit Codes map with paired HARD-HALT envelope contracts, a Batch Mode with per-target boundary events, and a Repo-Shape Sniff that gracefully degrades non-library targets. The primary gap to Excellent is one runtime path bug (`{forger_root}` does not resolve — `preferences.yaml` will never be read), a missing `customize.toml` despite at least three lift-worthy paths (template, registries data, batch output), and an exit-code/envelope hole on the ecosystem-check [I] Install / [A] Abort branches.

## Broken (critical/high)

- **{forger_root} variable does not resolve** | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116
  Issue: `{forger_root}` is not defined anywhere in the module (`module.yaml` declares `sidecar_path`, not `forger_root`; no other skill references `forger_root`). The line `Read \`{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml\` and \`{forger_root}/preferences.yaml\` in parallel` therefore reads a literal `{forger_root}/preferences.yaml` path that doesn't exist, so `headless_mode` resolution falls through to the default `false`. Headless invocations via `preferences.yaml` will silently fail; only `--headless`/`-H` arg-based activation works.
  Fix: Replace `{forger_root}` with `{sidecar_path}` (matches `skf-create-skill/references/load-brief.md:4` and `skf-forger/SKILL.md:54` pattern). Also extend On-Activation §1 config read to include `sidecar_path` in the resolved fields list (currently absent).

- **Ecosystem-check [I] Install and [A] Abort exit silently — no exit code, no result envelope** | src/skf-quick-skill/references/ecosystem-check.md:62-63
  Issue: Both terminal branches "Display install instructions, then end workflow" and "Display 'Compilation cancelled.', then end workflow" omit any exit code or HARD HALT envelope. Headless automators that hit an ecosystem match and the gate auto-resolves to `[P]` proceed are fine, but human-driven [I]/[A] paths return with no envelope on stderr, no `quick-skill-result-latest.json` on disk (since `{skill_package}` is unknown at this phase), and exit code 0 implying success — contradicting the SKILL.md "Exit Codes" promise that "Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable, documented code."
  Fix: Add an exit code for `ecosystem-cancelled` (or fold into `user-cancelled` at a new code 2 / 6 — pick one), and emit the HARD HALT stderr envelope per SKILL.md "Result Contract on HARD HALT" with `phase: "ecosystem-check"`, `skill_package: null`, before the workflow ends. Update the Exit Codes table accordingly.

## Findings by Dimension

### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)

- **[A1] Language-header style inconsistent with Excellent peer** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/references/*.md (top of each file)
  Issue: Every stage opens with a visible body line `Communicate with the user in \`{communication_language}\`.` while the Excellent peer (`skf-brief-skill/references/analyze-target.md:9`) uses an invisible `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->` HTML comment that doesn't render to the user but still seeds the LLM. Stages that produce written artifacts also need `{document_output_language}` (compile, write-and-validate, finalize handle this; others should too where appropriate).
  Fix: Replace the body-line directives with HTML-comment headers; add `{document_output_language}` mention to the comment in stages that emit user-facing prose.

- **[A2] Ecosystem-check is over-ceremonied for the work it does** | severity: medium | src/skf-quick-skill/references/ecosystem-check.md
  Issue: A trivial advisory query/match/no-match check (88 lines) carries two `Menu Handling Logic` blocks + two `EXECUTION RULES` blocks + a `CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` for what amounts to "if match, ask; otherwise proceed." The Menu Handling Logic for §4 ("After no-match or skip determination, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}") and its EXECUTION RULES are pure ceremony around a one-line auto-proceed.
  Fix: Collapse §3 and §4 into a single decision branch; drop the §4 Menu Handling Logic + EXECUTION RULES blocks; trim from 88 → ~50 lines. Resolves an "earn its keep" violation called out in the principles file ("multiple files that could be a single instruction" / "defensive padding").

- **[A3] SKILL.md Result Contract schema table duplicates partial content already in shared/references/output-contract-schema.md** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:100-112
  Issue: The 13-line schema table is faithfully Quick-Skill-specific but a near-duplicate of what `src/shared/references/output-contract-schema.md` already documents. Since SKILL.md already runs 233 lines (within ceiling but at the top of the multi-branch range), one option is to point at the shared doc plus a Quick-Skill-specific delta paragraph for the `error.code` enum and `skill_package: null` semantics.
  Fix: Either keep as-is (load-bearing for a HARD-HALT-heavy workflow) or extract `error.*` field rows to a `references/result-contract.md` carved-out. Recommend keep-as-is unless SKILL.md line-budget pressure increases — the schema is referenced from 7 stage HARD HALT branches and survives compaction better when inline. Mark as a deferred decision rather than a fix.

- **[A4] Batch Mode lives at SKILL.md scope but is a discrete sub-protocol** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:137-232
  Issue: ~95 lines of Batch Mode + its boundary-event JSON sub-schema sit at SKILL.md scope. Single-target users (the common case) pay weight for this on every activation. Carving Batch Mode to `references/batch-mode.md` and replacing the SKILL.md section with a 3-4-line summary + path pointer would cut SKILL.md from 233 → ~140 lines.

- **[A5] Workflow Rules over-rules — duplication with implicit conventions** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:24-39

The principles file calls these out as "Numbered procedural steps for things the LLM does naturally."
  Fix: Drop the three load/order rules; keep only "Never fabricate content — all data must come from source extraction or user input" (load-bearing for hallucination prevention), `{communication_language}`, and the two headless-mode lines (load-bearing for headless contract).

Quick-skill skips this — there is no `customize.toml` and no resolver call. Since C1 (below) proposes lifting the skill-template asset, registries data, and batch output to scalars, the resolver step needs to land alongside.
  Fix: Add a customization resolver step (mirror `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:46-67`) once a `customize.toml` is introduced — this is gated on C1 below. Token cost: zero (one batched activation script call replaces in-prompt path defaulting).

  Issue: `execution-deps-prepass.json` flags 3 sequential tool-call steps. Inspection: step 1 already correctly batches config + preferences ("one batched tool-call message — they are independent files"), but step 4 (`--batch` → read batch file) and step 5 (load `resolve-target.md`) are unavoidable serial dependencies on prior values. The prepass count is true but the steps are genuinely sequential — no parallelisation opportunity. The meaningful win is the parallel-fetch directive in `quick-extract.md:26` (README + manifest + entry-point batched as one message).
  Fix: Leave activation as-is. Tracked here so future reviewers don't re-raise the prepass false positive.

  Fix: Rewrite §1 + §2 into a single "Fetch in parallel, then parse" subsection that lists the URLs to fetch in one message and then walks the extraction in §1.x / §2.x.

- **[D4] Multi-module Maven / Gradle loop is serial in prose despite a parallel sub-module fetch directive** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/references/quick-extract.md:79, 107

  Fix: Verify whether `skf-extract-public-api.py` supports per-module aggregation in a single call; if yes, document the collapse. If no, keep the per-module helper loop but call out that the wall-clock saving is fetch-side, not helper-invocation-side.

### Customization Surface (customize.toml)

- **[C1] No customize.toml at all — at least three lift-worthy paths** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/ (missing customize.toml)
  Issue: Quick-skill ships no `customize.toml`. Three immediate lift candidates exist:

  Plus the BMad-default `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]` for orgs that want house-style enforcement. The principles file calls this out: workflow has 2+ hardcoded asset paths and no `customize.toml` at all → high-opportunity to opt in.
  Fix: Create `customize.toml` mirroring `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` shape (header, `[workflow]` block, BMad-default `persistent_facts`, three optional path scalars). Add the resolver step to On Activation per D1. Update compile.md / resolve-target.md / batch-summary write logic to reference `{skillTemplatePath}` / `{registryResolutionPath}` / `{batchOutputPath}`. Add the activation-time path-scalar fallback (empty-string override falls through to bundled default).

- **[C2] Six CLI override flags would benefit from documentation persistence** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:124-131
  Issue: The override flags (`--description`, `--exports`, `--skip-snippet`, `--no-active-pointer`, `--batch`, `--fail-fast`) are CLI-only — orgs can't pre-set defaults across all runs. A team that always wants `--no-active-pointer` (because they orchestrate pointer flips downstream) must remember to pass the flag every time.
  Fix: Optional — `[workflow.defaults]` block in customize.toml carrying the default flag values, merged into `{overrides}` at activation. Defer; the override surface is reasonable as-is and the flags are op-specific.

### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)

- **[E1] No cancel-line affordance on interactive gates** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/references/resolve-target.md:33-40, ecosystem-check.md:50-55, compile.md:148-150
  Issue: Brief-skill's gather-intent.md (line 96, 102) tells users they can type `cancel` / `exit` / `[X]` / `q` / `:q` at any prompt to exit cleanly with a `user-cancelled` exit code. Quick-skill's prompts have menu choices ([P]/[I]/[A], [C]/[E]/[S]/[Q]) but no universal cancel — a user who wanted to abort during the §1 Resolve Target prompt has no documented escape; the prompt waits indefinitely. The closest is [Q] at the compile step (exit code 6).
  Fix: Document a `cancel` / `exit` / `:q` universal cancel-line affordance in SKILL.md `Workflow Rules` and the §1 Resolve Target prompt at minimum. Wire to exit code 6 (compile-cancelled) or add a new exit code 2 (user-cancelled, matching brief-skill's exit code 6 mapping). Resolves the brief-skill peer-feature parity gap.

- **[E2] Activation lacks a forge_data_folder / skills_output_folder write probe** | severity: medium-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:116-135
  Issue: Brief-skill `gather-intent.md:34-40` runs a single-byte write-and-remove probe to `{forge_data_folder}` at the top of step 1 — catches read-only-mount / disk-full / permission-denied paths before the user invests 5–15 minutes in interactive scoping. Quick-skill skips this; on a quick interactive run the user invests 1-2 minutes on resolve+ecosystem before write-failure in step 5. A 5-second pre-flight write probe would shift the failure earlier with no UX cost.
  Fix: Add a pre-flight write probe to `{skills_output_folder}` (since that's where step 5 §1 writes) inside On Activation, mirror the brief-skill pattern. HALT with exit code 4 (write-failure) and the standard envelope if it fails.

- **[E3] Open-floor opening hint missing — straight to "Provide a target"** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/references/resolve-target.md:30-40
  Issue: The principles file's "Open-floor opening" pattern is reserved for conversational skills with scoping decisions to make. Quick-skill is intentionally non-conversational ("speed is the priority"), so a strict open-floor would dilute its identity. But a minimal hint — "If you have a language hint or scope hint up front, include them now to skip the multi-language gate later" — would help expert users who know their target multi-language repo will trip the §4 gate.
  Fix: Defer or add a one-line hint to the §1 Resolve Target prompt. Low priority — Quick-Skill's value prop is "no brief needed," not "guided discovery."

- **[E4] Progress event slug list duplicates the Stages table** | severity: low | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:34-39
  Issue: The bullet list of progress-event slugs (`resolve-target`, `ecosystem-check`, ...) at line 39 duplicates the Stages table at line 43-51. If the table is the source of truth, the slug list ages.
  Fix: Replace line 39 with "see the Stages table — `<slug>` is the kebab portion of the filename" (one-line, single source of truth).

- **[E5] No mention of recovery suggestion in HARD HALT envelopes** | severity: low-opportunity | src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md:101-112
  Issue: The completion summary in finalize.md:67-73 suggests `test-skill` and `export-skill` after success, but the HARD HALT envelopes carry no analogue — automators reading `quick-skill-result-latest.json` see `{status: "error", ...}` but no suggested-recovery field. Brief-skill emits an `error_code` enum that downstream tools can branch on; quick-skill emits `error.code` too, but no suggestion ladder ("re-run with --language-hint", "try /skf-create-skill from a brief").
  Fix: Optional. Add an `error.suggestion` field carrying a one-line recovery hint per error-code class. Low ROI but a delight-tier improvement for chained-pipeline UX.

## Deterministic Summary

- **path-standards:** 0 findings across 10 files (no double-prefix, no bare bmad, no absolute paths, no relative-prefix). Baseline-Excellent on path hygiene.
- **scripts:** 0 findings — no `scripts/` directory exists locally; all deterministic helpers live in `src/shared/scripts/` and are probe-order-resolved from stage frontmatter. Healthy — no per-skill duplication.
- **workflow-integrity:** 0 issues. 15 sections detected in SKILL.md; stage-summary shows zero stages parsed (the prepass parser doesn't recognize the `nextStepFile` frontmatter chain pattern as "stages" — prepass limitation, not a defect). Workflow type correctly classified as `simple-workflow`.
- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md at 233 lines / 3689 tokens, 6 tables, 7 fenced blocks, 15 sections — within "Multi-branch SKILL.md: up to ~250 lines" target. Two largest references: `compile.md` (172/2269) and `quick-extract.md` (180/2946). No waste patterns, no back-references. Pre-pass health: passes.
- **execution-deps:** 1 medium issue — "3 sequential tool-call steps." Inspection shows genuine data dependencies (config-read → batch-file-read → first-stage-load); not a parallelisation miss. See D2.

## Strengths (preserve)

- **Probe-order helper resolution** with installed-first / dev-fallback ordering — `packageResolverProbeOrder`, `publicApiExtractorProbeOrder`, `quickMetadataRendererProbeOrder`, `frontmatterValidatorProbeOrder`, `outputValidatorProbeOrder`, `atomicWriteProbeOrder` — every fragile-operation invocation is exact, with one right way (principles "When Procedure IS Value" section).
- **Ref-aware source reading** in quick-extract.md:24 — `?ref={source_ref}` propagates the user-specified `@version` through every fetch so the metadata claim matches the extracted source. Catches a quiet provenance bug that bare GitHub-content reads would silently hide.
- **Repo-Shape Sniff** in quick-extract.md:40-62 — graceful-degradation classifier for awesome-list / docs-site / examples-only repos, with a soft-warn gate and headless-mode auto-proceed that surfaces the signal in the result contract. Catches "wrap a library" misapplications before they produce a degenerate SKILL.md.
- **Paired success+error result contracts** — every HARD HALT path emits an `SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: {...}` stderr envelope with a stable exit code, and post-step-5 HALTs additionally write the `-latest.json` to disk. Headless automators never see a missing `-latest.json` after a failed run.
- **Batch Mode with per-target boundary events + batch summary contract** — the `{"batch":<n>,"target":...}` boundary events and the `partial`/`failed`/`success` resolution rule are well-designed for CI consumers and the future `skf-batch-skills` meta-workflow.
- **Headless coercion logic** — `--batch` implies `--headless` with explicit log entry "headless: coerced by --batch"; pre-empts the "headless flag silently overrode my intent" footgun.
- **Atomic active-pointer flip via shared helper** — explicit ban on `rm + ln -s` with two named failure modes, Windows junction fallback, locked via `flock` on `active.skf-lock`. Production-grade infrastructure for what looks like a "just symlink it" operation.
- **Zero-Exports Soft Gate (rescue mode)** at quick-extract.md:130-151 — catches the degenerate-SKILL.md case before write, offers retry-with-hints / proceed / abort, headless-mode auto-proceed surfaces the signal. Facilitative pattern the principles file calls "capture-don't-interrupt."

## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)

1. **Fix `{forger_root}` → `{sidecar_path}` and add ecosystem-check exit envelopes** — resolves 2 broken-section findings, effort: low. This is the only real runtime bug and the only contract gap; both fixes are tiny.

2. **Introduce customize.toml with three path scalars + resolver activation step** — resolves [C1] + [D1], effort: low-medium. Mirror the `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` shape, lift `skillTemplatePath` / `registryResolutionPath` / `batchOutputPath`, ship BMad-default `persistent_facts`. Adds the customize-resolver step to On Activation. This is the "Foundation" half of the Foundation+Tightening pattern that earned Excellent in #306.

3. **Tightening pass** — resolves [A1] (HTML-comment language headers), [A2] (collapse ecosystem-check ceremony), [A5] (drop redundant workflow rules), [E1] (cancel-line affordance), [E2] (write-probe), [E4] (slug list dedup), effort: medium. This is the "Tightening" half of the pattern from #323. Together with #1 and #2, brings quick-skill to par with the post-elevation Core skills.

1	# Quality Findings: skf-create-stack-skill
2	
3	**Grade:** Good
4	**Narrative:** The skill has unusually deep institutional substance — atomic-write rollback contract, file-list-intersection prune, exports resolution order, compose-mode co-mention precision guards, confidence-tier inheritance matrix — and a clean SKILL.md shape that mirrors the Excellent peers. The gap to Excellent is concentrated in two areas: zero customization surface (no `customize.toml`, no `{document_output_language}` plumbing for the artifacts that get persisted, no asset path lift candidates exposed despite 3+ asset-shaped paths) and a lifecycle/headless contract that is half-built (per-step error envelopes are well-specified but no Exit Codes table, no top-line `SKF_*_RESULT_JSON` envelope shape, and no `[X]` cancel option on the two interactive gates that the B6 cancel-lines pattern from the elevated peers requires).
5

7	
8	- **Cross-directory `./` reference in compile-stack.md** | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/compile-stack.md:77`
9	  Issue: `Link to reference file: \`./references/{library}.md\`` — `./` resolves to the *same folder* (i.e. `references/`), which would point at `references/references/{library}.md`. In the generated stack-skill artifact the per-library reference lives at `references/{library}.md` from the skill root. The template should emit a bare/anchored path, not `./references/`.
10	  Fix: change the emitted link template to `references/{library}.md` (bare from generated skill root). Also fix the matching pattern in `assets/stack-skill-template.md:35` which shows `[ref](./references/{name}.md)` — same bug propagating into every generated stack.

13	  Issue: `# (\`_bmad/skf/\` when installed, \`src/\` during development), NOT relative` — bare `_bmad` without `{project-root}` prefix per the path-standards scanner.
14	  Fix: write as `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` so the comment matches the project-wide path convention (the actual `nextStepFile: 'shared/health-check.md'` resolver lives in module-loader logic; the comment is the only user-facing artifact and should use the canonical prefix).

16	## Findings by Dimension
17	
18	### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)
19	
20	- **[A1] No customize.toml present** | severity: high | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/` (missing file)

22	  Fix: ship `customize.toml` with the standard `[workflow]` block + `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]` + scalar lifts for the 3 asset paths (see C1). Wire SKILL.md `## On Activation` to call the bmad-customize resolver script (peer pattern).
23	
24	- **[A2] Description trigger phrase incomplete** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:3`
25	  Issue: principles say "Use when user says 'specific phrase' or 'specific phrase'" — current text quotes only one phrase (`"create a stack skill."`). Real users say multiple things (stack the project, forge a stack, build a stack skill).
26	  Fix: extend to 2-3 quoted phrases per peer pattern (e.g., `Use when the user requests to "create a stack skill", "forge a stack", or "stack this project."`).
27	
28	- **[A3] `[workflow]` identity prose in `## Role`** | severity: low | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:22`

The "dependency analyst and integration architect" role is load-bearing; the Ferris-mode tag is noise.

31	
32	- **[A4] Missing Exit Codes table** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:52-59` (Invocation Contract)

This skill has multiple HALTs (init missing config, init missing forge-tier.yaml, step 2 no-manifests-headless, step 4 all-extractions-fail, step 7 group-dir collision, step 5 schemaVersion mismatch) with no stable exit-code mapping.

The "Exports resolution order (H1)" cascade also reads `metadata.json` and falls back to `references/` and `SKILL.md` prose parsing, all in parent. Parent never offloads to subagents.

Same for the metadata.json template (lines 142-179) which is already partially in `assets/stack-skill-template.md`.

90

A user who realizes mid-flow that they picked the wrong project or want to abort has no clean exit; they have to Ctrl-C and leave staging artifacts. The current init shows a calculated summary ("Stack Skill Forge initialized... Forge Tier: X") then auto-proceeds. A user with a non-standard project (monorepo, mixed languages, pre-existing skills) gets no chance to volunteer context before manifests are scanned blindly.
105	  Fix: in `init.md` §4, after the summary, add a "Anything else about this project I should know before I start scanning? (architecture quirks, pre-existing skills, custom layout) — type details or `C` to continue" prompt. Headless skips it.

108	  Issue: "If zero skills remain after filtering: HALT" — but the user invoked this because they wanted a stack. The fix message points at `[CS] Create Skill` and `[QS] Quick Skill` but doesn't offer to chain to those workflows.
109	  Fix: in the HALT message, surface a "Would you like me to invoke [CS] Create Skill now for a constituent library? (y/n)" path. Headless still HALTs with exit code; interactive offers the bridge.
110	
111	- **[E6] Headless decision log absent for compose-mode automated choices** | severity: medium-opportunity | `references/detect-manifests.md`, `references/rank-and-confirm.md`
112	  Issue: principles file says "When a skill supports headless invocation, the decision log absorbs every assumption made without the user — intent inference, proposed names, customization defaults, conflict resolutions, lint-fix calls". This skill auto-confirms 2 interactive gates in headless mode (rank-and-confirm + compile-stack), auto-detects compose mode, auto-resolves tier overrides, auto-handles stale manifest entries, auto-skips CCC failures — none of these decisions land in a structured `.decision-log.md`. The `workflow_warnings[]` accumulator catches a subset, but it's a warning channel, not a decision audit trail.
113	  Fix: add a `decisions[]` accumulator alongside `workflow_warnings[]` (or extend the latter with a `severity: "decision"` level), surfaced in the evidence-report.md and the final `-latest.json` envelope.
114	
115	- **[E7] `gotchas` line in context-snippet has no upstream source** | severity: low-opportunity | `references/generate-output.md:118`, `assets/stack-skill-template.md:61`
116	  Issue: the snippet template asks for "1-2 most critical integration pitfalls" but no upstream step extracts gotchas. The LLM has to invent them at write-time — exactly the kind of judgment-without-grounding the zero-hallucination workflow rule forbids elsewhere.
117	  Fix: either (a) drop the gotchas line from the template (the overflow strategy already drops it first; making it optional is honest), or (b) add a "gotchas extraction" output to `parallel-extract.md` that surfaces per-library T1-low warnings, and let the snippet roll those up.
118	
119	## Deterministic Summary
120	- **path-standards:** 2 high findings — (1) `./references/{library}.md` cross-dir-dot-slash in compile-stack.md:77 (propagates into the generated artifact via assets template), (2) bare `_bmad/skf/` in health-check.md:3 comment.
121	- **scripts:** 0 findings (no scripts/ dir found — but D1/D2/D5 flag this as a high-value gap, not a clean pass).
122	- **workflow-integrity:** pass — 0 issues. Note: prepass reports `total_stages: 0` because the workflow uses frontmatter `nextStepFile` chaining rather than the named-stage convention the prepass detects. The Stages table in SKILL.md and Step-N labels in references map cleanly.
123	- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md 73 lines / 1225 tokens (well under the 130 ceiling); largest reference is generate-output.md at 329 lines / 3428 tokens (lift candidate — see D4). Two other heavy references: detect-integrations.md 165 lines / 3402 tokens, parallel-extract.md 178 lines / 2511 tokens — both lift-worthy if customization/scripts trim them further.
124	- **execution-deps:** pass — 0 issues. The dependency graph between steps is linear via `nextStepFile` chaining; no parallel-group claims and no over-constraint. The parallelization opportunity is intra-step (per-library extraction in step 4, per-pair grep in step 5).
125	
126	## Strengths (preserve)
127	- Atomic-write contract with stage-dir / commit-dir / flip-link / rollback (`generate-output.md` §1-9) — institutional rigor most skills lack.
128	- File-list intersection as MANDATORY first pass in pair detection (`detect-integrations.md` §1) — turns 210 prescribed greps into ~12 for typical N≈21 codebases.
129	- Exports resolution order (H1) in `parallel-extract.md` §0 — three explicit fallback layers with warning logging.
130	- Compose-mode co-mention precision guards (H2 — word-boundary + section filtering + 2-paragraph minimum) — exactly the kind of "make heuristic detection less wrong" rigor that earns its keep.
131	- Confidence Tier Inheritance matrix in `compose-mode-rules.md` — covers all 6 pair combinations with audit-friendly suffixes.
132	- `workflow_warnings[]` accumulator pattern (SKILL.md Workflow Rules) — single sink, structured shape, surfaced at step 9 §5.
133	- Schema-version guard on feasibility-report consumption (`detect-integrations.md` §2, `compose-mode-rules.md` Feasibility Report Integration) — refuses to interpret unknown versions.
134	- Headless precedence resolver (B2) with explicit-disable / explicit-enable / preferences / default ordering — peer-quality.
135	- Top-K cap at 20 with explicit warning + record in evidence report (S7) — graceful degradation done right.
136	
137	## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)
138	
139	1. **Ship `customize.toml` + thread `{document_output_language}` through artifact-producing steps** — resolves C1, C2, C3, and unblocks the broader "headless automator" story by making the surface inspectable. Effort: medium (one new file + ~6 prompt insertions). This is the single largest gap to Excellent.
140	
141	2. **Add Exit Codes table + top-level `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON:` stdout envelope + `[X] Cancel` option on both interactive gates** — resolves A4, E1, E2, and aligns the headless contract surface 1:1 with the elevated peer. Effort: medium. Without this, headless automators can't branch cleanly on failures and interactive users have no abort path.
142	
143	3. **Lift 2-3 deterministic operations to `scripts/`** — at minimum `scan-manifests.py` (D2) + `enumerate-skills.py` (compose-mode discovery with the existing export-manifest/symlink/cycle/hash logic) + `pair-intersect.py` (D5). Resolves D1, D2, D5; trims detect-manifests.md and detect-integrations.md by ~50% each; replaces N-skill SKILL.md reads with one script invocation. Effort: medium-high (3 PEP 723 scripts + JSON contracts + prompt rewrites). This is what makes the determinism story credible at the Excellent bar.
