# Quality Findings: skf-create-stack-skill

**Grade:** Good
**Narrative:** The skill has unusually deep institutional substance — atomic-write rollback contract, file-list-intersection prune, exports resolution order, compose-mode co-mention precision guards, confidence-tier inheritance matrix — and a clean SKILL.md shape that mirrors the Excellent peers. The gap to Excellent is concentrated in two areas: zero customization surface (no `customize.toml`, no `{document_output_language}` plumbing for the artifacts that get persisted, no asset path lift candidates exposed despite 3+ asset-shaped paths) and a lifecycle/headless contract that is half-built (per-step error envelopes are well-specified but no Exit Codes table, no top-line `SKF_*_RESULT_JSON` envelope shape, and no `[X]` cancel option on the two interactive gates that the B6 cancel-lines pattern from the elevated peers requires).

## Broken (critical/high)

- **Cross-directory `./` reference in compile-stack.md** | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/compile-stack.md:77`
  Issue: `Link to reference file: \`./references/{library}.md\`` — `./` resolves to the *same folder* (i.e. `references/`), which would point at `references/references/{library}.md`. In the generated stack-skill artifact the per-library reference lives at `references/{library}.md` from the skill root. The template should emit a bare/anchored path, not `./references/`.
  Fix: change the emitted link template to `references/{library}.md` (bare from generated skill root). Also fix the matching pattern in `assets/stack-skill-template.md:35` which shows `[ref](./references/{name}.md)` — same bug propagating into every generated stack.

- **Bare `_bmad/` reference in health-check.md frontmatter comment** | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/health-check.md:3`
  Issue: `# (\`_bmad/skf/\` when installed, \`src/\` during development), NOT relative` — bare `_bmad` without `{project-root}` prefix per the path-standards scanner.
  Fix: write as `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` so the comment matches the project-wide path convention (the actual `nextStepFile: 'shared/health-check.md'` resolver lives in module-loader logic; the comment is the only user-facing artifact and should use the canonical prefix).

## Findings by Dimension

### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)

- **[A1] No customize.toml present** | severity: high | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/` (missing file)
  Issue: principles file says workflow-shape conventions ship `customize.toml` with `activation_steps_prepend/append`, `persistent_facts`, and any workflow scalars; the Excellent peer `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` is the calibration shape. This skill has none — overrides require forking.
  Fix: ship `customize.toml` with the standard `[workflow]` block + `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]` + scalar lifts for the 3 asset paths (see C1). Wire SKILL.md `## On Activation` to call the bmad-customize resolver script (peer pattern).

- **[A2] Description trigger phrase incomplete** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:3`
  Issue: principles say "Use when user says 'specific phrase' or 'specific phrase'" — current text quotes only one phrase (`"create a stack skill."`). Real users say multiple things (stack the project, forge a stack, build a stack skill).
  Fix: extend to 2-3 quoted phrases per peer pattern (e.g., `Use when the user requests to "create a stack skill", "forge a stack", or "stack this project."`).

- **[A3] `[workflow]` identity prose in `## Role`** | severity: low | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:22`
  Issue: "operating in Ferris Architect mode" is an agent-persona phrasing that does not earn its keep (principles: "Identity / communication-style / principles in [workflow] → workflow wants to be an agent"). The "dependency analyst and integration architect" role is load-bearing; the Ferris-mode tag is noise.
  Fix: drop the "operating in Ferris Architect mode" clause. Keep the analyst+integration-architect framing.

- **[A4] Missing Exit Codes table** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:52-59` (Invocation Contract)
  Issue: peer `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-104` has a full Exit Codes table (`0 success`, `2 input-missing`, etc.) so headless automators can branch without grepping. This skill has multiple HALTs (init missing config, init missing forge-tier.yaml, step 2 no-manifests-headless, step 4 all-extractions-fail, step 7 group-dir collision, step 5 schemaVersion mismatch) with no stable exit-code mapping.
  Fix: add an Exit Codes section to SKILL.md mirroring the peer shape; classify each documented HALT (B7 contracts, S1/S2/S3, etc.).

- **[A5] `## Stages` step 10 (Workflow Health Check) is a 22-line shim** | severity: low | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:50` / `references/health-check.md`
  Issue: step 10 is a delegation to `shared/health-check.md` with no logic of its own; the Stages table lists it as a peer of the substantive 9 prior steps. step 9 `report.md` §7 already documents the chain to step 10 — the intermediate file adds a 22-line shim that could be removed.
  Fix: collapse step 10 — either inline the `shared/health-check.md` load into `report.md` §7 directly, or relabel the row as "Terminal handoff to shared health-check" so its shim role is explicit.

- **[A6] Workflow Rules item M4 is a 6-line essay in a bullet list** | severity: low | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md:35`
  Issue: the `workflow_warnings[]` accumulator spec is genuinely load-bearing institutional knowledge (shared accumulator vs per-step channel) but it consumes a 350-token bullet in a list whose siblings are all single-line. Visually it derails the section.
  Fix: lift the long accumulator definition to a one-line bullet (`workflow_warnings[] is the single warning accumulator; see <ref> for shape and surfacing`) and move the prose into a short subsection (e.g. `### Workflow state contract`) or a dedicated `references/workflow-state.md` referenced from there.

### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)

- **[D1] No `scripts/` directory despite multiple deterministic operations** | severity: high | scripts-temp.json reports "No scripts/ directory found"
  Issue: the LLM is asked to do several things that are deterministic and would benefit from a script: file-list-intersection prune (detect-integrations.md §1, computed against per-library file lists), manifest parsing across 8 ecosystems (detect-manifests.md §3), export-manifest JSON parsing with stale-entry fallback + cycle detection + filter (detect-manifests.md §0/§1), sha256 of metadata.json (S13), token estimation for context-snippet via `ceil(char_count / 4)` (generate-output.md §5), confidence_distribution dominant-tier computation (generate-output.md §6 + validate.md §5).
  Fix: ship `scripts/` with at minimum (a) `scan-manifests.py` emitting structured JSON per ecosystem, (b) `enumerate-skills.py` for compose-mode skill discovery with export-manifest + symlink fallback + cycle guard + metadata-hash, (c) `pair-intersect.py` for the file-list intersection prune. Each replaces a 50-150 line prompt block with deterministic JSON.

- **[D2] No pre-pass for manifest detection** | severity: high | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/detect-manifests.md:88-132`
  Issue: §2 (Scan for Manifest Files) and §3 (Parse Each Manifest) ask the LLM to scan, dedupe, normalize across ecosystems. With the existing pre-pass pattern (peer skills hand the LLM compact JSON), this could be one script invocation returning `{manifests: [{path, ecosystem, deps: [...]}], total_unique: N}`.
  Fix: add `scripts/scan-manifests.py` (PEP 723) → JSON; SKILL.md §2 reads the JSON; LLM applies judgment only for unusual structures (monorepo flag).

- **[D3] Implicit-read trap in compose-mode per-skill SKILL.md loads** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/parallel-extract.md:27-54`
  Issue: §0 says "For each confirmed skill, load SKILL.md from the version-aware path resolved in step 2" — that's a parent-side read multiplied by N skills, exactly the pattern the principles file flags. The "Exports resolution order (H1)" cascade also reads `metadata.json` and falls back to `references/` and `SKILL.md` prose parsing, all in parent. Parent never offloads to subagents.
  Fix: delegate per-skill extraction to subagents with an explicit "Return ONLY {exports, usage_patterns, exports_source, confidence}. No other output." constraint; parent never reads constituent SKILL.md files directly.

- **[D4] generate-output.md is 328 lines with inline JSON schema and bash blocks** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/generate-output.md`
  Issue: largest reference at 328 lines / 3428 tokens. The provenance-map.json schemas (code-mode and compose-mode) take ~80 lines of inline JSON template that could lift to `assets/provenance-map-schema.md` — the prompt would route to them. Same for the metadata.json template (lines 142-179) which is already partially in `assets/stack-skill-template.md`.
  Fix: lift schema bodies to assets; replace inline with `Use the schema from {provenanceMapSchema}` frontmatter routing.

- **[D5] Pair-grep scope work pushed onto the LLM without a script** | severity: medium | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/detect-integrations.md:24-31`
  Issue: file-list intersection is described as "MANDATORY first pass" but implemented in prose, asking the LLM to intersect sets and rank by size. A `scripts/pair-intersect.py` reading per-library file lists + outputting `{pairs: [{a, b, intersection: N, files: [...]}]}` collapses the prose to a script invocation. Then the prompt does the Top-K cap with judgment for ties.
  Fix: add `scripts/pair-intersect.py`; §1 calls the script and reads JSON.

### Customization Surface (customize.toml)

- **[C1] No customize.toml — opt-in opportunity is high** | severity: high-opportunity | (no file)
  Issue: this is the most impactful single gap to Excellent. The peer `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` ships the canonical shape (activation_steps prepend/append, persistent_facts with `file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md`, three path scalars). This skill has comparable scalar candidates and zero surface.
  Fix: ship `customize.toml` with:
    - `activation_steps_prepend = []`, `activation_steps_append = []`
    - `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`
    - Path scalars: `stack_skill_template_path = ""` (lifts `assets/stack-skill-template.md`), `integration_patterns_path = ""` (lifts `references/integration-patterns.md`), `manifest_patterns_path = ""` (lifts `references/manifest-patterns.md`), `compose_mode_rules_path = ""` (lifts `references/compose-mode-rules.md`).
    - Optional: `stack_output_path = ""` if orgs want to redirect from `{skills_output_folder}/{project_name}-stack/` to a custom destination (low-opportunity; flag-only).
  Wire `## On Activation` step 3 to run the bmad-customize resolver per peer.

- **[C2] No `{document_output_language}` in any artifact-producing step** | severity: high-opportunity | `references/generate-output.md`, `references/compile-stack.md`, `references/report.md`
  Issue: the workflow writes SKILL.md prose, references/{library}.md prose, evidence-report.md, context-snippet.md — all user-facing documents. The peer pattern (and principles file) requires carved files using `{document_output_language}` when they produce docs. Zero references in the whole skill (grep returned 0 matches across all step files).
  Fix: thread `{document_output_language}` into the prose-emitting sections of compile-stack.md, generate-output.md (§3/§4/§5/§7), report.md user-facing strings, and validate.md "Display Validation Results" — same way peer threads it through `description`, `notes`, and free-form fields. SKILL.md `## On Activation` already loads `document_output_language` from config (line 64) but no downstream step consumes it.

- **[C3] Frontmatter inline path scalars in stage files are not declared as overridable** | severity: medium-opportunity | `init.md:3`, `rank-and-confirm.md` etc.
  Issue: `manifestPatterns`, `integrationPatterns`, `composeModeRules`, `stackSkillTemplate` are inlined as stage-file frontmatter scalars that point at bundled assets — fine for now but they're the candidates for the customize.toml lifts in C1. The bigger story is the four references/*-rules.md / assets/*.md files that are *workflow-defining policy* and should be org-overridable.
  Fix: covered by C1; ensure stage-file frontmatter consumes the resolved scalars from `## On Activation`.

- **[C4] `forge_tier` override surface exists but is undocumented in customize.toml shape** | severity: low-opportunity | `references/init.md:53`
  Issue: "Read `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`. If `tier_override` is set..." — this is a user-overridable behavior, but it lives in the sidecar preferences file (correct) rather than customize.toml (which is the workflow-config surface). Documenting the tier_override key as a `persistent_facts`-style hint in customize.toml comments would help discoverability.
  Fix: optional — add a comment block to the new customize.toml pointing at the preferences.yaml `tier_override` key.

### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)

- **[E1] No `[X]` cancel option on interactive gates** | severity: high-opportunity | `references/rank-and-confirm.md:124-127`, `references/compile-stack.md:120-122`
  Issue: peer `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:103` exposes `6 user-cancelled` exit code — "any interactive menu in step 1/03/04 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)". This skill's two interactive gates show only `[C] Continue`. A user who realizes mid-flow that they picked the wrong project or want to abort has no clean exit; they have to Ctrl-C and leave staging artifacts. The atomic-write rollback contract exists but is only triggered on errors, not on cancel.
  Fix: add `[X] Cancel and exit` to both gates; wire to the existing rollback contract (purge staging dir, exit with stable code `6 user-cancelled`).

- **[E2] No top-level result envelope on stdout** | severity: high-opportunity | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/report.md:97-123`
  Issue: peer ships `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON: {...}` on stdout for headless automators to parse without scraping file paths. This skill writes per-run JSON to `{forge_version}/create-stack-skill-result-{ts}.json` + a stable `-latest.json` — both file artifacts. Automators must `cat` a file path they have to compute. The peer's one-line stdout envelope is cheaper for pipelines.
  Fix: add a `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON:` final line on stdout in step 9 (before chaining to step 10) carrying the same fields written to `-latest.json` — peer pattern exactly.

- **[E3] Resume protocol absent** | severity: high-opportunity | (no resume hook anywhere)
  Issue: this is a long workflow (10 steps, parallel extraction, atomic-write commit, symlink flip). If context compacts between step 4 and step 6 the user loses state. No `.decision-log.md` workspace exists (principles file: Decision-Log Workspace pattern is the default for multi-turn workflows producing substantive revisable artifacts). The forge workspace at `{forge_version}/` has provenance + evidence but no resume protocol — re-running the workflow rebuilds from scratch.
  Fix: at minimum add a "if a `{forge_version}/in-progress.json` exists, offer to resume from the last completed step" check at the top of `init.md`. Full DLW pattern would write a `.decision-log.md` to `{forge_version}/` as the load-bearing artifact (peer skills do this).

- **[E4] No open-floor opening on `init.md`** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/init.md:101-118`
  Issue: principles file calls this out — "open-floor opening: conversational skills start with an explicit invitation for the user to share everything they have". The current init shows a calculated summary ("Stack Skill Forge initialized... Forge Tier: X") then auto-proceeds. A user with a non-standard project (monorepo, mixed languages, pre-existing skills) gets no chance to volunteer context before manifests are scanned blindly.
  Fix: in `init.md` §4, after the summary, add a "Anything else about this project I should know before I start scanning? (architecture quirks, pre-existing skills, custom layout) — type details or `C` to continue" prompt. Headless skips it.

- **[E5] Compose-mode discovery surfaces 0 → HALT with no recovery path** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/detect-manifests.md:53`
  Issue: "If zero skills remain after filtering: HALT" — but the user invoked this because they wanted a stack. The fix message points at `[CS] Create Skill` and `[QS] Quick Skill` but doesn't offer to chain to those workflows.
  Fix: in the HALT message, surface a "Would you like me to invoke [CS] Create Skill now for a constituent library? (y/n)" path. Headless still HALTs with exit code; interactive offers the bridge.

- **[E6] Headless decision log absent for compose-mode automated choices** | severity: medium-opportunity | `references/detect-manifests.md`, `references/rank-and-confirm.md`
  Issue: principles file says "When a skill supports headless invocation, the decision log absorbs every assumption made without the user — intent inference, proposed names, customization defaults, conflict resolutions, lint-fix calls". This skill auto-confirms 2 interactive gates in headless mode (rank-and-confirm + compile-stack), auto-detects compose mode, auto-resolves tier overrides, auto-handles stale manifest entries, auto-skips CCC failures — none of these decisions land in a structured `.decision-log.md`. The `workflow_warnings[]` accumulator catches a subset, but it's a warning channel, not a decision audit trail.
  Fix: add a `decisions[]` accumulator alongside `workflow_warnings[]` (or extend the latter with a `severity: "decision"` level), surfaced in the evidence-report.md and the final `-latest.json` envelope.

- **[E7] `gotchas` line in context-snippet has no upstream source** | severity: low-opportunity | `references/generate-output.md:118`, `assets/stack-skill-template.md:61`
  Issue: the snippet template asks for "1-2 most critical integration pitfalls" but no upstream step extracts gotchas. The LLM has to invent them at write-time — exactly the kind of judgment-without-grounding the zero-hallucination workflow rule forbids elsewhere.
  Fix: either (a) drop the gotchas line from the template (the overflow strategy already drops it first; making it optional is honest), or (b) add a "gotchas extraction" output to `parallel-extract.md` that surfaces per-library T1-low warnings, and let the snippet roll those up.

## Deterministic Summary
- **path-standards:** 2 high findings — (1) `./references/{library}.md` cross-dir-dot-slash in compile-stack.md:77 (propagates into the generated artifact via assets template), (2) bare `_bmad/skf/` in health-check.md:3 comment.
- **scripts:** 0 findings (no scripts/ dir found — but D1/D2/D5 flag this as a high-value gap, not a clean pass).
- **workflow-integrity:** pass — 0 issues. Note: prepass reports `total_stages: 0` because the workflow uses frontmatter `nextStepFile` chaining rather than the named-stage convention the prepass detects. The Stages table in SKILL.md and Step-N labels in references map cleanly.
- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md 73 lines / 1225 tokens (well under the 130 ceiling); largest reference is generate-output.md at 329 lines / 3428 tokens (lift candidate — see D4). Two other heavy references: detect-integrations.md 165 lines / 3402 tokens, parallel-extract.md 178 lines / 2511 tokens — both lift-worthy if customization/scripts trim them further.
- **execution-deps:** pass — 0 issues. The dependency graph between steps is linear via `nextStepFile` chaining; no parallel-group claims and no over-constraint. The parallelization opportunity is intra-step (per-library extraction in step 4, per-pair grep in step 5).

## Strengths (preserve)
- Atomic-write contract with stage-dir / commit-dir / flip-link / rollback (`generate-output.md` §1-9) — institutional rigor most skills lack.
- File-list intersection as MANDATORY first pass in pair detection (`detect-integrations.md` §1) — turns 210 prescribed greps into ~12 for typical N≈21 codebases.
- Exports resolution order (H1) in `parallel-extract.md` §0 — three explicit fallback layers with warning logging.
- Compose-mode co-mention precision guards (H2 — word-boundary + section filtering + 2-paragraph minimum) — exactly the kind of "make heuristic detection less wrong" rigor that earns its keep.
- Confidence Tier Inheritance matrix in `compose-mode-rules.md` — covers all 6 pair combinations with audit-friendly suffixes.
- `workflow_warnings[]` accumulator pattern (SKILL.md Workflow Rules) — single sink, structured shape, surfaced at step 9 §5.
- Schema-version guard on feasibility-report consumption (`detect-integrations.md` §2, `compose-mode-rules.md` Feasibility Report Integration) — refuses to interpret unknown versions.
- Headless precedence resolver (B2) with explicit-disable / explicit-enable / preferences / default ordering — peer-quality.
- Top-K cap at 20 with explicit warning + record in evidence report (S7) — graceful degradation done right.

## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)

1. **Ship `customize.toml` + thread `{document_output_language}` through artifact-producing steps** — resolves C1, C2, C3, and unblocks the broader "headless automator" story by making the surface inspectable. Effort: medium (one new file + ~6 prompt insertions). This is the single largest gap to Excellent.

2. **Add Exit Codes table + top-level `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON:` stdout envelope + `[X] Cancel` option on both interactive gates** — resolves A4, E1, E2, and aligns the headless contract surface 1:1 with the elevated peer. Effort: medium. Without this, headless automators can't branch cleanly on failures and interactive users have no abort path.

3. **Lift 2-3 deterministic operations to `scripts/`** — at minimum `scan-manifests.py` (D2) + `enumerate-skills.py` (compose-mode discovery with the existing export-manifest/symlink/cycle/hash logic) + `pair-intersect.py` (D5). Resolves D1, D2, D5; trims detect-manifests.md and detect-integrations.md by ~50% each; replaces N-skill SKILL.md reads with one script invocation. Effort: medium-high (3 PEP 723 scripts + JSON contracts + prompt rewrites). This is what makes the determinism story credible at the Excellent bar.

content.content:
1	# Quality Findings: skf-analyze-source
2	
3	**Grade:** Good
4	**Narrative:** A well-structured, multi-stage discovery workflow with clean stage chaining, a thoughtful tier-aware analysis model, and a serviceable result contract. To reach Excellent it needs the same Foundation+Tightening treatment given to skf-brief-skill: a customize.toml surface, an explicit headless Result Contract / Exit Codes block in SKILL.md, B6-style cancel-lines on the three confirm gates, and `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` wiring inside every reference. There is also one path-standards violation and one orphaned forward-reference (subprocess Patterns) that the LLM cannot resolve at runtime.
5	
6	## Broken (critical/high)
7	
8	- **Path standards violation: bare `_bmad/skf/` reference without `{project-root}` prefix** | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/health-check.md:3`. The frontmatter comment uses `_bmad/skf/` as a bare prefix; the path-standards scanner correctly flags it. Fix: rewrite the comment to refer to `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` (or rephrase to avoid the path entirely — the comment is explanatory only).
9	- **Orphaned forward-reference: "subprocess Pattern 1" and "Pattern 2" cited but never defined anywhere in the skill or shared/** | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:17` and `references/map-and-detect.md:15`. The LLM has no patterns file to consult, so the instruction is a noop at best and a confused tangent at worst. Fix: either inline what the "pattern" means (a per-unit subagent fan-out vs. a single-process scan) in the rule itself, or drop the Pattern N labels.
10	
11	## Findings by Dimension
12	
13	### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)
14	
15	- **[A1] SKILL.md has no headless Result Contract / Exit Codes section; the contract lives buried at generate-briefs.md §9** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:49-58`
16	  Issue: The Invocation Contract table mentions outputs but never declares the result envelope shape, exit codes, or the latest.json pointer. Excellent peer (skf-brief-skill) stamps `## Exit Codes` and `## Result Contract (Headless)` directly in SKILL.md so automators can read the contract without traversing six stage files.
17	  Fix: Add `## Exit Codes` table and `## Result Contract (Headless)` section to SKILL.md modeled on `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-114`. Choose an envelope name (e.g. `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`) and document `status`, `report_path`, `brief_paths[]`, `unit_counts`, `exit_code`, `halt_reason`.
18	
19	- **[A2] Stage files use no language tokens — every user-facing prose block is implicitly English** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md`, `scan-project.md`, `identify-units.md`, `map-and-detect.md`, `recommend.md`, `generate-briefs.md` (every prose block)
20	  Issue: SKILL.md says "Always communicate in `{communication_language}`" but zero stage files reference `{communication_language}` or `{document_output_language}`. On compaction, the rule from SKILL.md disappears and the stage file becomes the only context — the LLM defaults to English regardless of config.
21	  Fix: Add a configuration header at the top of each stage prompt (after frontmatter, before `# Step N`) declaring `Communicate in {communication_language}` and, where the stage writes persisted artifact text (e.g. recommendation cards, brief description fields in generate-briefs), `Written artifact text is in {document_output_language}`.
22	
23	- **[A3] Confirm gates have no Cancel / exit option — users can only `[C] Continue`, leaving them stuck if they want to abort cleanly** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:139`, `identify-units.md:123`, `recommend.md:156`
24	  Issue: All three Confirm Gates display `[C] Continue` only. A user who realizes mid-flow that they pointed the workflow at the wrong project has no graceful exit; they can only hard-kill the session, which orphans the partial report. skf-brief-skill defines a `[X] Cancel and exit` cancel-line with exit code 6 (`user-cancelled`).
25	  Fix: Add `[X] Cancel and exit` to every menu in scan-project §7, identify-units §7, recommend §8 (and the generate-briefs Y/N/M prompt at §4 which currently overloads "N" with "abort"). Define the cancel behavior in SKILL.md's Exit Codes block.
26	
27	- **[A4] Workflow Rules omit the canonical language-token paragraph** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:24-34`
28	  Issue: The peer (`skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:36`) calls out both `{communication_language}` (user-facing prose) and `{document_output_language}` (persisted artifact text). The target skill only mentions `{communication_language}`.
29	  Fix: Update Workflow Rules to mirror peer phrasing: "Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the per-unit recommendation `description` and `scope.notes` persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`."
30	
31	- **[A5] generate-briefs.md is 189 lines / ~1881 tokens with field-mapping table duplicating schema content** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/generate-briefs.md:38-58, 114-126`
32	  Issue: The field-mapping table and next-workflow recommendation table together re-state content from the schema asset and step 5 recommendation cards. The LLM does not need the field-mapping spelled out as a table when the source-cell wording is "Confirmed name from step 05" — it already knows where the data is.
33	  Fix: Reduce the field mapping to a 2-3 line note ("All fields trace back to step 05 recommendation cards plus frontmatter; consult `{schemaFile}` for the field schema and constraints"). Compress the next-workflow recommendation table's right-hand explanations.
34	
35	- **[A6] Workflow Rule "If any instruction references a subprocess or tool you lack, achieve the outcome in your main context thread" is defensive padding** | severity: low | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:32`
36	  Issue: This is the kind of "remember to" the principles file calls out as not earning its keep. The LLM falls back to its own tools by default. The two stages that name subprocess already say "If subprocess unavailable: Perform … in main thread" inline.
37	  Fix: Remove the rule from SKILL.md Workflow Rules.
38	
39	- **[A7] Workflow Rule "Update `stepsCompleted` … before loading next step" duplicates per-stage Append to Report blocks** | severity: low | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:31`
40	  Issue: Every stage file already updates `stepsCompleted` in its own append step. The global rule adds nothing the stage file doesn't say.
41	  Fix: Drop the rule from SKILL.md.
42	
43	### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)
44	
45	- **[D1] Per-unit export mapping is sequential by default; the spec says "subprocess Pattern 2 when available" but never tells the LLM to fan out** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md:32-69`
46	  Issue: For projects with 10+ qualifying units, sequential per-unit scanning is the dominant cost. The phrase "Pattern 2" is meaningless without a definition.
47	  Fix: Make the per-unit analysis an explicit parallel fan-out — "Spawn one subagent per qualifying unit. Each subagent returns ONLY: `{unit_name, files_count, exports_count, export_pattern, api_surface, scripts_assets, ccc_signals, strategy_used, confidence}`. No prose." Instruct the parent not to read unit source files before delegating (implicit-read trap).
48	
49	- **[D2] No script directory at all — the skill is 100% LLM-driven for operations that are deterministic by construction** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/` (no `scripts/`)
50	  Issue: Several operations in stages 02-04 are pure plumbing the LLM is paying tokens to perform:
51	  - manifest discovery (find package.json / Cargo.toml / pyproject.toml / go.mod across `project_paths[]`) — a 30-line python script with `pathlib.Path.rglob` returns structured JSON.
52	  - disqualification counts (file count < 3, LoC < 100, generated-code detection by `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `dist/` heuristics) — fully deterministic.
53	  - import-graph extraction for Forge/Deep tiers — even a regex-grade pass for Quick tier saves ~5-10k tokens per unit.
54	  - stack-skill co-import frequency (count of cross-unit imports across 3+ files) — pure counting.
55	  Fix: Add `scripts/scan-manifests.py` (returns structured catalog by project_path) and `scripts/disqualify-candidates.py` (input: boundary list; output: filtered list + reasons). Defer the import-graph extractor (multi-language complexity); the simpler scripts pay off first.
56	
57	- **[D3] "Read all files, then analyze" pattern in scan-project §2** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:42`
58	  Issue: "Return structured findings — file paths and types only, not contents" is the right idea but the LLM still does the scanning in-context.
59	  Fix: Roll up with D2 — the scan-manifests script also enumerates entry points and service configs (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, kubernetes manifests) in the same pass.
60	
61	- **[D4] Forge+ tier CCC integration references a nonexistent `knowledge/tool-resolution.md` resolution layer** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md:48`
62	  Issue: The instruction is "see `knowledge/tool-resolution.md`" but no such file exists in the skill, shared/, or any neighboring path. The Forge+ branch will fail or noop silently.
63	  Fix: Grep `_bmad/skf/knowledge/` if it exists; if not, either create the doc or simplify to "If CCC search is available in this environment, query `{unit_name} exports public API` and merge top-15 results with the scoped file list; otherwise skip."
64	
65	### Customization Surface (customize.toml)
66	
67	- **[C1] No `customize.toml` at all — workflow is fork-only for any org-level adjustment** | severity: high-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/` (no customize.toml)
68	  Issue: The skill has clear customization candidates that peers expose as scalars: the heuristics file path (orgs with house naming conventions or unusual stack patterns benefit from substituting), the brief schema asset, the report template, and `persistent_facts` for project-context.md.
69	  Fix: Add `customize.toml` modeled on `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` with:
70	  ```toml
71	  [workflow]
72	  activation_steps_prepend = []
73	  activation_steps_append = []
74	  persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]
75	  unit_detection_heuristics_path = ""   # default: references/unit-detection-heuristics.md
76	  brief_schema_path = ""                # default: assets/skill-brief-schema.md
77	  analysis_report_template_path = ""    # default: templates/analysis-report-template.md
78	  ```
79	  Wire each scalar into SKILL.md's On Activation per the resolve_customization.py pattern at `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:46-67`.
80	
81	- **[C2] Hardcoded `{templateFile}` and `{schemaFile}` paths inside reference frontmatter** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:5`, `recommend.md:5`, `generate-briefs.md:4`
82	  Issue: Each is declared as `templates/analysis-report-template.md` or `assets/skill-brief-schema.md` in stage frontmatter. Once customize.toml exists (C1), these must read from resolved scalars; otherwise overrides silently no-op (per principles file: hardcoded path next to declared scalar = override does nothing).
83	  Fix: After C1 is wired, replace stage-frontmatter hardcoded paths with `{briefSchemaPath}` / `{analysisReportTemplatePath}` / `{unitDetectionHeuristicsPath}` resolved during On Activation.
84	
85	- **[C3] No `on_complete` hook surface** | severity: low-opportunity | n/a
86	  Issue: Workflow produces multiple skill-brief.yaml files and stops; orgs may want to chain into checkpoint-preview, CI submission, or a linter. Single `on_complete` hook is low-cost to expose.
87	  Fix: Optional; defer if not requested.
88	
89	### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)
90	
91	- **[E1] No intent-before-ingestion: workflow scans the whole project before asking what the user actually wants out of the analysis** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:48-79`
92	  Issue: init.md asks for project path and scope hints but never asks why the user is analyzing — "all skills possible", "skills for this team's domain", "skills to onboard this service into our agent stack". Without intent, the recommendations stage shows every boundary that passed disqualification; a user with focused intent gets a noisier-than-needed list.
93	  Fix: Add a single open-floor question after path collection: "What are you hoping to get out of this analysis? Pointers to specific domains, target consumer agents, or constraints help me tune the recommendations." Capture into a frontmatter field (`intent_hint`) and reference it in recommend.md when ranking units.
94	
95	- **[E2] No headless arg-driven entry: `--headless` only flips gates to auto-proceed** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:32-34, 49-57`
96	  Issue: For an automator running this in CI, headless mode means "auto-confirm every recommendation" — which offers zero way to pre-supply `project_path`, `scope_hint`, or `intent_hint`. Peer (skf-brief-skill) accepts a rich pre-supplied input set in its Invocation Contract.
97	  Fix: Add `--project-path`, `--scope-hint`, `--intent-hint` as headless-only args. In init.md §3-4, branch on `{headless_mode}` to consume the args instead of prompting. Document in the Invocation Contract table.
98	
99	- **[E3] Resume protocol doesn't surface the workspace path on activation when the report already exists** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:24-29`, `continue.md:27`
100	  Issue: When init.md finds an existing report, it says "Resuming previous session..." and chains into continue.md, but the user never sees the report's path or its `lastContinued` timestamp until step 1b §3. The Decision-Log Workspace pattern in the principles file calls for surfacing the workspace path immediately.
101	  Fix: Quick edit in init.md §1 to print the report path + last-modified info before chaining.
102	
103	- **[E4] No graceful degradation when subagents aren't available at map-and-detect** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md:32`
104	  Issue: The fan-out fallback "If subprocess unavailable: Perform in main thread" exists at scan-project §2 but not at map-and-detect §2 (the more expensive stage).
105	  Fix: Add the same fallback sentence to map-and-detect.md §2.
106	
107	- **[E5] Per-unit `[Y/N/M]` in recommend.md is dense for large projects** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/recommend.md:79-86`
108	  Issue: The "1:Y, 2:Y, 3:N, 4:M" syntax is fine for small projects but tedious for 10+ units. A "select-all-default-Y" shortcut for cases where the user trusts the analysis would speed common-path usage.
109	  Fix: Add to the prompt: "Or respond `all-Y` / `all-N` to bulk-confirm, then individually flip exceptions."
110	
111	## Deterministic Summary
112	
113	- **path-standards:** 1 finding (high) — bare `_bmad/skf/` path in `references/health-check.md:3` frontmatter comment without `{project-root}` prefix. Trivial fix.
114	- **scripts:** 0 findings (info only — no `scripts/` directory exists). See D2 for opportunity-side cost.
115	- **workflow-integrity:** prepass pass — frontmatter clean (name + description with properly quoted trigger phrases), 7 sections at level 2, simple-workflow type matches structure. `total_stages: 0` reported because stage chaining via reference frontmatter `nextStepFile` isn't the standard schema — informational.
116	- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md is 66 lines / ~926 tokens — well under the 130-line ceiling. Reference files range from 23 (health-check.md) to 189 lines (generate-briefs.md, the only one approaching the lift threshold — see A5). No waste patterns, no back-references.
117	- **execution-deps:** prepass pass — no formal stage graph means no cycles or transitive redundancies could be detected; the implicit chain (init → scan → identify → map → recommend → generate → health-check) is linear and dependency-correct.
118	
119	## Strengths (preserve)
120	
121	- Stage chaining via reference-frontmatter `nextStepFile` is consistent and matches the peer.
122	- Resume protocol (continue.md) is implemented properly with stage-routing table.
123	- The unit-detection-heuristics.md asset is genuinely BMad-institutional content the LLM wouldn't generate by default — strong/moderate/weak signal taxonomy plus disqualification rules earn their keep.
124	- Frontmatter is canonical: name + quoted-trigger description only.
125	- Conventions block is stamped correctly per the principles file.
126	- generate-briefs.md §9 already writes the result contract per the shared schema (just not surfaced in SKILL.md — see A1).
127	- health-check.md correctly chains to the shared module-level health check and is appropriately minimal.
128	- Per-unit size-aware strategy selection in map-and-detect §2 (< 50 / 50-200 / 200+ files) is good engineering and should not be flattened.
129	
130	## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)
131	
132	1. **Foundation pass:** Add `customize.toml` + headless Result Contract / Exit Codes to SKILL.md + cancel-line `[X]` on the three confirm gates + `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` headers in every stage file. Resolves A1, A2, A3, A4, C1, C2, E2 (7 findings); effort: medium. Directly mirrors the peer envelope.
133	2. **Determinism pass:** Add `scripts/scan-manifests.py` (manifest + entry-point + service-config enumeration) and `scripts/disqualify-candidates.py` (deterministic filter) + define what "subprocess Pattern 1/2" actually means (or drop the labels) + make map-and-detect's per-unit step an explicit subagent fan-out. Resolves D1, D2, D3, and the orphaned forward-reference Broken item; effort: medium. Largest token-cost wins.
134	3. **Tightening pass:** Trim A6 (defensive workflow rule) + A7 (duplicate stepsCompleted rule), shrink generate-briefs.md per A5, add intent-before-ingestion question (E1), surface the resume path in init.md §1 (E3), add subagent fallback to map-and-detect (E4), bulk-confirm shortcut in recommend (E5), fix the path-standards `_bmad/skf/` violation, and resolve the CCC tool-resolution doc path (D4). Resolves A5, A6, A7, D4, E1, E3, E4, E5, plus the path-standards Broken item; effort: low. Polish lane bringing the skill to peer parity.
135

# Quality Findings: skf-analyze-source

**Grade:** Good
**Narrative:** A well-structured, multi-stage discovery workflow with clean stage chaining, a thoughtful tier-aware analysis model, and a serviceable result contract. To reach Excellent it needs the same Foundation+Tightening treatment given to skf-brief-skill: a customize.toml surface, an explicit headless Result Contract / Exit Codes block in SKILL.md, B6-style cancel-lines on the three confirm gates, and `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` wiring inside every reference. There is also one path-standards violation and one orphaned forward-reference (subprocess Patterns) that the LLM cannot resolve at runtime.

## Broken (critical/high)

- **Path standards violation: bare `_bmad/skf/` reference without `{project-root}` prefix** | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/health-check.md:3`. The frontmatter comment uses `_bmad/skf/` as a bare prefix; the path-standards scanner correctly flags it. Fix: rewrite the comment to refer to `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` (or rephrase to avoid the path entirely — the comment is explanatory only).
- **Orphaned forward-reference: "subprocess Pattern 1" and "Pattern 2" cited but never defined anywhere in the skill or shared/** | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:17` and `references/map-and-detect.md:15`. The LLM has no patterns file to consult, so the instruction is a noop at best and a confused tangent at worst. Fix: either inline what the "pattern" means (a per-unit subagent fan-out vs. a single-process scan) in the rule itself, or drop the Pattern N labels.

## Findings by Dimension

### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)

- **[A1] SKILL.md has no headless Result Contract / Exit Codes section; the contract lives buried at generate-briefs.md §9** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:49-58`

Excellent peer (skf-brief-skill) stamps `## Exit Codes` and `## Result Contract (Headless)` directly in SKILL.md so automators can read the contract without traversing six stage files.
  Fix: Add `## Exit Codes` table and `## Result Contract (Headless)` section to SKILL.md modeled on `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-114`. Choose an envelope name (e.g. `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`) and document `status`, `report_path`, `brief_paths[]`, `unit_counts`, `exit_code`, `halt_reason`.

  Issue: SKILL.md says "Always communicate in `{communication_language}`" but zero stage files reference `{communication_language}` or `{document_output_language}`. On compaction, the rule from SKILL.md disappears and the stage file becomes the only context — the LLM defaults to English regardless of config.
  Fix: Add a configuration header at the top of each stage prompt (after frontmatter, before `# Step N`) declaring `Communicate in {communication_language}` and, where the stage writes persisted artifact text (e.g. recommendation cards, brief description fields in generate-briefs), `Written artifact text is in {document_output_language}`.

- **[A3] Confirm gates have no Cancel / exit option — users can only `[C] Continue`, leaving them stuck if they want to abort cleanly** | severity: high | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:139`, `identify-units.md:123`, `recommend.md:156`
  Issue: All three Confirm Gates display `[C] Continue` only. A user who realizes mid-flow that they pointed the workflow at the wrong project has no graceful exit; they can only hard-kill the session, which orphans the partial report. skf-brief-skill defines a `[X] Cancel and exit` cancel-line with exit code 6 (`user-cancelled`).
  Fix: Add `[X] Cancel and exit` to every menu in scan-project §7, identify-units §7, recommend §8 (and the generate-briefs Y/N/M prompt at §4 which currently overloads "N" with "abort"). Define the cancel behavior in SKILL.md's Exit Codes block.

- **[A4] Workflow Rules omit the canonical language-token paragraph** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:24-34`
  Issue: The peer (`skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:36`) calls out both `{communication_language}` (user-facing prose) and `{document_output_language}` (persisted artifact text). The target skill only mentions `{communication_language}`.
  Fix: Update Workflow Rules to mirror peer phrasing: "Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the per-unit recommendation `description` and `scope.notes` persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`."

The LLM does not need the field-mapping spelled out as a table when the source-cell wording is "Confirmed name from step 05" — it already knows where the data is.
  Fix: Reduce the field mapping to a 2-3 line note ("All fields trace back to step 05 recommendation cards plus frontmatter; consult `{schemaFile}` for the field schema and constraints"). Compress the next-workflow recommendation table's right-hand explanations.

  - disqualification counts (file count < 3, LoC < 100, generated-code detection by `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `dist/` heuristics) — fully deterministic.
  - import-graph extraction for Forge/Deep tiers — even a regex-grade pass for Quick tier saves ~5-10k tokens per unit.
  - stack-skill co-import frequency (count of cross-unit imports across 3+ files) — pure counting.
  Fix: Add `scripts/scan-manifests.py` (returns structured catalog by project_path) and `scripts/disqualify-candidates.py` (input: boundary list; output: filtered list + reasons). Defer the import-graph extractor (multi-language complexity); the simpler scripts pay off first.

- **[D3] "Read all files, then analyze" pattern in scan-project §2** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md:42`

- **[D4] Forge+ tier CCC integration references a nonexistent `knowledge/tool-resolution.md` resolution layer** | severity: medium | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md:48`
  Issue: The instruction is "see `knowledge/tool-resolution.md`" but no such file exists in the skill, shared/, or any neighboring path. The Forge+ branch will fail or noop silently.
  Fix: Grep `_bmad/skf/knowledge/` if it exists; if not, either create the doc or simplify to "If CCC search is available in this environment, query `{unit_name} exports public API` and merge top-15 results with the scoped file list; otherwise skip."

### Customization Surface (customize.toml)

- **[C1] No `customize.toml` at all — workflow is fork-only for any org-level adjustment** | severity: high-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/` (no customize.toml)
  Issue: The skill has clear customization candidates that peers expose as scalars: the heuristics file path (orgs with house naming conventions or unusual stack patterns benefit from substituting), the brief schema asset, the report template, and `persistent_facts` for project-context.md.
  Fix: Add `customize.toml` modeled on `skf-brief-skill/customize.toml` with:
  ```toml
  [workflow]
  activation_steps_prepend = []
  activation_steps_append = []
  persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]
  unit_detection_heuristics_path = ""   # default: references/unit-detection-heuristics.md
  brief_schema_path = ""                # default: assets/skill-brief-schema.md
  analysis_report_template_path = ""    # default: templates/analysis-report-template.md
  ```
  Wire each scalar into SKILL.md's On Activation per the resolve_customization.py pattern at `skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:46-67`.

- **[C2] Hardcoded `{templateFile}` and `{schemaFile}` paths inside reference frontmatter** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:5`, `recommend.md:5`, `generate-briefs.md:4`
  Issue: Each is declared as `templates/analysis-report-template.md` or `assets/skill-brief-schema.md` in stage frontmatter. Once customize.toml exists (C1), these must read from resolved scalars; otherwise overrides silently no-op (per principles file: hardcoded path next to declared scalar = override does nothing).
  Fix: After C1 is wired, replace stage-frontmatter hardcoded paths with `{briefSchemaPath}` / `{analysisReportTemplatePath}` / `{unitDetectionHeuristicsPath}` resolved during On Activation.

- **[C3] No `on_complete` hook surface** | severity: low-opportunity | n/a
  Issue: Workflow produces multiple skill-brief.yaml files and stops; orgs may want to chain into checkpoint-preview, CI submission, or a linter. Single `on_complete` hook is low-cost to expose.
  Fix: Optional; defer if not requested.

### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)

- **[E1] No intent-before-ingestion: workflow scans the whole project before asking what the user actually wants out of the analysis** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:48-79`
  Issue: init.md asks for project path and scope hints but never asks why the user is analyzing — "all skills possible", "skills for this team's domain", "skills to onboard this service into our agent stack". Without intent, the recommendations stage shows every boundary that passed disqualification; a user with focused intent gets a noisier-than-needed list.
  Fix: Add a single open-floor question after path collection: "What are you hoping to get out of this analysis? Pointers to specific domains, target consumer agents, or constraints help me tune the recommendations." Capture into a frontmatter field (`intent_hint`) and reference it in recommend.md when ranking units.

- **[E2] No headless arg-driven entry: `--headless` only flips gates to auto-proceed** | severity: medium-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md:32-34, 49-57`
  Issue: For an automator running this in CI, headless mode means "auto-confirm every recommendation" — which offers zero way to pre-supply `project_path`, `scope_hint`, or `intent_hint`. Peer (skf-brief-skill) accepts a rich pre-supplied input set in its Invocation Contract.
  Fix: Add `--project-path`, `--scope-hint`, `--intent-hint` as headless-only args. In init.md §3-4, branch on `{headless_mode}` to consume the args instead of prompting. Document in the Invocation Contract table.

- **[E3] Resume protocol doesn't surface the workspace path on activation when the report already exists** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/init.md:24-29`, `continue.md:27`
  Issue: When init.md finds an existing report, it says "Resuming previous session..." and chains into continue.md, but the user never sees the report's path or its `lastContinued` timestamp until step 1b §3. The Decision-Log Workspace pattern in the principles file calls for surfacing the workspace path immediately.
  Fix: Quick edit in init.md §1 to print the report path + last-modified info before chaining.

- **[E4] No graceful degradation when subagents aren't available at map-and-detect** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md:32`
  Issue: The fan-out fallback "If subprocess unavailable: Perform in main thread" exists at scan-project §2 but not at map-and-detect §2 (the more expensive stage).
  Fix: Add the same fallback sentence to map-and-detect.md §2.

- **[E5] Per-unit `[Y/N/M]` in recommend.md is dense for large projects** | severity: low-opportunity | `src/skf-analyze-source/references/recommend.md:79-86`
  Issue: The "1:Y, 2:Y, 3:N, 4:M" syntax is fine for small projects but tedious for 10+ units. A "select-all-default-Y" shortcut for cases where the user trusts the analysis would speed common-path usage.
  Fix: Add to the prompt: "Or respond `all-Y` / `all-N` to bulk-confirm, then individually flip exceptions."

## Deterministic Summary

- **path-standards:** 1 finding (high) — bare `_bmad/skf/` path in `references/health-check.md:3` frontmatter comment without `{project-root}` prefix. Trivial fix.
- **scripts:** 0 findings (info only — no `scripts/` directory exists). See D2 for opportunity-side cost.
- **workflow-integrity:** prepass pass — frontmatter clean (name + description with properly quoted trigger phrases), 7 sections at level 2, simple-workflow type matches structure. `total_stages: 0` reported because stage chaining via reference frontmatter `nextStepFile` isn't the standard schema — informational.
- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md is 66 lines / ~926 tokens — well under the 130-line ceiling. Reference files range from 23 (health-check.md) to 189 lines (generate-briefs.md, the only one approaching the lift threshold — see A5). No waste patterns, no back-references.
- **execution-deps:** prepass pass — no formal stage graph means no cycles or transitive redundancies could be detected; the implicit chain (init → scan → identify → map → recommend → generate → health-check) is linear and dependency-correct.

## Strengths (preserve)

- Stage chaining via reference-frontmatter `nextStepFile` is consistent and matches the peer.
- Resume protocol (continue.md) is implemented properly with stage-routing table.
- The unit-detection-heuristics.md asset is genuinely BMad-institutional content the LLM wouldn't generate by default — strong/moderate/weak signal taxonomy plus disqualification rules earn their keep.
- Frontmatter is canonical: name + quoted-trigger description only.
- Conventions block is stamped correctly per the principles file.
- generate-briefs.md §9 already writes the result contract per the shared schema (just not surfaced in SKILL.md — see A1).
- health-check.md correctly chains to the shared module-level health check and is appropriately minimal.
- Per-unit size-aware strategy selection in map-and-detect §2 (< 50 / 50-200 / 200+ files) is good engineering and should not be flattened.

## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)

1. **Foundation pass:** Add `customize.toml` + headless Result Contract / Exit Codes to SKILL.md + cancel-line `[X]` on the three confirm gates + `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` headers in every stage file. Resolves A1, A2, A3, A4, C1, C2, E2 (7 findings); effort: medium. Directly mirrors the peer envelope.
2. **Determinism pass:** Add `scripts/scan-manifests.py` (manifest + entry-point + service-config enumeration) and `scripts/disqualify-candidates.py` (deterministic filter) + define what "subprocess Pattern 1/2" actually means (or drop the labels) + make map-and-detect's per-unit step an explicit subagent fan-out. Resolves D1, D2, D3, and the orphaned forward-reference Broken item; effort: medium. Largest token-cost wins.
3. **Tightening pass:** Trim A6 (defensive workflow rule) + A7 (duplicate stepsCompleted rule), shrink generate-briefs.md per A5, add intent-before-ingestion question (E1), surface the resume path in init.md §1 (E3), add subagent fallback to map-and-detect (E4), bulk-confirm shortcut in recommend (E5), fix the path-standards `_bmad/skf/` violation, and resolve the CCC tool-resolution doc path (D4). Resolves A5, A6, A7, D4, E1, E3, E4, E5, plus the path-standards Broken item; effort: low. Polish lane bringing the skill to peer parity.

content.content:
1	# Quality Findings: skf-audit-skill
2	
3	**Grade:** Good
4	**Narrative:** Structurally solid — SKILL.md is lean (68 lines), routing is intact, frontmatter chains cleanly, and the workflow's load-bearing institutional knowledge (manifest-vs-symlink drift gate, dirty-worktree probe, hash-prefix normalization, extractor canonicalization) is well-earned text that prevents real failure modes seen in commits #302/#312. To clear the Excellent bar, the skill needs the customization surface every peer SKF skill ships (no `customize.toml`), the headless contract every Excellent peer surfaces (no Exit Codes table, no `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON` stdout envelope), language-header consistency across carved files, and one path-standards fix.
5	
6	## Broken (critical/high)
7	
8	- **Bare `_bmad` path in comment** | references/health-check.md:3
9	  Issue: comment reads `(``_bmad/skf/`` when installed, ``src/`` during development)` without `{project-root}` prefix. The peer skf-brief-skill at the equivalent location uses `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` / `{project-root}/src/`.
10	  Fix: prepend `{project-root}/` to both path mentions inside the comment.
11	
12	## Findings by Dimension
13	
14	### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)
15	
16	- **[A1] Carved reference files do not carry `{communication_language}`** | severity: medium | references/init.md:1, references/re-index.md:1, references/structural-diff.md:1, references/semantic-diff.md:1, references/severity-classify.md:1, references/report.md:1
17	  Issue: principles file requires each carved file to use `{communication_language}` (and `{document_output_language}` where it produces a doc) so it survives context compaction standalone. SKILL.md:34 sets the rule once but no reference file restates or uses the variable. The drift report itself is written user-facing — `{document_output_language}` is the right knob for it.
18	  Fix: add a one-line "Always communicate in `{communication_language}`; drift report prose in `{document_output_language}`" rule at the top of each reference file (or in the per-step `## Rules` block).
19	
20	- **[A2] Inputs are under-declared for an analyze-and-report workflow** | severity: medium | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md:53
21	  Issue: Invocation Contract declares only `skill_name [required]`. The workflow actually consumes a half-dozen inputs in init.md (full-path override, tier override via preferences.yaml, degraded-mode opt-in, upstream-drift gate choice C/S/X, dirty-worktree sub-gate T/A/F). Headless callers and the automator archetype have no documented surface for pre-supplying these answers — they default-resolve silently.
22	  Fix: enumerate the optional inputs (`skill_path`, `tier_override`, `degraded`, `upstream_drift_choice`, `dirty_worktree_choice`, `force`) in the Inputs row so headless invocations are first-class. Mirror brief-skill's contract shape (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:86).
23	
24	- **[A3] Workflow Rules carries a brittle blanket clause** | severity: low | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md:33
25	  Issue: "If any instruction references a subprocess or tool you lack, achieve the outcome in your main context thread." This is duplicated in every step file's "Pattern N if available, else main thread" wording and overrides them weakly. Real fallback decisions are step-specific (e.g. structural-diff needs sequential iteration, semantic-diff short-circuits at non-Deep).
26	  Fix: drop the blanket clause; per-step fallback wording already covers it.
27	
28	### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)
29	
30	- **[D1] Hash comparison and prefix normalization belong in a script** | severity: medium | references/structural-diff.md:86-100 (§4b Script/Asset Drift)
31	  Issue: For each file in `provenance-map.file_entries`, the LLM is told to compute SHA-256 of the source file, strip the algorithm prefix off the stored hash (per the §4b note that fixed the original false-positive bug), and compare. This is pure plumbing — identical input → identical output, unit-testable. At even modest skill sizes (20+ tracked file_entries) the LLM tax for hashing prose alone is meaningful, plus the LLM has no native SHA-256 — it has to shell out per file regardless.
32	  Fix: ship `scripts/compare-file-hashes.py {provenance-map.json} {source-root}` that returns a compact JSON of `{added: [], removed: [], changed: [{path, stored_hash, current_hash}]}`. The LLM consumes the JSON; the prefix-stripping logic and the writer-vs-reader compatibility note (currently load-bearing prose) move into the script and stop being a hazard.
33	
34	- **[D2] Pre-pass opportunity: provenance-map normalization** | severity: medium | references/init.md:79-122 (loading provenance map, detecting stack skill, resolving source path) + references/structural-diff.md:32-44 (canonicalization transforms)
35	  Issue: init.md and structural-diff.md both walk the provenance map to extract: `entries[].source_file`, `file_entries[].source_file`, `provenance_version`, `skill_type`, top-level `libraries` key, `__init__.py` re-export map. Then structural-diff.md re-walks to apply three canonicalization transforms (quote-style, stdlib module qualification, public-API re-export). The bounded scan list, the stack-skill flags, and the re-export map are all deterministic projections.
36	  Fix: a small pre-pass script (`scripts/load-provenance.py`) that returns `{bounded_scan_files: [...], is_stack_skill, legacy_stack_provenance, source_root, baseline_commit, baseline_ref, reexport_map: {...}}` would replace ~80 lines of LLM walk-and-extract prose with one read of a JSON.
37	
38	- **[D3] Bounded-scan extraction has no `Read multiple files in one batch` framing** | severity: low | references/re-index.md:77-89 (§3 Extract Current Exports)
39	  Issue: "For EACH file in the bounded scan list ... launch a subprocess" is correct under Pattern 2, but for the main-thread fallback it doesn't tell the LLM to batch Read calls in one message (parallel-by-default) when subprocess is unavailable. The current fallback wording ("Perform extraction in main thread, processing each file sequentially") locks in sequential when a batched-Read pass would be free.
40	  Fix: amend the fallback line: "Perform extraction in main thread, batching Read calls across files in one message where the bounded scan list permits."
41	
42	### Customization Surface (customize.toml)
43	
44	- **[C1] No `customize.toml` exists** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-audit-skill/ (file absent)
45	  Issue: skf-audit-skill is the only first-party SKF workflow without `customize.toml`. The four peer skills (skf-brief-skill, skf-create-skill, skf-update-skill, skf-setup) all ship one. Without it: (a) `persistent_facts` discovery (the BMad default `["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`) is unreachable; (b) the drift-report template (`assets/drift-report-template.md`) is not overrideable, so an org wanting house-styled drift reports must fork the skill; (c) `activation_steps_prepend` / `activation_steps_append` for org-wide pre-flight (compliance, auth) is unavailable.
46	  Fix: ship a baseline `customize.toml` mirroring skf-brief-skill's shape — empty `activation_steps_prepend`/`activation_steps_append`, seeded `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`, and one lifted scalar: `drift_report_template_path = ""` with fallback to `assets/drift-report-template.md`. Wire the scalar in On Activation via `python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py --skill {skill-root} --key workflow`, matching the brief-skill pattern at src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:47-67.
47	
48	- **[C2] `severity-rules.md` is a candidate for an `*_template` scalar** | severity: low-opportunity | references/severity-classify.md:4 (`severityRulesFile: 'references/severity-rules.md'`)
49	  Issue: Severity thresholds are organizational policy (what counts as CRITICAL vs HIGH for a given team's risk tolerance). The file is referenced as `severityRulesFile` frontmatter scalar in severity-classify.md but cannot be redirected via customize.toml. Teams wanting stricter or looser drift thresholds must fork.
50	  Fix: when adding `customize.toml` per C1, also expose `severity_rules_path = ""` (default fallback to `references/severity-rules.md`). Low-opportunity because severity tables are stable institutional knowledge; flag it only because the seam is already present in the frontmatter.
51	
52	### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)
53	
54	- **[E1] No Exit Codes table** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md (absent)
55	  Issue: The Excellent peer brief-skill (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-104) ships a stable Exit Codes table so headless automators can branch on failure class. audit-skill has half a dozen HARD HALT paths (skill not found at resolved path, setup-forge not run, manifest-vs-symlink user abort, upstream-drift user abort, dirty-worktree user abort, source dir missing) that currently emit free-text prose and no exit code. Pipelines wrapping `audit-skill` cannot distinguish "no setup forge" from "user aborted upstream gate" without grepping.
56	  Fix: add an Exit Codes table (success=0; input-missing=2; resolution-failure=3 for missing forge-tier.yaml / missing source dir; user-cancelled=6 for X selections at the upstream-drift / dirty-worktree / baseline gates). Mirror brief-skill's table shape.
57	
58	- **[E2] No `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON` stdout envelope for headless callers** | severity: high-opportunity | references/report.md:169-171 (Result Contract)
59	  Issue: report.md writes a result JSON to disk at `{forge_version}/audit-skill-result-{timestamp}.json` (+ `-latest.json` copy), but does not emit a single-line `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON: {...}` envelope on stdout. Brief-skill (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:106-114) and update-skill both ship the envelope so a chaining workflow can read drift_score + report_path from a captured stdout line without filesystem polling.
60	  Fix: emit `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","skill_name":"…","drift_score":"CLEAN|MINOR|SIGNIFICANT|CRITICAL","report_path":"…","next_workflow":"update-skill|null","audit_ref":"…","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null}` on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true (and mirror-on-stderr on HALT, per the brief-skill pattern). Add it to report.md §5 alongside the on-disk Result Contract.
61	
62	- **[E3] Update mode / Resume protocol absent** | severity: medium-opportunity | references/init.md:219 (Step 6 Create Drift Report)
63	  Issue: Drift reports are timestamped (`drift-report-{timestamp}.md`) and the workflow always creates a new one. There's no detection of an existing recent report for the same skill — a user who re-runs after fixing one finding gets a fresh report with no diff against the prior one. The principles file's Decision-Log Workspace pattern would surface "skill X was last audited 3 days ago at SIGNIFICANT — re-audit, diff against prior report, or compare?" The drift report itself is appended-to across steps and has frontmatter (`stepsCompleted`, `drift_score`, `nextWorkflow`) — it's already a workspace artifact in shape, just missing the resume gesture.
64	  Fix: at init.md §6, before creating the new report, glob `{forge_version}/drift-report-*.md` and if a recent (< 7 days) report exists for the same `audit_ref`, surface a soft gate: `[F] Fresh audit (default) / [D] Diff against prior report / [R] Resume the prior report (recover incomplete run)`. Headless default = F.
65	
66	- **[E4] Degraded-mode "T1-low" findings are second-class without explicit signaling in the report header** | severity: low-opportunity | references/init.md:104, references/re-index.md:71, references/report.md:97 (Provenance section)
67	  Issue: When the user selects `[D]egraded mode` at init.md §4 (no provenance map), all findings are tagged T1-low — but the drift-report template doesn't surface "this entire report ran in degraded mode" prominently at the top. The Provenance section at the bottom carries the `Mode: degraded` cell, but a reader scanning the summary first sees "CRITICAL: 5 findings" without knowing those are T1-low confidence.
68	  Fix: in assets/drift-report-template.md, add a `**Confidence Mode:** {normal | degraded — all findings T1-low}` line under the Audit Summary heading at line 24, and have init.md §6 / report.md §1 populate it from the resolved mode. Cheap-to-add visual cue that prevents misreading.
69	
70	- **[E5] No proactive guidance when audit detects "skill needs major rework" rather than "minor drift"** | severity: low-opportunity | references/report.md:75-90 (Workflow Recommendation)
71	  Issue: The Workflow Recommendation branches on count/severity but always points at `update-skill`. When drift score is CRITICAL with >20 findings concentrated in deleted/renamed packages (the rollup case from structural-diff §5), update-skill is a worse fit than re-running brief-skill → create-skill (which the source-version-bump branch at report.md:80-83 already half-acknowledges). The signal exists (rollup-row count, deleted-package density) but the recommendation doesn't read it.
72	  Fix: add a fourth branch for "structural rebuild" (CRITICAL + ≥10-finding rollup row OR `audit_ref != baseline_ref` AND new top-level modules detected): "Recommend re-running brief-skill to refresh scope, then create-skill, rather than incremental update-skill — the drift surface is large enough that incremental remediation will leave coverage gaps."
73	
74	## Deterministic Summary
75	
76	- **path-standards:** 1 finding — high-severity bare `_bmad` in references/health-check.md:3 comment. Easy fix, matches existing peer pattern.
77	- **scripts:** 0 findings — no `scripts/` directory exists. (D1, D2 surface script *opportunities* the scanner can't see by definition.)
78	- **workflow-integrity:** clean prepass. 7 sections at expected positions in SKILL.md (Overview, Conventions, Role, Workflow Rules, Stages, Invocation Contract, On Activation). Workflow-type classified as `simple-workflow` (stage routing via `nextStepFile` frontmatter, no `<step n>` sub-protocol). No stage-routing orphans or missing references.
79	- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md is 68 lines / 943 tokens — well under the 130-line hard ceiling. References sized 199 to 4661 tokens (init.md is the heaviest at 268 lines, justified by the load-bearing manifest-vs-symlink and upstream-drift / dirty-worktree gates). No waste patterns flagged. Zero back-references between files. Overall prose budget is healthy.
80	- **execution-deps:** No dependency-graph issues — stages chain linearly via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, no cycles, no transitive redundancies. The linear chain is appropriate for a deterministic audit workflow.
81	
82	## Strengths (preserve)
83	
84	- Load-bearing safety prose: manifest-vs-symlink drift gate (init.md:31-56), dirty-worktree probe with stash/abort/force handling (init.md:187-208), `sha256:` prefix normalization (structural-diff.md:93), extractor canonicalization (structural-diff.md:32-44), three QMD-collection states distinguished in semantic-diff (§2). Each fix has a body count in the commit history (#302, #312) — exactly the "earns its keep" hard-rule prose the principles file calls out.
85	- Tier-graceful degradation throughout — Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep with explicit skip notices, never silent.
86	- Bounded-scan discipline (re-index.md:48-75) — audit-skill explicitly does NOT discover new files and routes that responsibility to update-skill. Cohesion across SKF pipeline preserved.
87	- Stack-skill awareness (init.md:108-122, structural-diff.md:101-117) — handles compose-mode constituent freshness and code-mode per-library diffs.
88	- Provenance section in the drift report exposes the audit-ref vs baseline-ref split so readers can tell which comparison ran (report.md:108-113).
89	
90	## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)
91	
92	1. **Add headless contract + Exit Codes + stdout envelope** — resolves E1, E2, A2 (3 findings). Effort: medium. Brings audit-skill to parity with brief-skill's contract shape; unlocks the automator archetype as a first-class user. Mirror src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-114 verbatim.
93	2. **Ship baseline `customize.toml` + Foundation pass** — resolves C1, C2, A1 (3 findings) plus the broken path-standards finding. Effort: low. Wire `resolve_customization.py` in On Activation, seed `persistent_facts`, lift `drift_report_template_path` + `severity_rules_path` scalars (empty default, fallback to bundled), add the `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` header to each carved file. Closes the Foundation half of the elevation pair.
94	3. **Extract two pre-pass scripts (file-hash diff + provenance normalization)** — resolves D1, D2 (2 findings, ~80-100 lines of LLM walk-and-extract prose lifted out, plus all SHA-256 plumbing). Effort: medium. The hash-prefix normalization story (currently load-bearing prose explaining a writer-vs-reader bug) becomes a script implementation detail; the bounded-scan + stack-skill + re-export map become a single JSON read at step entry.
95
