# Quality Findings: skf-audit-skill

**Grade:** Good
**Narrative:** Structurally solid — SKILL.md is lean (68 lines), routing is intact, frontmatter chains cleanly, and the workflow's load-bearing institutional knowledge (manifest-vs-symlink drift gate, dirty-worktree probe, hash-prefix normalization, extractor canonicalization) is well-earned text that prevents real failure modes seen in commits #302/#312. To clear the Excellent bar, the skill needs the customization surface every peer SKF skill ships (no `customize.toml`), the headless contract every Excellent peer surfaces (no Exit Codes table, no `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON` stdout envelope), language-header consistency across carved files, and one path-standards fix.

## Broken (critical/high)

- **Bare `_bmad` path in comment** | references/health-check.md:3
  Issue: comment reads `(``_bmad/skf/`` when installed, ``src/`` during development)` without `{project-root}` prefix. The peer skf-brief-skill at the equivalent location uses `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` / `{project-root}/src/`.
  Fix: prepend `{project-root}/` to both path mentions inside the comment.

## Findings by Dimension

### Architecture (structural integrity, prose craft, cohesion)

- **[A1] Carved reference files do not carry `{communication_language}`** | severity: medium | references/init.md:1, references/re-index.md:1, references/structural-diff.md:1, references/semantic-diff.md:1, references/severity-classify.md:1, references/report.md:1
  Issue: principles file requires each carved file to use `{communication_language}` (and `{document_output_language}` where it produces a doc) so it survives context compaction standalone. SKILL.md:34 sets the rule once but no reference file restates or uses the variable. The drift report itself is written user-facing — `{document_output_language}` is the right knob for it.
  Fix: add a one-line "Always communicate in `{communication_language}`; drift report prose in `{document_output_language}`" rule at the top of each reference file (or in the per-step `## Rules` block).

- **[A2] Inputs are under-declared for an analyze-and-report workflow** | severity: medium | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md:53
  Issue: Invocation Contract declares only `skill_name [required]`. The workflow actually consumes a half-dozen inputs in init.md (full-path override, tier override via preferences.yaml, degraded-mode opt-in, upstream-drift gate choice C/S/X, dirty-worktree sub-gate T/A/F). Headless callers and the automator archetype have no documented surface for pre-supplying these answers — they default-resolve silently.
  Fix: enumerate the optional inputs (`skill_path`, `tier_override`, `degraded`, `upstream_drift_choice`, `dirty_worktree_choice`, `force`) in the Inputs row so headless invocations are first-class. Mirror brief-skill's contract shape (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:86).

- **[A3] Workflow Rules carries a brittle blanket clause** | severity: low | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md:33
  Issue: "If any instruction references a subprocess or tool you lack, achieve the outcome in your main context thread." This is duplicated in every step file's "Pattern N if available, else main thread" wording and overrides them weakly. Real fallback decisions are step-specific (e.g. structural-diff needs sequential iteration, semantic-diff short-circuits at non-Deep).
  Fix: drop the blanket clause; per-step fallback wording already covers it.

### Determinism (intelligence placement, parallelization, script opportunities)

- **[D1] Hash comparison and prefix normalization belong in a script** | severity: medium | references/structural-diff.md:86-100 (§4b Script/Asset Drift)
  Issue: For each file in `provenance-map.file_entries`, the LLM is told to compute SHA-256 of the source file, strip the algorithm prefix off the stored hash (per the §4b note that fixed the original false-positive bug), and compare. This is pure plumbing — identical input → identical output, unit-testable. At even modest skill sizes (20+ tracked file_entries) the LLM tax for hashing prose alone is meaningful, plus the LLM has no native SHA-256 — it has to shell out per file regardless.
  Fix: ship `scripts/compare-file-hashes.py {provenance-map.json} {source-root}` that returns a compact JSON of `{added: [], removed: [], changed: [{path, stored_hash, current_hash}]}`. The LLM consumes the JSON; the prefix-stripping logic and the writer-vs-reader compatibility note (currently load-bearing prose) move into the script and stop being a hazard.

- **[D2] Pre-pass opportunity: provenance-map normalization** | severity: medium | references/init.md:79-122 (loading provenance map, detecting stack skill, resolving source path) + references/structural-diff.md:32-44 (canonicalization transforms)
  Issue: init.md and structural-diff.md both walk the provenance map to extract: `entries[].source_file`, `file_entries[].source_file`, `provenance_version`, `skill_type`, top-level `libraries` key, `__init__.py` re-export map. Then structural-diff.md re-walks to apply three canonicalization transforms (quote-style, stdlib module qualification, public-API re-export). The bounded scan list, the stack-skill flags, and the re-export map are all deterministic projections.
  Fix: a small pre-pass script (`scripts/load-provenance.py`) that returns `{bounded_scan_files: [...], is_stack_skill, legacy_stack_provenance, source_root, baseline_commit, baseline_ref, reexport_map: {...}}` would replace ~80 lines of LLM walk-and-extract prose with one read of a JSON.

- **[D3] Bounded-scan extraction has no `Read multiple files in one batch` framing** | severity: low | references/re-index.md:77-89 (§3 Extract Current Exports)
  Issue: "For EACH file in the bounded scan list ... launch a subprocess" is correct under Pattern 2, but for the main-thread fallback it doesn't tell the LLM to batch Read calls in one message (parallel-by-default) when subprocess is unavailable. The current fallback wording ("Perform extraction in main thread, processing each file sequentially") locks in sequential when a batched-Read pass would be free.
  Fix: amend the fallback line: "Perform extraction in main thread, batching Read calls across files in one message where the bounded scan list permits."

### Customization Surface (customize.toml)

- **[C1] No `customize.toml` exists** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-audit-skill/ (file absent)
  Issue: skf-audit-skill is the only first-party SKF workflow without `customize.toml`. The four peer skills (skf-brief-skill, skf-create-skill, skf-update-skill, skf-setup) all ship one. Without it: (a) `persistent_facts` discovery (the BMad default `["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`) is unreachable; (b) the drift-report template (`assets/drift-report-template.md`) is not overrideable, so an org wanting house-styled drift reports must fork the skill; (c) `activation_steps_prepend` / `activation_steps_append` for org-wide pre-flight (compliance, auth) is unavailable.
  Fix: ship a baseline `customize.toml` mirroring skf-brief-skill's shape — empty `activation_steps_prepend`/`activation_steps_append`, seeded `persistent_facts = ["file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md"]`, and one lifted scalar: `drift_report_template_path = ""` with fallback to `assets/drift-report-template.md`. Wire the scalar in On Activation via `python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py --skill {skill-root} --key workflow`, matching the brief-skill pattern at src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:47-67.

- **[C2] `severity-rules.md` is a candidate for an `*_template` scalar** | severity: low-opportunity | references/severity-classify.md:4 (`severityRulesFile: 'references/severity-rules.md'`)
  Issue: Severity thresholds are organizational policy (what counts as CRITICAL vs HIGH for a given team's risk tolerance). The file is referenced as `severityRulesFile` frontmatter scalar in severity-classify.md but cannot be redirected via customize.toml. Teams wanting stricter or looser drift thresholds must fork.
  Fix: when adding `customize.toml` per C1, also expose `severity_rules_path = ""` (default fallback to `references/severity-rules.md`). Low-opportunity because severity tables are stable institutional knowledge; flag it only because the seam is already present in the frontmatter.

### Enhancement (UX gaps, headless contract, edge cases, facilitative patterns)

- **[E1] No Exit Codes table** | severity: high-opportunity | src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md (absent)
  Issue: The Excellent peer brief-skill (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-104) ships a stable Exit Codes table so headless automators can branch on failure class. audit-skill has half a dozen HARD HALT paths (skill not found at resolved path, setup-forge not run, manifest-vs-symlink user abort, upstream-drift user abort, dirty-worktree user abort, source dir missing) that currently emit free-text prose and no exit code. Pipelines wrapping `audit-skill` cannot distinguish "no setup forge" from "user aborted upstream gate" without grepping.
  Fix: add an Exit Codes table (success=0; input-missing=2; resolution-failure=3 for missing forge-tier.yaml / missing source dir; user-cancelled=6 for X selections at the upstream-drift / dirty-worktree / baseline gates). Mirror brief-skill's table shape.

- **[E2] No `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON` stdout envelope for headless callers** | severity: high-opportunity | references/report.md:169-171 (Result Contract)
  Issue: report.md writes a result JSON to disk at `{forge_version}/audit-skill-result-{timestamp}.json` (+ `-latest.json` copy), but does not emit a single-line `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON: {...}` envelope on stdout. Brief-skill (src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:106-114) and update-skill both ship the envelope so a chaining workflow can read drift_score + report_path from a captured stdout line without filesystem polling.
  Fix: emit `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","skill_name":"…","drift_score":"CLEAN|MINOR|SIGNIFICANT|CRITICAL","report_path":"…","next_workflow":"update-skill|null","audit_ref":"…","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null}` on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true (and mirror-on-stderr on HALT, per the brief-skill pattern). Add it to report.md §5 alongside the on-disk Result Contract.

- **[E3] Update mode / Resume protocol absent** | severity: medium-opportunity | references/init.md:219 (Step 6 Create Drift Report)
  Issue: Drift reports are timestamped (`drift-report-{timestamp}.md`) and the workflow always creates a new one. There's no detection of an existing recent report for the same skill — a user who re-runs after fixing one finding gets a fresh report with no diff against the prior one. The principles file's Decision-Log Workspace pattern would surface "skill X was last audited 3 days ago at SIGNIFICANT — re-audit, diff against prior report, or compare?" The drift report itself is appended-to across steps and has frontmatter (`stepsCompleted`, `drift_score`, `nextWorkflow`) — it's already a workspace artifact in shape, just missing the resume gesture.
  Fix: at init.md §6, before creating the new report, glob `{forge_version}/drift-report-*.md` and if a recent (< 7 days) report exists for the same `audit_ref`, surface a soft gate: `[F] Fresh audit (default) / [D] Diff against prior report / [R] Resume the prior report (recover incomplete run)`. Headless default = F.

- **[E4] Degraded-mode "T1-low" findings are second-class without explicit signaling in the report header** | severity: low-opportunity | references/init.md:104, references/re-index.md:71, references/report.md:97 (Provenance section)
  Issue: When the user selects `[D]egraded mode` at init.md §4 (no provenance map), all findings are tagged T1-low — but the drift-report template doesn't surface "this entire report ran in degraded mode" prominently at the top. The Provenance section at the bottom carries the `Mode: degraded` cell, but a reader scanning the summary first sees "CRITICAL: 5 findings" without knowing those are T1-low confidence.
  Fix: in assets/drift-report-template.md, add a `**Confidence Mode:** {normal | degraded — all findings T1-low}` line under the Audit Summary heading at line 24, and have init.md §6 / report.md §1 populate it from the resolved mode. Cheap-to-add visual cue that prevents misreading.

- **[E5] No proactive guidance when audit detects "skill needs major rework" rather than "minor drift"** | severity: low-opportunity | references/report.md:75-90 (Workflow Recommendation)
  Issue: The Workflow Recommendation branches on count/severity but always points at `update-skill`. When drift score is CRITICAL with >20 findings concentrated in deleted/renamed packages (the rollup case from structural-diff §5), update-skill is a worse fit than re-running brief-skill → create-skill (which the source-version-bump branch at report.md:80-83 already half-acknowledges). The signal exists (rollup-row count, deleted-package density) but the recommendation doesn't read it.
  Fix: add a fourth branch for "structural rebuild" (CRITICAL + ≥10-finding rollup row OR `audit_ref != baseline_ref` AND new top-level modules detected): "Recommend re-running brief-skill to refresh scope, then create-skill, rather than incremental update-skill — the drift surface is large enough that incremental remediation will leave coverage gaps."

## Deterministic Summary

- **path-standards:** 1 finding — high-severity bare `_bmad` in references/health-check.md:3 comment. Easy fix, matches existing peer pattern.
- **scripts:** 0 findings — no `scripts/` directory exists. (D1, D2 surface script *opportunities* the scanner can't see by definition.)
- **workflow-integrity:** clean prepass. 7 sections at expected positions in SKILL.md (Overview, Conventions, Role, Workflow Rules, Stages, Invocation Contract, On Activation). Workflow-type classified as `simple-workflow` (stage routing via `nextStepFile` frontmatter, no `<step n>` sub-protocol). No stage-routing orphans or missing references.
- **prompt-metrics:** SKILL.md is 68 lines / 943 tokens — well under the 130-line hard ceiling. References sized 199 to 4661 tokens (init.md is the heaviest at 268 lines, justified by the load-bearing manifest-vs-symlink and upstream-drift / dirty-worktree gates). No waste patterns flagged. Zero back-references between files. Overall prose budget is healthy.
- **execution-deps:** No dependency-graph issues — stages chain linearly via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, no cycles, no transitive redundancies. The linear chain is appropriate for a deterministic audit workflow.

## Strengths (preserve)

- Load-bearing safety prose: manifest-vs-symlink drift gate (init.md:31-56), dirty-worktree probe with stash/abort/force handling (init.md:187-208), `sha256:` prefix normalization (structural-diff.md:93), extractor canonicalization (structural-diff.md:32-44), three QMD-collection states distinguished in semantic-diff (§2). Each fix has a body count in the commit history (#302, #312) — exactly the "earns its keep" hard-rule prose the principles file calls out.
- Tier-graceful degradation throughout — Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep with explicit skip notices, never silent.
- Bounded-scan discipline (re-index.md:48-75) — audit-skill explicitly does NOT discover new files and routes that responsibility to update-skill. Cohesion across SKF pipeline preserved.
- Stack-skill awareness (init.md:108-122, structural-diff.md:101-117) — handles compose-mode constituent freshness and code-mode per-library diffs.
- Provenance section in the drift report exposes the audit-ref vs baseline-ref split so readers can tell which comparison ran (report.md:108-113).

## Recommendations (top 3 ranked by impact)

1. **Add headless contract + Exit Codes + stdout envelope** — resolves E1, E2, A2 (3 findings). Effort: medium. Brings audit-skill to parity with brief-skill's contract shape; unlocks the automator archetype as a first-class user. Mirror src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md:93-114 verbatim.
2. **Ship baseline `customize.toml` + Foundation pass** — resolves C1, C2, A1 (3 findings) plus the broken path-standards finding. Effort: low. Wire `resolve_customization.py` in On Activation, seed `persistent_facts`, lift `drift_report_template_path` + `severity_rules_path` scalars (empty default, fallback to bundled), add the `{communication_language}` / `{document_output_language}` header to each carved file. Closes the Foundation half of the elevation pair.
3. **Extract two pre-pass scripts (file-hash diff + provenance normalization)** — resolves D1, D2 (2 findings, ~80-100 lines of LLM walk-and-extract prose lifted out, plus all SHA-256 plumbing). Effort: medium. The hash-prefix normalization story (currently load-bearing prose explaining a writer-vs-reader bug) becomes a script implementation detail; the bounded-scan + stack-skill + re-export map become a single JSON read at step entry.

input.content:
# SKF Feature Workflows Elevation — Cross-Skill Synthesis

**Date:** 2026-05-15
**Scope:** QS, SS, AN, AS, TS — elevate to Excellent
**Plan target:** 2 PRs (Foundation + Tightening) + 1 tracking issue for substantive deferrals

All 5 scans graded **Good**. Same elevation pattern as Core (#306/#323) applies cleanly.

---

## Cross-Skill Pattern Map (the genius compression)

### Foundation PR scope — items that exist in ≥2 skills, mechanical, batchable

| Item | QS | SS | AN | AS | TS | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **F1** No customize.toml | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Ship customize.toml in each skill mirroring skf-brief-skill shape |
| **F2** No customization resolver step in `## On Activation` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Wire `resolve_customization.py` call per peer pattern |
| **F3** Reference files missing language-config header | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Stamp `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. Artifact text in {document_output_language}. -->` (or shorter where no doc emitted) |
| **F4** Missing `## Exit Codes` table in SKILL.md | partial | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Add table per skf-brief-skill SKILL.md:93-105 |
| **F5** Missing `## Result Contract (Headless)` block in SKILL.md | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Add block + `SKF_*_RESULT_JSON:` stdout envelope where missing |
| **F6** No `[X] Cancel and exit` on interactive gates | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | Wire to exit code 6 (user-cancelled) per brief-skill |
| **F7** `{document_output_language}` not threaded into artifact-producing steps | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Add to relevant reference files |
| **F8** Defensive "subprocess fallback" workflow rule (no-op padding) | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Drop the bullet from SKILL.md Workflow Rules |
| **F9** Bare `_bmad/skf/` path in health-check.md frontmatter comment | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (report.md:8,96) | Prepend `{project-root}/` |

### Foundation PR scope — skill-specific high-impact items folded in

- **QS-F**: `{forger_root}` → `{sidecar_path}` (B2 from QS — runtime bug, 1-line fix)
- **TS-F**: Trim `## Conventions` block to canonical 5-bullet shape (TS A3)
- **SS-F**: Description trigger phrase expansion (SS A2 — 1 line)

### Tightening PR scope — low/medium-severity remainders + remaining Broken items

| Item | Skill | Type | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **T1** Ecosystem-check terminal branches missing exit code/envelope | QS | broken | Add exit-code mapping + HARD HALT envelope per existing pattern |
| **T2** Cross-dir `./references/{library}.md` link bug | SS | broken | Strip `./`; fix in both compile-stack.md:77 AND assets/stack-skill-template.md:35 |
| **T3** Orphaned "subprocess Pattern 1/2" references | AN | broken | Either define inline or drop the labels (drop is cleaner) |
| **T4** `knowledge/tool-resolution.md` referenced but missing | AN | broken | Simplify the Forge+ CCC instruction to be self-contained |
| **T5** Reference-file boilerplate cleanup (`## Rules`/`## MANDATORY SEQUENCE`/`CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` repeated across 7 step files) | TS | A4 | Drop scaffolding, keep step goal + work + chain line |
| **T6** "Auto-Proceed" subsections duplicate chain instruction | TS | A5 | Collapse into one closing line |
| **T7** `health-check.md` 25-line shim for 2-line probe | TS | A6 | Shrink to ≤6 lines (don't fully inline — keep shared-protocol entry point) |
| **T8** Workflow Rules duplicates / no-op directives | QS, AN, AS, TS | A5/A6/A7 | Trim to load-bearing rules only |
| **T9** Batch Mode at SKILL.md scope (~95 lines of single-target-user weight) | QS | A4 | DEFER — substantive carve, see Tracking Issue |
| **T10** Inline JSON schema templates ~80 lines | SS | D4 | Optional asset lift; defer if low ROI |
| **T11** Spec-ID inline codes (B1/B2/M5...) without glossary | TS | A8 | Drop codes inline (defer glossary creation) |
| **T12** Progress event slug list duplicates Stages table | QS | E4 | Replace with one-liner pointer |
| **T13** Pre-flight write probe in On Activation | QS | E2 | Mirror brief-skill 5-second probe |
| **T14** Resume protocol surface (decision-log gesture) | AN, SS, AS | E3 | DEFER substantive DLW; small "resume hint" tweaks OK |
| **T15** Headless arg surface — `--project-path`, `--scope-hint`, `--intent-hint` etc. | AN, AS, TS | E2/E3 | Add documented optional inputs (mostly doc edits) |

### Tracking Issue — Substantive deferrals (no PR)

Per user's strict 2-PR target, these become a tracking issue mirroring #307:

- **TR1** New scripts/ extractions:
  - SS: `scan-manifests.py`, `enumerate-skills.py`, `pair-intersect.py` (D1, D2, D5)
  - AN: `scan-manifests.py`, `disqualify-candidates.py` (D2, D3)
  - AS: `compare-file-hashes.py`, `load-provenance.py` (D1, D2)
  - TS: `scan-skill-md-structure.py` (D1, D2, D3)
- **TR2** TS compute-score.py unit tests + argparse (D4, D5)
- **TR3** Subagent fan-out for AN map-and-detect per-unit (D1)
- **TR4** TS subagent return-format consistency pass (D6)
- **TR5** Decision-Log Workspace pattern for SS, AS (full resume protocol)
- **TR6** Carve QS Batch Mode to `references/batch-mode.md`
- **TR7** Carve SS provenance-map.json schemas to `assets/` (D4)
- **TR8** New headless modes: TS `--no-discovery`/`--no-health-check`, AN `--intent-hint`, etc.
- **TR9** `on_complete` hook scalars (TS, AN)
- **TR10** `default_threshold` scalar (TS C2)

---

## Foundation PR — File Inventory (estimate)

**5 new customize.toml files:**
- src/skf-quick-skill/customize.toml
- src/skf-create-stack-skill/customize.toml
- src/skf-analyze-source/customize.toml
- src/skf-audit-skill/customize.toml
- src/skf-test-skill/customize.toml

**5 SKILL.md edits** (Exit Codes + Result Contract + On Activation resolver + Workflow Rules trim):
- src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md
- src/skf-create-stack-skill/SKILL.md
- src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md
- src/skf-audit-skill/SKILL.md
- src/skf-test-skill/SKILL.md

**~50 reference file edits** (language headers):
- src/skf-quick-skill/references/*.md (10 files)
- src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/*.md (15 files)
- src/skf-analyze-source/references/*.md (8 files)
- src/skf-audit-skill/references/*.md (7 files)
- src/skf-test-skill/references/*.md (8 files)

**~5 health-check.md frontmatter comment fixes** (the `_bmad/skf/` path-standards items)

**~6-10 interactive-gate edits** for cancel-line `[X]` addition

**Cross-cutting smaller edits:** QS `{forger_root}` rename, SS description trigger expansion, TS Conventions trim, defensive workflow-rule drops.

**Estimated PR size:** ~75-90 files changed, +500 to +700 lines, -50 to -100 deletions.

This is similar in scale to PR #306 (31 files, +174 -2) but doubled because we're covering 5 skills instead of 3 and adding the headless-contract surfacing that #306 didn't need to do (Core skills already had them).

---

## Tightening PR — File Inventory (estimate)

- 4 remaining Broken bugs (QS ecosystem-envelope, SS ./references/, AN orphaned refs, AN CCC doc)
- TS reference boilerplate cleanup (~7 files, ~150 line deletions)
- Low-severity sweep across all 5 skills

**Estimated PR size:** ~15-25 files changed, +50 to +150 lines, -150 to -300 deletions (net negative).

- **TS-F**: Trim `## Conventions` block to canonical 5-bullet shape (TS A3)
- **SS-F**: Description trigger phrase expansion (SS A2 — 1 line)

### Tightening PR scope — low/medium-severity remainders + remaining Broken items

| Item | Skill | Type | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **T1** Ecosystem-check terminal branches missing exit code/envelope | QS | broken | Add exit-code mapping + HARD HALT envelope per existing pattern |
| **T2** Cross-dir `./references/{library}.md` link bug | SS | broken | Strip `./`; fix in both compile-stack.md:77 AND assets/stack-skill-template.md:35 |

| **T5** Reference-file boilerplate cleanup (`## Rules`/`## MANDATORY SEQUENCE`/`CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` repeated across 7 step files) | TS | A4 | Drop scaffolding, keep step goal + work + chain line |
| **T6** "Auto-Proceed" subsections duplicate chain instruction | TS | A5 | Collapse into one closing line |

| **T8** Workflow Rules duplicates / no-op directives | QS, AN, AS, TS | A5/A6/A7 | Trim to load-bearing rules only |
| **T9** Batch Mode at SKILL.md scope (~95 lines of single-target-user weight) | QS | A4 | DEFER — substantive carve, see Tracking Issue |
| **T10** Inline JSON schema templates ~80 lines | SS | D4 | Optional asset lift; defer if low ROI |

| **T14** Resume protocol surface (decision-log gesture) | AN, SS, AS | E3 | DEFER substantive DLW; small "resume hint" tweaks OK |
| **T15** Headless arg surface — `--project-path`, `--scope-hint`, `--intent-hint` etc. | AN, AS, TS | E2/E3 | Add documented optional inputs (mostly doc edits) |

### Tracking Issue — Substantive deferrals (no PR)

Per user's strict 2-PR target, these become a tracking issue mirroring #307:

- **TR1** New scripts/ extractions:
  - SS: `scan-manifests.py`, `enumerate-skills.py`, `pair-intersect.py` (D1, D2, D5)

- **TR8** New headless modes: TS `--no-discovery`/`--no-health-check`, AN `--intent-hint`, etc.
- **TR9** `on_complete` hook scalars (TS, AN)

content.content:
1	---
2	name: skf-brief-skill
3	description: Design a skill scope through guided discovery. Use when the user requests to "create a skill brief" or "brief a skill".
4	---
5	
6	# Brief Skill
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	Helps the user define what to skill — target repo, scope, language, inclusion/exclusion patterns — and produces a `skill-brief.yaml` that drives create-skill. This is the first step in the skill creation pipeline; the brief is the input contract for create-skill, which performs the actual compilation.
11	
12	A good skill brief sets a tight, cohesive boundary: one capability with 3-8 primary functions, an unambiguous public API surface, and a description short enough to fit in a registry row. Briefs that try to cover several unrelated concerns (e.g. authentication *and* data visualization) compile into skills that no agent can route to confidently — a brief covering too much is a worse failure mode than a brief covering too little, and this workflow steers toward the smaller, sharper version when scope is unclear.
13	
14	Brief-skill is split from create-skill so the scoping conversation runs *once*, on cheap signals (manifests, top-level exports, intent), without paying for AST extraction. Compilation is expensive; scoping decisions are cheap to revise. Keeping them in separate workflows lets a user iterate on the brief, share it for review, and re-run create-skill against the same brief whenever the upstream version moves.
15	
16	## Conventions
17	
18	- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
19	- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
20	- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
21	- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
22	- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.
23	
24	## Role
25	
26	You are a skill scoping architect collaborating with a developer who wants to create an agent skill. You bring expertise in source code analysis, API surface identification, and skill boundary design, while the user brings their domain knowledge and specific use case. Work together as equals.
27	
28	## Workflow Rules
29	
30	These rules apply to every step in this workflow:
31	
32	- Read each step file completely before taking any action
33	- Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly — do not skip, reorder, or optimize
34	- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
35	- **Lazy-load references and assets:** `references/*.md` and `assets/*.md` files are loaded inside the section that needs them, not at step entry. If a section is skipped (e.g. `version-resolution.md` when `{extractPublicApiScript}` already returned a version, `scope-templates.md` for the `docs-only` branch that bypasses §2c), do not load that file. Each unnecessary load costs context (~5-10 KB per reference) and biases the LLM toward consulting material the current path does not need.
36	- Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the `description`, `notes`, and other free-form fields persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`; per-step rules call this out where it applies (see step 5). The two values may be the same.
37	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
38	
39	## On Activation
40	
41	1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
42	   - `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`
43	
44	2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.
45	
46	3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:
47	
48	   ```bash
49	   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
50	       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
51	   ```
52	
53	   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):
54	
55	   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
56	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
57	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)
58	
59	   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.
60	
61	   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each of the three scalars, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:
62	
63	   - `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` ← `workflow.description_voice_examples_path` if non-empty, else `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
64	   - `{scopeTemplatesPath}` ← `workflow.scope_templates_path` if non-empty, else `assets/scope-templates.md`
65	   - `{briefSchemaPath}` ← `workflow.brief_schema_path` if non-empty, else `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`
66	
67	   Stash all three as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` / `{scopeTemplatesPath}` / `{briefSchemaPath}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies without forking the skill.
68	
69	4. Load, read the full file, and execute `references/gather-intent.md`.
70	
71	## Stages
72	
73	| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
74	|---|------|------|--------------|
75	| 1 | Gather Intent | references/gather-intent.md | No (interactive) |
76	| 2 | Analyze Target | references/analyze-target.md | Yes |
77	| 3 | Scope Definition | references/scope-definition.md | No (interactive) |
78	| 4 | Confirm Brief | references/confirm-brief.md | No (confirm) |
79	| 5 | Write Brief | references/write-brief.md | Yes |
80	| 6 | Workflow Health Check (terminal) | references/health-check.md | Yes |
81	
82	## Invocation Contract
83	
84	| Aspect | Detail |
85	|--------|--------|
86	| **Inputs** | `target_repo` [required], `skill_name` [required], `scope_hint` [optional], `language_hint` [optional], `target_version` [optional], `source_authority` [optional: official/community/internal, default community], `source_type` [optional: source/docs-only, default source], `doc_urls` [optional: list of `url[,label]` for source_type=docs-only or supplemental], `scope_type` [optional: full-library/specific-modules/public-api/component-library/reference-app/docs-only], `include` [optional: comma-separated globs], `exclude` [optional: comma-separated globs], `scripts_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `assets_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `intent` [optional: free-text used to derive description], `force` [optional: overwrite existing brief without prompting] |
87	| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args] | step 3: Confirm Gate [C] | step 4: Confirm Gate [C] |
88	| **Outputs** | `skill-brief.yaml` at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`; final `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` line on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true |
89	| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with heuristic-driven or default action when `{headless_mode}` is true; pre-supplied inputs consumed at the gates that would otherwise prompt; absent `source_authority` and `scope_type` are resolved by signal-driven detection (see `references/headless-args.md`); existing briefs are preserved unless `--force` was supplied (HALT with `overwrite-cancelled` otherwise) |
90	| **Transient-failure retry** | This workflow does **not** auto-retry network or subprocess failures. A failed `gh` fetch (analyze-target.md §1, portfolio-similarity-check.md), QMD probe, or extraction script is logged and surfaced in the final result envelope as a warning, but the workflow continues with whatever signal it has. Headless pipelines that want retry semantics should wrap the invocation at their orchestrator level (e.g. CI re-runner on non-zero exit). Rationale: brief-skill is read-mostly with one terminal write (the YAML at step 5); a partial-signal retry has more failure modes than just re-running the whole workflow, which is cheap. |
91	| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |
92	
93	## Exit Codes
94	
95	Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:
96	
97	| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
98	| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
99	| 0    | success              | step 6 (terminal)                                                                         |
100	| 2    | input-missing / input-invalid | step 1 GATE — required headless arg absent (`target_repo`, `skill_name`, or `doc_urls` when `source_type=docs-only`) → `input-missing`; enum violation, malformed semver, non-kebab `skill_name`, or step 5 brief-context schema validation failure → `input-invalid` |
101	| 3    | resolution-failure   | step 1 §1 (`forge-tier.yaml` missing); step 2 §1 (target inaccessible / `gh auth` fails) |
102	| 4    | write-failure        | step 1 §1 pre-flight write probe (data folder unwritable: read-only mount, disk full, permissions denied); step 5 §4 (write to `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml` failed) |
103	| 5    | overwrite-cancelled  | step 5 §2 (existing brief, `force` not supplied)                                          |
104	| 6    | user-cancelled       | any interactive menu in step 1/03/04 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)                |
105	
106	## Result Contract (Headless)
107	
108	When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 5 emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 6, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:
109	
110	```
111	SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","brief_path":"…|null","skill_name":"…","version":"…|null","language":"…|null","scope_type":"…|null","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null}
112	```
113	
114	`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path, `"error"` on any HALT. `halt_reason` is one of: `null` (success), `"input-missing"`, `"input-invalid"`, `"forge-tier-missing"`, `"target-inaccessible"`, `"gh-auth-failed"`, `"write-failed"`, `"overwrite-cancelled"`, `"user-cancelled"`. `exit_code` matches the table above.
115

---
name: skf-brief-skill
description: Design a skill scope through guided discovery. Use when the user requests to "create a skill brief" or "brief a skill".
---

# Brief Skill

## Overview

Helps the user define what to skill — target repo, scope, language, inclusion/exclusion patterns — and produces a `skill-brief.yaml` that drives create-skill. This is the first step in the skill creation pipeline; the brief is the input contract for create-skill, which performs the actual compilation.

A good skill brief sets a tight, cohesive boundary: one capability with 3-8 primary functions, an unambiguous public API surface, and a description short enough to fit in a registry row. Briefs that try to cover several unrelated concerns (e.g. authentication *and* data visualization) compile into skills that no agent can route to confidently — a brief covering too much is a worse failure mode than a brief covering too little, and this workflow steers toward the smaller, sharper version when scope is unclear.

Brief-skill is split from create-skill so the scoping conversation runs *once*, on cheap signals (manifests, top-level exports, intent), without paying for AST extraction. Compilation is expensive; scoping decisions are cheap to revise. Keeping them in separate workflows lets a user iterate on the brief, share it for review, and re-run create-skill against the same brief whenever the upstream version moves.

## Conventions

- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

## Role

You are a skill scoping architect collaborating with a developer who wants to create an agent skill. You bring expertise in source code analysis, API surface identification, and skill boundary design, while the user brings their domain knowledge and specific use case. Work together as equals.

## Workflow Rules

These rules apply to every step in this workflow:

- Read each step file completely before taking any action
- Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly — do not skip, reorder, or optimize
- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
- **Lazy-load references and assets:** `references/*.md` and `assets/*.md` files are loaded inside the section that needs them, not at step entry. If a section is skipped (e.g. `version-resolution.md` when `{extractPublicApiScript}` already returned a version, `scope-templates.md` for the `docs-only` branch that bypasses §2c), do not load that file. Each unnecessary load costs context (~5-10 KB per reference) and biases the LLM toward consulting material the current path does not need.
- Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the `description`, `notes`, and other free-form fields persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`; per-step rules call this out where it applies (see step 5). The two values may be the same.
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision

## On Activation

1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
   - `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`

2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.

3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:

   ```bash
   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
   ```

   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):

   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)

   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.

   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each of the three scalars, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:

   - `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` ← `workflow.description_voice_examples_path` if non-empty, else `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
   - `{scopeTemplatesPath}` ← `workflow.scope_templates_path` if non-empty, else `assets/scope-templates.md`
   - `{briefSchemaPath}` ← `workflow.brief_schema_path` if non-empty, else `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`

   Stash all three as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` / `{scopeTemplatesPath}` / `{briefSchemaPath}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies without forking the skill.

4. Load, read the full file, and execute `references/gather-intent.md`.

## Stages

| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
|---|------|------|--------------|
| 1 | Gather Intent | references/gather-intent.md | No (interactive) |
| 2 | Analyze Target | references/analyze-target.md | Yes |
| 3 | Scope Definition | references/scope-definition.md | No (interactive) |
| 4 | Confirm Brief | references/confirm-brief.md | No (confirm) |
| 5 | Write Brief | references/write-brief.md | Yes |
| 6 | Workflow Health Check (terminal) | references/health-check.md | Yes |

## Invocation Contract

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| **Inputs** | `target_repo` [required], `skill_name` [required], `scope_hint` [optional], `language_hint` [optional], `target_version` [optional], `source_authority` [optional: official/community/internal, default community], `source_type` [optional: source/docs-only, default source], `doc_urls` [optional: list of `url[,label]` for source_type=docs-only or supplemental], `scope_type` [optional: full-library/specific-modules/public-api/component-library/reference-app/docs-only], `include` [optional: comma-separated globs], `exclude` [optional: comma-separated globs], `scripts_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `assets_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `intent` [optional: free-text used to derive description], `force` [optional: overwrite existing brief without prompting] |
| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args] | step 3: Confirm Gate [C] | step 4: Confirm Gate [C] |
| **Outputs** | `skill-brief.yaml` at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`; final `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` line on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true |
| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with heuristic-driven or default action when `{headless_mode}` is true; pre-supplied inputs consumed at the gates that would otherwise prompt; absent `source_authority` and `scope_type` are resolved by signal-driven detection (see `references/headless-args.md`); existing briefs are preserved unless `--force` was supplied (HALT with `overwrite-cancelled` otherwise) |
| **Transient-failure retry** | This workflow does **not** auto-retry network or subprocess failures. A failed `gh` fetch (analyze-target.md §1, portfolio-similarity-check.md), QMD probe, or extraction script is logged and surfaced in the final result envelope as a warning, but the workflow continues with whatever signal it has. Headless pipelines that want retry semantics should wrap the invocation at their orchestrator level (e.g. CI re-runner on non-zero exit). Rationale: brief-skill is read-mostly with one terminal write (the YAML at step 5); a partial-signal retry has more failure modes than just re-running the whole workflow, which is cheap. |
| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |

## Exit Codes

Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:

| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0    | success              | step 6 (terminal)                                                                         |
| 2    | input-missing / input-invalid | step 1 GATE — required headless arg absent (`target_repo`, `skill_name`, or `doc_urls` when `source_type=docs-only`) → `input-missing`; enum violation, malformed semver, non-kebab `skill_name`, or step 5 brief-context schema validation failure → `input-invalid` |
| 3    | resolution-failure   | step 1 §1 (`forge-tier.yaml` missing); step 2 §1 (target inaccessible / `gh auth` fails) |
| 4    | write-failure        | step 1 §1 pre-flight write probe (data folder unwritable: read-only mount, disk full, permissions denied); step 5 §4 (write to `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml` failed) |
| 5    | overwrite-cancelled  | step 5 §2 (existing brief, `force` not supplied)                                          |
| 6    | user-cancelled       | any interactive menu in step 1/03/04 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)                |

## Result Contract (Headless)

When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 5 emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 6, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:

```
SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","brief_path":"…|null","skill_name":"…","version":"…|null","language":"…|null","scope_type":"…|null","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null}
```

`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path, `"error"` on any HALT. `halt_reason` is one of: `null` (success), `"input-missing"`, `"input-invalid"`, `"forge-tier-missing"`, `"target-inaccessible"`, `"gh-auth-failed"`, `"write-failed"`, `"overwrite-cancelled"`, `"user-cancelled"`. `exit_code` matches the table above.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: skf-quick-skill
3	description: Fast skill from a package name or GitHub URL — no brief needed. Use when the user requests a "quick skill" or "skill from URL" or "skill from package."
4	---
5	
6	# Quick Skill
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	The fastest path to a skill — accept a GitHub URL or package name, resolve to source, extract the public API surface, and produce a best-effort SKILL.md with context snippet and metadata. No brief needed. Quick Skill is tier-unaware by design — all output is produced at community-tier quality regardless of available tools.
11	
12	## Conventions
13	
14	- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
15	- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
16	- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
17	- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
18	- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.
19	
20	## Role
21	
22	You are a rapid skill compiler collaborating with a developer. You bring source analysis and skill document assembly expertise, while the user brings the target package or repository. Work together efficiently — speed is the priority.
23	
24	## Workflow Rules
25	
26	These rules apply to every step in this workflow:
27	
28	- Never fabricate content — all data must come from source extraction or user input
29	- Read each step file completely before taking any action
30	- Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly — do not skip, reorder, or optimize
31	- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
32	- Always communicate in `{communication_language}`
33	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
34	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, emit a single-line JSON progress event to **stderr** at each step's entry and exit so pipeline schedulers can stream live progress instead of post-mortem-parsing the result contract:
35	  - entry: `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"start"}`
36	  - exit (just before chaining to nextStepFile): `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"done"}`
37	  - on HARD HALT: `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"halt","exit":<code>}` instead of "done"
38	
39	  `N` is the step number (1–7) and `<slug>` is the kebab portion of the filename after the number — `resolve-target`, `ecosystem-check`, `quick-extract`, `compile`, `write-and-validate`, `finalize`, `health-check`. One line per event; do not pretty-print.
40	
41	## Stages
42	
43	| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
44	|---|------|------|--------------|
45	| 1 | Resolve Target | references/resolve-target.md | Yes |
46	| 2 | Ecosystem Check | references/ecosystem-check.md | Yes |
47	| 3 | Quick Extract | references/quick-extract.md | Yes |
48	| 4 | Compile | references/compile.md | No (review) |
49	| 5 | Write & Validate | references/write-and-validate.md | Yes |
50	| 6 | Finalize | references/finalize.md | Yes |
51	| 7 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes |
52	
53	## Invocation Contract
54	
55	| Aspect | Detail |
56	|--------|--------|
57	| **Inputs** | target (GitHub URL or package name) [required for single-target mode], language_hint [optional], scope_hint [optional] |
58	| **Overrides** | `--description`, `--exports`, `--skip-snippet`, `--no-active-pointer`, `--batch <file>`, `--fail-fast` — see On Activation step 3 |
59	| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args]; step 2: Choice Gate [P] (if match); step 4: Review Gate [C/E/S/Q] |
60	| **Outputs** | SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, active pointer, result contract (timestamped + `-latest` copy). Snippet and active pointer can be skipped per overrides. |
61	| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |
62	| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |
63	
64	## Exit Codes
65	
66	Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable, documented code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:
67	
68	| Code | Meaning                | Raised by                                                   |
69	| ---- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
70	| 0    | success                | step 7 (terminal)                                          |
71	| 3    | resolution-failure     | step 1 §2c (prose input), step 1 §3 (registry chain failed) |
72	| 4    | write-failure          | step 5 §2 (deliverable write failed)                       |
73	| 5    | overwrite-cancelled    | step 5 §1 (user selected [N])                              |
74	| 6    | compile-cancelled      | step 4 §6 (user selected [Q])                              |
75	| 7    | finalize-blocked       | step 6 §1 (active-pointer flip refused — non-link in place) |
76	
77	Reserved: `validator-missing` may be promoted from advisory log to fatal exit code in a future revision; consumers should not assume code 8+ is unused.
78	
79	## Result Contract on HARD HALT
80	
81	In addition to the success-variant result contract written by step 6 §3, every HARD HALT must surface an **error variant** so headless automators don't silently break when `quick-skill-result-latest.json` is missing on failed runs.
82	
83	**Always (every HARD HALT, regardless of phase)** — emit a single line on **stderr**:
84	
85	```
86	SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","exit_code":<N>,"phase":"<slug>","error":{"code":"<class>","message":"<short>"},"outputs":{},"summary":{},"skill_package":"<path-or-null>"}
87	```
88	
89	One line, no pretty-print. Matches the prefix-and-envelope convention used by `skf-emit-result-envelope.py`.
90	
91	**Additionally, when `{skill_package}` is known** (HALT at step 5 §1 onward) — write the same JSON object (without the `SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: ` prefix) to disk:
92	
93	```
94	{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json
95	{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-latest.json   (copy, not symlink)
96	```
97	
98	so consumers that hardcode the `-latest.json` path see a deterministic file even on failed runs. HALTs at step 1/02/03/04 cannot write to disk because `{skill_package}` is computed only in step 5 §1; for those, the stderr envelope plus exit code is the contract.
99	
100	**Schema:**
101	
102	| Field           | Type           | Notes                                                                                                       |
103	| --------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
104	| `status`        | string         | always `"error"` for HARD HALTs                                                                             |
105	| `exit_code`     | integer        | matches the Exit Codes table                                                                                |
106	| `phase`         | string         | step slug where the HALT occurred (e.g. `resolve-target`, `compile`)                                        |
107	| `error.code`    | string         | one of: `resolution-failure`, `write-failure`, `overwrite-cancelled`, `compile-cancelled`, `finalize-blocked` |
108	| `error.message` | string         | the user-facing message that was displayed                                                                  |
109	| `error.details` | any            | optional — phase-specific context (e.g. the failed file path)                                               |
110	| `outputs`       | object         | empty `{}` on early HALTs; partial when files were already written                                          |
111	| `summary`       | object         | empty `{}` on early HALTs                                                                                   |
112	| `skill_package` | string \| null | absolute path when known, `null` when HALT preceded step 5 §1                                              |
113	
114	## On Activation
115	
116	1. Read `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and `{forger_root}/preferences.yaml` in parallel (one batched tool-call message — they are independent files), then resolve:
117	   - From config: `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`
118	   - From preferences: `headless_mode` (default false)
119	
120	2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in `preferences.yaml`. Default: false.
121	
122	3. **Parse CLI overrides** — capture optional override flags into the workflow context as `{overrides}`. Each override is opt-in; when omitted, the workflow runs as today.
123	
124	   | Flag | Effect |
125	   | --- | --- |
126	   | `--description "<string>"` | Override the LLM-derived description in step 4 §2 (used in SKILL.md frontmatter and metadata.json). Subject to the same agentskills.io length (1–1024 chars) and voice (third-person) checks as extracted descriptions. |
127	   | `--exports "<name1,name2,...>"` | Override the extracted export list. Parse as comma-separated; trim whitespace per item; skip empty items. Used in step 4 §2 Key Exports and the count-derived metadata stats. |
128	   | `--skip-snippet` | Skip context-snippet.md generation in step 4 §3 and its write in step 5 §2. Artifact omitted from `outputs`; step 5 §5 advisory snippet validation reports a "skipped" entry. |
129	   | `--no-active-pointer` | Skip the active-pointer flip in step 6 §1. Deliverables still land in `{skill_package}` but `{skill_group}/active` is not updated. Useful for batch automators that flip pointers in a separate stage. |
130	   | `--batch <file>` | Run the workflow against a list of targets from a text file rather than a single argument. Implies `--headless` (gates cannot be human-driven across N targets). See "Batch Mode" below for input format and summary contract. Single-target overrides above apply globally to every target in the batch. |
131	   | `--fail-fast` | Only meaningful with `--batch`. Abort the whole batch on the first per-target failure instead of recording the failure in the summary and proceeding to the next target. |
132	
133	4. **If `--batch` is set**, force `{headless_mode} = true` (log "headless: coerced by --batch" if it was false), read the batch file, and parse the target list per "Batch Mode" below. Continue at step 5; the batch loop documented in "Batch Mode" wraps the step 1 → step 7 pipeline that follows.
134	
135	5. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/resolve-target.md` to begin the workflow. (In batch mode, control returns here for each subsequent target after step 7 completes; see "Batch Mode" below.)
136	
137	## Batch Mode
138	
139	When `--batch <file>` is supplied, quick-skill processes a list of targets from a text file in sequence rather than a single target from arguments. Designed for unattended bulk runs (CI pipelines, mass-rebuilds, the skf-batch-skills meta-workflow when it lands).
140	
141	### Input format
142	
143	One target per line. Empty lines and lines starting with `#` (after optional leading whitespace) are ignored. Each non-empty line has the same shape as the single-target `target` argument, with optional space-separated per-line modifiers:
144	
145	```
146	# A batch input file.
147	lodash
148	@vercel/og
149	cognee@0.5.0
150	https://github.com/foo/bar
151	https://github.com/foo/bar@2.1.0-beta
152	
153	# Per-line modifiers — overrides for THIS target only:
154	lodash language=javascript scope=src/
155	cognee@0.5.0 language=python scope=cognee/api/
156	```
157	
158	Recognised per-line modifiers:
159	
160	| Modifier | Effect (this target only) |
161	| --- | --- |
162	| `language=<lang>` | Sets `language_hint` for this target — same effect as the optional `language_hint` input on a single-target run. |
163	| `scope=<path>` | Sets `scope_hint` for this target — same effect as the optional `scope_hint` input on a single-target run. |
164	
165	Per-line modifiers shadow the global `--description` / `--exports` / `--skip-snippet` / `--no-active-pointer` overrides only when those override fields are not set. Global overrides apply to every target unless a future modifier extends per-line override syntax.
166	
167	### Execution
168	
169	`--batch` implies `--headless`. The batch loop runs the full quick-skill pipeline (steps 1–7) for each target in file order:
170	
171	1. Set `target`, `target_version`, `language_hint`, `scope_hint` from the batch line into the workflow context.
172	2. Execute steps 1–7 per the normal pipeline.
173	3. After step 7 completes (success or HARD HALT), record the per-target outcome (target, status, exit_code, skill_package, error.code) into the batch result list.
174	4. If `--fail-fast` is set and the target failed, exit the batch loop immediately. Otherwise continue with the next target.
175	
176	Per-target output lands in `{skill_package}/` as today, with the per-target result contract at `{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-latest.json` (success or error variant per "Result Contract on HARD HALT" above).
177	
178	### Batch summary contract
179	
180	After the last target completes (or `--fail-fast` triggers an early exit), write the batch summary at:
181	
182	```
183	{skills_output_folder}/_batch/quick-skill-batch-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json
184	{skills_output_folder}/_batch/quick-skill-batch-latest.json   (copy, not symlink)
185	```
186	
187	Schema:
188	
189	```json
190	{
191	  "skill": "skf-quick-skill",
192	  "mode": "batch",
193	  "status": "success | partial | failed",
194	  "timestamp": "<ISO 8601 UTC>",
195	  "input_file": "<path passed to --batch>",
196	  "targets_total": 0,
197	  "succeeded": 0,
198	  "failed": 0,
199	  "fail_fast_triggered": false,
200	  "results": [
201	    {
202	      "target": "<line from batch file>",
203	      "status": "success | error",
204	      "exit_code": 0,
205	      "skill_package": "<absolute path or null>",
206	      "error_code": null
207	    }
208	  ]
209	}
210	```
211	
212	`status` resolves as: `"success"` when `failed == 0`; `"partial"` when `failed > 0 && succeeded > 0`; `"failed"` when `succeeded == 0`. `fail_fast_triggered` is `true` only when `--fail-fast` aborted the loop early — `targets_total` then reflects the count actually attempted, not the file's line count.
213	
214	### Headless events
215	
216	Batch mode emits per-target boundary events on stderr in addition to the per-step events documented in Workflow Rules:
217	
218	```
219	{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"start"}
220	{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"done","exit":<code>}
221	{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"fail","exit":<code>,"error_code":"<class>"}
222	```
223	
224	`<n>` is the 1-based index of the target in the parsed list. After the loop ends, emit one final batch-summary event:
225	
226	```
227	{"batch_summary":true,"targets_total":N,"succeeded":K,"failed":M,"status":"<...>","fail_fast_triggered":<bool>}
228	```
229	
230	### Exit code
231	
232	The batch process exits with code `0` when `failed == 0`, otherwise with the exit code of the first failed target (so automators that already branch on the single-target exit-code map continue to work without batch-specific handling). When `--fail-fast` triggers, the exit code is the failing target's code.
233

---
name: skf-quick-skill
description: Fast skill from a package name or GitHub URL — no brief needed. Use when the user requests a "quick skill" or "skill from URL" or "skill from package."
---

# Quick Skill

## Overview

The fastest path to a skill — accept a GitHub URL or package name, resolve to source, extract the public API surface, and produce a best-effort SKILL.md with context snippet and metadata. No brief needed. Quick Skill is tier-unaware by design — all output is produced at community-tier quality regardless of available tools.

## Conventions

- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

## Role

You are a rapid skill compiler collaborating with a developer. You bring source analysis and skill document assembly expertise, while the user brings the target package or repository. Work together efficiently — speed is the priority.

## Workflow Rules

These rules apply to every step in this workflow:

- Never fabricate content — all data must come from source extraction or user input
- Read each step file completely before taking any action
- Follow the mandatory sequence in each step exactly — do not skip, reorder, or optimize
- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
- Always communicate in `{communication_language}`
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, emit a single-line JSON progress event to **stderr** at each step's entry and exit so pipeline schedulers can stream live progress instead of post-mortem-parsing the result contract:
  - entry: `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"start"}`
  - exit (just before chaining to nextStepFile): `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"done"}`
  - on HARD HALT: `{"step":N,"name":"<slug>","status":"halt","exit":<code>}` instead of "done"

  `N` is the step number (1–7) and `<slug>` is the kebab portion of the filename after the number — `resolve-target`, `ecosystem-check`, `quick-extract`, `compile`, `write-and-validate`, `finalize`, `health-check`. One line per event; do not pretty-print.

## Stages

| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
|---|------|------|--------------|
| 1 | Resolve Target | references/resolve-target.md | Yes |
| 2 | Ecosystem Check | references/ecosystem-check.md | Yes |
| 3 | Quick Extract | references/quick-extract.md | Yes |
| 4 | Compile | references/compile.md | No (review) |
| 5 | Write & Validate | references/write-and-validate.md | Yes |
| 6 | Finalize | references/finalize.md | Yes |
| 7 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes |

## Invocation Contract

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| **Inputs** | target (GitHub URL or package name) [required for single-target mode], language_hint [optional], scope_hint [optional] |
| **Overrides** | `--description`, `--exports`, `--skip-snippet`, `--no-active-pointer`, `--batch <file>`, `--fail-fast` — see On Activation step 3 |
| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args]; step 2: Choice Gate [P] (if match); step 4: Review Gate [C/E/S/Q] |
| **Outputs** | SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, active pointer, result contract (timestamped + `-latest` copy). Snippet and active pointer can be skipped per overrides. |
| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |
| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |

## Exit Codes

Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable, documented code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:

| Code | Meaning                | Raised by                                                   |
| ---- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0    | success                | step 7 (terminal)                                          |
| 3    | resolution-failure     | step 1 §2c (prose input), step 1 §3 (registry chain failed) |
| 4    | write-failure          | step 5 §2 (deliverable write failed)                       |
| 5    | overwrite-cancelled    | step 5 §1 (user selected [N])                              |
| 6    | compile-cancelled      | step 4 §6 (user selected [Q])                              |
| 7    | finalize-blocked       | step 6 §1 (active-pointer flip refused — non-link in place) |

Reserved: `validator-missing` may be promoted from advisory log to fatal exit code in a future revision; consumers should not assume code 8+ is unused.

## Result Contract on HARD HALT

In addition to the success-variant result contract written by step 6 §3, every HARD HALT must surface an **error variant** so headless automators don't silently break when `quick-skill-result-latest.json` is missing on failed runs.

**Always (every HARD HALT, regardless of phase)** — emit a single line on **stderr**:

```
SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","exit_code":<N>,"phase":"<slug>","error":{"code":"<class>","message":"<short>"},"outputs":{},"summary":{},"skill_package":"<path-or-null>"}
```

One line, no pretty-print. Matches the prefix-and-envelope convention used by `skf-emit-result-envelope.py`.

**Additionally, when `{skill_package}` is known** (HALT at step 5 §1 onward) — write the same JSON object (without the `SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: ` prefix) to disk:

```
{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json
{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-latest.json   (copy, not symlink)
```

so consumers that hardcode the `-latest.json` path see a deterministic file even on failed runs. HALTs at step 1/02/03/04 cannot write to disk because `{skill_package}` is computed only in step 5 §1; for those, the stderr envelope plus exit code is the contract.

**Schema:**

| Field           | Type           | Notes                                                                                                       |
| --------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `status`        | string         | always `"error"` for HARD HALTs                                                                             |
| `exit_code`     | integer        | matches the Exit Codes table                                                                                |
| `phase`         | string         | step slug where the HALT occurred (e.g. `resolve-target`, `compile`)                                        |
| `error.code`    | string         | one of: `resolution-failure`, `write-failure`, `overwrite-cancelled`, `compile-cancelled`, `finalize-blocked` |
| `error.message` | string         | the user-facing message that was displayed                                                                  |
| `error.details` | any            | optional — phase-specific context (e.g. the failed file path)                                               |
| `outputs`       | object         | empty `{}` on early HALTs; partial when files were already written                                          |
| `summary`       | object         | empty `{}` on early HALTs                                                                                   |
| `skill_package` | string \| null | absolute path when known, `null` when HALT preceded step 5 §1                                              |

## On Activation

1. Read `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and `{forger_root}/preferences.yaml` in parallel (one batched tool-call message — they are independent files), then resolve:
   - From config: `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`
   - From preferences: `headless_mode` (default false)

2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in `preferences.yaml`. Default: false.

3. **Parse CLI overrides** — capture optional override flags into the workflow context as `{overrides}`. Each override is opt-in; when omitted, the workflow runs as today.

   | Flag | Effect |
   | --- | --- |
   | `--description "<string>"` | Override the LLM-derived description in step 4 §2 (used in SKILL.md frontmatter and metadata.json). Subject to the same agentskills.io length (1–1024 chars) and voice (third-person) checks as extracted descriptions. |
   | `--exports "<name1,name2,...>"` | Override the extracted export list. Parse as comma-separated; trim whitespace per item; skip empty items. Used in step 4 §2 Key Exports and the count-derived metadata stats. |
   | `--skip-snippet` | Skip context-snippet.md generation in step 4 §3 and its write in step 5 §2. Artifact omitted from `outputs`; step 5 §5 advisory snippet validation reports a "skipped" entry. |
   | `--no-active-pointer` | Skip the active-pointer flip in step 6 §1. Deliverables still land in `{skill_package}` but `{skill_group}/active` is not updated. Useful for batch automators that flip pointers in a separate stage. |
   | `--batch <file>` | Run the workflow against a list of targets from a text file rather than a single argument. Implies `--headless` (gates cannot be human-driven across N targets). See "Batch Mode" below for input format and summary contract. Single-target overrides above apply globally to every target in the batch. |
   | `--fail-fast` | Only meaningful with `--batch`. Abort the whole batch on the first per-target failure instead of recording the failure in the summary and proceeding to the next target. |

4. **If `--batch` is set**, force `{headless_mode} = true` (log "headless: coerced by --batch" if it was false), read the batch file, and parse the target list per "Batch Mode" below. Continue at step 5; the batch loop documented in "Batch Mode" wraps the step 1 → step 7 pipeline that follows.

5. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/resolve-target.md` to begin the workflow. (In batch mode, control returns here for each subsequent target after step 7 completes; see "Batch Mode" below.)

## Batch Mode

When `--batch <file>` is supplied, quick-skill processes a list of targets from a text file in sequence rather than a single target from arguments. Designed for unattended bulk runs (CI pipelines, mass-rebuilds, the skf-batch-skills meta-workflow when it lands).

### Input format

One target per line. Empty lines and lines starting with `#` (after optional leading whitespace) are ignored. Each non-empty line has the same shape as the single-target `target` argument, with optional space-separated per-line modifiers:

```
# A batch input file.
lodash
@vercel/og
cognee@0.5.0
https://github.com/foo/bar
https://github.com/foo/bar@2.1.0-beta

# Per-line modifiers — overrides for THIS target only:
lodash language=javascript scope=src/
cognee@0.5.0 language=python scope=cognee/api/
```

Recognised per-line modifiers:

| Modifier | Effect (this target only) |
| --- | --- |
| `language=<lang>` | Sets `language_hint` for this target — same effect as the optional `language_hint` input on a single-target run. |
| `scope=<path>` | Sets `scope_hint` for this target — same effect as the optional `scope_hint` input on a single-target run. |

Per-line modifiers shadow the global `--description` / `--exports` / `--skip-snippet` / `--no-active-pointer` overrides only when those override fields are not set. Global overrides apply to every target unless a future modifier extends per-line override syntax.

### Execution

`--batch` implies `--headless`. The batch loop runs the full quick-skill pipeline (steps 1–7) for each target in file order:

1. Set `target`, `target_version`, `language_hint`, `scope_hint` from the batch line into the workflow context.
2. Execute steps 1–7 per the normal pipeline.
3. After step 7 completes (success or HARD HALT), record the per-target outcome (target, status, exit_code, skill_package, error.code) into the batch result list.
4. If `--fail-fast` is set and the target failed, exit the batch loop immediately. Otherwise continue with the next target.

Per-target output lands in `{skill_package}/` as today, with the per-target result contract at `{skill_package}/quick-skill-result-latest.json` (success or error variant per "Result Contract on HARD HALT" above).

### Batch summary contract

After the last target completes (or `--fail-fast` triggers an early exit), write the batch summary at:

```
{skills_output_folder}/_batch/quick-skill-batch-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json
{skills_output_folder}/_batch/quick-skill-batch-latest.json   (copy, not symlink)
```

Schema:

```json
{
  "skill": "skf-quick-skill",
  "mode": "batch",
  "status": "success | partial | failed",
  "timestamp": "<ISO 8601 UTC>",
  "input_file": "<path passed to --batch>",
  "targets_total": 0,
  "succeeded": 0,
  "failed": 0,
  "fail_fast_triggered": false,
  "results": [
    {
      "target": "<line from batch file>",
      "status": "success | error",
      "exit_code": 0,
      "skill_package": "<absolute path or null>",
      "error_code": null
    }
  ]
}
```

`status` resolves as: `"success"` when `failed == 0`; `"partial"` when `failed > 0 && succeeded > 0`; `"failed"` when `succeeded == 0`. `fail_fast_triggered` is `true` only when `--fail-fast` aborted the loop early — `targets_total` then reflects the count actually attempted, not the file's line count.

### Headless events

Batch mode emits per-target boundary events on stderr in addition to the per-step events documented in Workflow Rules:

```
{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"start"}
{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"done","exit":<code>}
{"batch":<n>,"target":"<target>","status":"fail","exit":<code>,"error_code":"<class>"}
```

`<n>` is the 1-based index of the target in the parsed list. After the loop ends, emit one final batch-summary event:

```
{"batch_summary":true,"targets_total":N,"succeeded":K,"failed":M,"status":"<...>","fail_fast_triggered":<bool>}
```

### Exit code

The batch process exits with code `0` when `failed == 0`, otherwise with the exit code of the first failed target (so automators that already branch on the single-target exit-code map continue to work without batch-specific handling). When `--fail-fast` triggers, the exit code is the failing target's code.

#     "file:{project-root}/docs/test-policy.md" (globs supported; file
#     contents are loaded and treated as facts).

persistent_facts = [
  "file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md",
]

# --- Optional asset overrides ---
#
# Lift the canonical asset paths so orgs can substitute house-style copies
# without forking the skill. Empty string = use the bundled default.

test_report_template_path = ""
output_formats_path = ""
scoring_rules_path = ""

content.subject: Run deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills
content.description: Run scan-path-standards.py, scan-scripts.py, prepass-workflow-integrity.py, prepass-prompt-metrics.py, prepass-execution-deps.py against QS, SS, AN, AS, TS in parallel. Output JSON goes to each skill's .analysis/<ts>/ directory.

content.subject: Synthesize cross-skill findings table

content.subject: Land Tightening PR (sweep low-severity items)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #323 pattern: every low-severity actionable finding swept in one commit. Confirms all 5 Feature skills reach Excellent. Body includes the per-skill before/after grade table.

content.subject: Open tracking issue for deferred substantive items
content.description: Mirrors #307. Single tracking issue listing any substantive items (new shared scripts, new headless modes, structural carves) the scans surface but we deliberately deferred. Each item gets a code.
