<summary>Agent "Foundation edits for skf-quick-skill" completed</summary>
<result>The `_bmad/skf/` already has `{project-root}/` prefix — no fix needed.

All edits complete. Summary report:

**Files touched:**

1. **`src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md`**
   - **Task 1 (forger_root typo)**: Replaced `{forger_root}` with `{sidecar_path}` in On Activation step 1; added `sidecar_path` to the resolved-fields list.
   - **Task 2 (customization resolver)**: Inserted new step 3 "Resolve workflow customization" (calls `resolve_customization.py --skill {skill-root} --key workflow`), with path-scalar fallback for `{skillTemplatePath}` → `assets/skill-template.md`, `{registryResolutionPath}` → `references/registry-resolution.md`, `{batchOutputPath}` → `{skills_output_folder}/_batch/`. Renumbered subsequent steps (4: parse CLI overrides; 5: batch handling; 6: execute resolve-target.md); updated Invocation Contract row "see On Activation step 3" → "step 4"; updated step-5's "continue at step 5" → "continue at step 6".
   - **Task 3 (drop redundant Workflow Rules)**: Removed three LLM-native default rules ("Read each step file completely…", "Follow the mandatory sequence…", "Only load one step file at a time…"). Kept "Never fabricate content", `{communication_language}`, and the two headless-mode bullets.
   - **Task 5 (cancel-line affordance)**: Added a new Workflow Rules bullet documenting the universal `cancel` / `exit` / `:q` / `[X]` affordance wired to exit code 6 (user-cancelled) with envelope contract; relabeled Exit Codes row 6 from `compile-cancelled` → `user-cancelled` (citing step 1 §1 and step 4 §6); updated the `error.code` enum row to list `user-cancelled (formerly compile-cancelled)`.

2. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/resolve-target.md`**
   - **Task 4**: Replaced body-line `Communicate with the user in {communication_language}.` with `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->` HTML comment after the frontmatter close.
   - **Task 5**: Added `[X]` / `cancel` / `exit` / `:q` affordance to the §1 prompt and a new "Cancel branch" handler that HARD HALTs with exit code 6, `error.code: "user-cancelled"`, `phase: "resolve-target"`.
   - **Renumbering**: Updated "On Activation step 4" reference (batch mode handler) to "step 5".

3. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/compile.md`**
   - **Task 4**: Replaced body-line directive with `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. Generated SKILL.md text in {document_output_language}. -->`.
   - **Task 5**: Updated [Q] handler to use exit-code label `user-cancelled` and `error.code: "user-cancelled"` (was `compile-cancelled`).

4. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/ecosystem-check.md`** — Task 4 only (HTML comment, no document_output_language).
5. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/quick-extract.md`** — Task 4 only (HTML comment, no document_output_language).
6. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/write-and-validate.md`** — Task 4 only (HTML comment + document_output_language).
7. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/finalize.md`** — Task 4 only (HTML comment + document_output_language).
8. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/health-check.md`** — Task 4 only (HTML comment, no document_output_language).
9. **`src/skf-quick-skill/references/registry-resolution.md`** — Task 4 only; file had no existing directive and no frontmatter, so inserted `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->` at the top before the H1.

**Skipped:**
- **Task 6**: health-check.md frontmatter already has `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/` correctly prefixed — no bare `_bmad/skf/` present. No edit needed.
- **customize.toml**: not touched (already done per task scope).</result>

content.subject: Run deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills
content.description: Run scan-path-standards.py, scan-scripts.py, prepass-workflow-integrity.py, prepass-prompt-metrics.py, prepass-execution-deps.py against QS, SS, AN, AS, TS in parallel. Output JSON goes to each skill's .analysis/<ts>/ directory.
content.activeForm: Running deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills

content.subject: Parallel LLM scan: 5 agents over QS/SS/AN/AS/TS
content.description: Spawn 5 parallel general-purpose agents, one per Feature skill. Each loads the 4 scanner reference files (architecture, determinism, customization, enhancement) + skill-quality-principles.md + its skill's deterministic script output, then writes one unified coded findings report.

content.subject: Synthesize cross-skill findings table
content.description: Read all 5 findings reports. Group by finding TYPE (customize.toml parity, language headers, headless contract, UX polish, shared scripts, broken items) — not by skill. Produce single ranked table with coded items (Q1, S1, A1, AS1, T1...) to drive the Foundation + Tightening PR scopes.

content.subject: Land Foundation PR (mechanical parity across all 5 skills)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #306 pattern: customize.toml parity, language-config headers on stage files, B6-style cancel-line/UX polish, cross-skill coupling docs if applicable. All 5 skills touched in one branch/PR.

content.subject: Land Tightening PR (sweep low-severity items)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #323 pattern: every low-severity actionable finding swept in one commit. Confirms all 5 Feature skills reach Excellent. Body includes the per-skill before/after grade table.

content.subject: Open tracking issue for deferred substantive items
content.description: Mirrors #307. Single tracking issue listing any substantive items (new shared scripts, new headless modes, structural carves) the scans surface but we deliberately deferred. Each item gets a code.

content.subject: Run deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills
content.description: Run scan-path-standards.py, scan-scripts.py, prepass-workflow-integrity.py, prepass-prompt-metrics.py, prepass-execution-deps.py against QS, SS, AN, AS, TS in parallel. Output JSON goes to each skill's .analysis/<ts>/ directory.
content.activeForm: Running deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills

content.subject: Parallel LLM scan: 5 agents over QS/SS/AN/AS/TS
content.description: Spawn 5 parallel general-purpose agents, one per Feature skill. Each loads the 4 scanner reference files (architecture, determinism, customization, enhancement) + skill-quality-principles.md + its skill's deterministic script output, then writes one unified coded findings report.

content.subject: Synthesize cross-skill findings table
content.description: Read all 5 findings reports. Group by finding TYPE (customize.toml parity, language headers, headless contract, UX polish, shared scripts, broken items) — not by skill. Produce single ranked table with coded items (Q1, S1, A1, AS1, T1...) to drive the Foundation + Tightening PR scopes.

content.subject: Land Foundation PR (mechanical parity across all 5 skills)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #306 pattern: customize.toml parity, language-config headers on stage files, B6-style cancel-line/UX polish, cross-skill coupling docs if applicable. All 5 skills touched in one branch/PR.

content.subject: Land Tightening PR (sweep low-severity items)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #323 pattern: every low-severity actionable finding swept in one commit. Confirms all 5 Feature skills reach Excellent. Body includes the per-skill before/after grade table.

content.subject: Open tracking issue for deferred substantive items
content.description: Mirrors #307. Single tracking issue listing any substantive items (new shared scripts, new headless modes, structural carves) the scans surface but we deliberately deferred. Each item gets a code.

When the flag is set, rebuild `catalog_size` from the broader pool before the precondition check.

90	After gap enumeration, perform minimum-viable discovery testing. This is a **Medium-weight** check contributing to the Discovery Quality subsection — no longer advisory boilerplate.
91	
92	**4b.0 Precondition — catalog size check:**
93	
94	Count the skills in `{skillsOutputFolder}`: `ls -1d {skillsOutputFolder}/*/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l` (directories only — each skill package lives at `{skillsOutputFolder}/<skill-name>/`).
95	
96	- If `catalog_size < 2`: **skip §4b.1–§4b.3**. Record an Info-severity note in the Discovery Quality subsection: `discovery — skipped: catalog size N={catalog_size}, requires ≥2 candidates for meaningful routing`. The routing test is vacuous with one candidate (any prompt returns the sole skill); reporting `3/3 PASS` under those conditions inflates the Discovery score and masks genuinely bad description triggers. Proceed to §4b.4 (description optimization) if tessl/skill-check flagged description issues; otherwise skip to §4c.
97	- If `catalog_size >= 2`: continue with §4b.1 as written.
98	
99	Optional escape hatch: the workflow accepts `--discovery-catalog=all` to broaden the candidate pool to `{project-root}/.claude/skills/` or `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/` for single-skill repos where the repo-local catalog is trivially too small. When the flag is set, rebuild `catalog_size` from the broader pool before the precondition check.
100	
101	**4b.1 Extract realistic prompts from the skill under test:**
102	
103	Parse SKILL.md for the three most "organic" prompts found in its `description`, `Triggers`, or example sections. Prefer prompts that:
104	- Use conversational phrasing (contractions, casual language, implicit context)
105	- Omit the skill name or explicit command invocation
106	- Reflect how a user would actually ask for this capability
107	
108	If SKILL.md does not contain enough organic examples, synthesize 3 from the skill's exports/capability summary using the patterns from §4b.4 below.

If no gaps found, append a clean pass message recommending **export-skill** workflow.

### 4b. Discovery Testing (MANDATORY — H4)

After gap enumeration, perform minimum-viable discovery testing. This is a **Medium-weight** check contributing to the Discovery Quality subsection — no longer advisory boilerplate.

**4b.0 Precondition — catalog size check:**

Count the skills in `{skillsOutputFolder}`: `ls -1d {skillsOutputFolder}/*/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l` (directories only — each skill package lives at `{skillsOutputFolder}/<skill-name>/`).

- If `catalog_size < 2`: **skip §4b.1–§4b.3**. Record an Info-severity note in the Discovery Quality subsection: `discovery — skipped: catalog size N={catalog_size}, requires ≥2 candidates for meaningful routing`. The routing test is vacuous with one candidate (any prompt returns the sole skill); reporting `3/3 PASS` under those conditions inflates the Discovery score and masks genuinely bad description triggers. Proceed to §4b.4 (description optimization) if tessl/skill-check flagged description issues; otherwise skip to §4c.
- If `catalog_size >= 2`: continue with §4b.1 as written.

Optional escape hatch: the workflow accepts `--discovery-catalog=all` to broaden the candidate pool to `{project-root}/.claude/skills/` or `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/` for single-skill repos where the repo-local catalog is trivially too small. When the flag is set, rebuild `catalog_size` from the broader pool before the precondition check.

**4b.1 Extract realistic prompts from the skill under test:**

Parse SKILL.md for the three most "organic" prompts found in its `description`, `Triggers`, or example sections. Prefer prompts that:
- Use conversational phrasing (contractions, casual language, implicit context)
- Omit the skill name or explicit command invocation
- Reflect how a user would actually ask for this capability

If SKILL.md does not contain enough organic examples, synthesize 3 from the skill's exports/capability summary using the patterns from §4b.4 below.

content.subject: Parallel LLM scan: 5 agents over QS/SS/AN/AS/TS
content.description: Spawn 5 parallel general-purpose agents, one per Feature skill. Each loads the 4 scanner reference files (architecture, determinism, customization, enhancement) + skill-quality-principles.md + its skill's deterministic script output, then writes one unified coded findings report.

content.subject: Synthesize cross-skill findings table
content.description: Read all 5 findings reports. Group by finding TYPE (customize.toml parity, language headers, headless contract, UX polish, shared scripts, broken items) — not by skill. Produce single ranked table with coded items (Q1, S1, A1, AS1, T1...) to drive the Foundation + Tightening PR scopes.

content.subject: Land Foundation PR (mechanical parity across all 5 skills)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #306 pattern: customize.toml parity, language-config headers on stage files, B6-style cancel-line/UX polish, cross-skill coupling docs if applicable. All 5 skills touched in one branch/PR.

content.subject: Land Tightening PR (sweep low-severity items)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #323 pattern: every low-severity actionable finding swept in one commit. Confirms all 5 Feature skills reach Excellent. Body includes the per-skill before/after grade table.

content.subject: Open tracking issue for deferred substantive items
content.description: Mirrors #307. Single tracking issue listing any substantive items (new shared scripts, new headless modes, structural carves) the scans surface but we deliberately deferred. Each item gets a code.

content.subject: Run deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills
content.description: Run scan-path-standards.py, scan-scripts.py, prepass-workflow-integrity.py, prepass-prompt-metrics.py, prepass-execution-deps.py against QS, SS, AN, AS, TS in parallel. Output JSON goes to each skill's .analysis/<ts>/ directory.
content.activeForm: Running deterministic scans for all 5 Feature skills

content.subject: Parallel LLM scan: 5 agents over QS/SS/AN/AS/TS
content.description: Spawn 5 parallel general-purpose agents, one per Feature skill. Each loads the 4 scanner reference files (architecture, determinism, customization, enhancement) + skill-quality-principles.md + its skill's deterministic script output, then writes one unified coded findings report.

content.subject: Synthesize cross-skill findings table
content.description: Read all 5 findings reports. Group by finding TYPE (customize.toml parity, language headers, headless contract, UX polish, shared scripts, broken items) — not by skill. Produce single ranked table with coded items (Q1, S1, A1, AS1, T1...) to drive the Foundation + Tightening PR scopes.

content.subject: Land Foundation PR (mechanical parity across all 5 skills)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #306 pattern: customize.toml parity, language-config headers on stage files, B6-style cancel-line/UX polish, cross-skill coupling docs if applicable. All 5 skills touched in one branch/PR.

content.subject: Land Tightening PR (sweep low-severity items)
content.description: Single PR mirroring #323 pattern: every low-severity actionable finding swept in one commit. Confirms all 5 Feature skills reach Excellent. Body includes the per-skill before/after grade table.

content.subject: Open tracking issue for deferred substantive items
content.description: Mirrors #307. Single tracking issue listing any substantive items (new shared scripts, new headless modes, structural carves) the scans surface but we deliberately deferred. Each item gets a code.

content.text: All gates green. Verifying customize.toml files weren't touched, then committing.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(skf-feature): elevate 5 Feature workflows toward Excellent (foundation pass)

Brings skf-quick-skill, skf-create-stack-skill, skf-analyze-source,
skf-audit-skill, and skf-test-skill to parity with the Core-elevated
peers from PRs #305-#323.

Customization surface (S1 across all 5):
- customize.toml shipped per skill: [workflow] block with three
  append-merge arrays (activation_steps_prepend/append, persistent_facts)
  plus 2-4 asset-path scalars per skill (e.g. skill_template_path,
  drift_report_template_path, scoring_rules_path).
- On Activation gains a `python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py`
  step computing path-scalar fallback so stage files reference
  `{<purpose>Path}` directly with no conditional at the call site.

Headless contract (E1/E2 across the 4 skills that lacked it):
- `## Exit Codes` table added/lifted to SKILL.md for create-stack,
  analyze-source, audit-skill, test-skill (mirrors brief-skill).
- `## Result Contract (Headless)` block added with single-line
  envelope prefixes: `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON`, `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`,
  `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON`, `SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON`. Mirror-on-stderr
  for HARD HALTs.

Language headers (path-standards C1 across 50+ reference files):
- Each `references/*.md` gains `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->`
  after frontmatter close. Artifact-producing steps get the
  `Artifact text in {document_output_language}.` variant.

UX (B6 cancel-line across QS/SS/AN):
- `[X] Cancel and exit` added to interactive gates in resolve-target.md
  (QS), rank-and-confirm.md + compile-stack.md (SS), and scan-project.md
  + identify-units.md + recommend.md (AN), wired to exit code 6.

Bug fixes folded in (Broken items, all 1-line):
- skf-quick-skill SKILL.md: `{forger_root}` was an undefined variable
  (no module declares it), silently breaking headless detection via
  preferences.yaml. Replaced with `{sidecar_path}`.
- Bare `_bmad/skf/` references in health-check.md frontmatter
  comments now correctly prefixed with `{project-root}/`.
- test-skill references/report.md: bare `_bmad/agents/` and
  `.claude/skills/` in --discovery-catalog=all prose now prefixed.

Workflow Rules tightened (A5/A6/A7 across 4 skills):
- Dropped defensive "main-thread fallback" bullets covered by per-step
  fallback wording.
- Dropped LLM-native procedural rules ("read each step file completely",
  "follow the mandatory sequence exactly") from QS Workflow Rules.

Skill-specific polish:
- skf-create-stack-skill: description trigger expanded to 3 quoted
  phrases; "Ferris Architect mode" persona clause dropped; Invocation
  Contract gains Exit codes row.
- skf-audit-skill: Invocation Contract Inputs row enumerates the
  optional inputs the workflow actually consumes (skill_path,
  tier_override, degraded, upstream_drift_choice, dirty_worktree_choice,
  force) rather than just `skill_name`.

Validator updates (directly motivated by surfaces added in this PR):
- `tools/validate-file-refs.js`: skip `.analysis/` analysis dirs (which
  are gitignored runtime state); add `agents/` to INSTALL_ONLY_PATHS
  so the test-skill `--discovery-catalog=all` reference to
  `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/` resolves cleanly.

Substantive items deliberately deferred to a tracking issue:
- New shared scripts (scan-manifests.py, pair-intersect.py,
  compare-file-hashes.py, scan-skill-md-structure.py, etc.)
- compute-score.py unit tests + argparse migration
- Reference-file boilerplate cleanup in test-skill
- Decision-Log Workspace resume protocols for stack/audit
- New optional headless modes (--no-discovery, --no-health-check, --tier=)
- Carving Batch Mode out of skf-quick-skill SKILL.md

Test plan:
- node tools/validate-skills.js — 0 findings (15 skills scanned)
- node tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict — 0 broken, 0 leaks (189 files / 124 refs)
- node test/test-installation-components.js — 179 passed
- node test/test-workflow-state.js — 38 passed
- node test/test-cli-integration.js — 83 passed
- node test/test-knowledge-base.js — 95 passed
- npm run test:python — 1076 passed
- npm run lint, lint:md, format:check — all clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

input.command:
gh pr create --base v2 --head refactor/skf-feature-elevation-foundation --title "refactor(skf-feature): elevate 5 Feature workflows toward Excellent (foundation pass)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Foundation pass mirroring PR #306 for the 5 SKF **Feature workflows** (skf-quick-skill, skf-create-stack-skill, skf-analyze-source, skf-audit-skill, skf-test-skill), bringing them to parity with the Core-elevated peers from PRs #305–#323.

Pre-pass quality scans graded all 5 skills as **Good**; this PR addresses every cross-skill mechanical item identified in those scans:

| Skill | Before | After this PR | After Tightening (follow-up) |
|---|---|---|---|
| skf-quick-skill | Good | Good (foundation parity) | Excellent (target) |
| skf-create-stack-skill | Good | Good (foundation parity) | Excellent (target) |
| skf-analyze-source | Good | Good (foundation parity) | Excellent (target) |
| skf-audit-skill | Good | Good (foundation parity) | Excellent (target) |
| skf-test-skill | Good | Good (foundation parity) | Excellent (target) |

## What changed

### Customization surface (S1 across all 5)

- `customize.toml` shipped per skill: `[workflow]` block with three append-merge arrays (`activation_steps_prepend`, `activation_steps_append`, `persistent_facts`) plus 2–4 asset-path scalars per skill (e.g. `skill_template_path`, `drift_report_template_path`, `scoring_rules_path`).
- `## On Activation` gains a `python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py` step computing path-scalar fallback so stage files reference `{<purpose>Path}` directly — no conditional at the call site.

### Headless contract (E1/E2 across the 4 skills that lacked it)

- `## Exit Codes` table added/lifted to SKILL.md for create-stack, analyze-source, audit-skill, test-skill (mirrors skf-brief-skill).
- `## Result Contract (Headless)` block added with single-line envelope prefixes: `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON`, `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`, `SKF_AUDIT_RESULT_JSON`, `SKF_TEST_RESULT_JSON`. Mirror-on-stderr for HARD HALTs.

### Language headers (C1 across 50+ reference files)

- Each `references/*.md` gains `<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->` after the frontmatter close — survives compaction when SKILL.md is dropped mid-flow.
- Artifact-producing steps get the `Artifact text in {document_output_language}.` variant.

### UX cancel-line (B6 across QS/SS/AN)

`[X] Cancel and exit` wired to exit code 6 on interactive gates:
- skf-quick-skill: `resolve-target.md`
- skf-create-stack-skill: `rank-and-confirm.md`, `compile-stack.md`
- skf-analyze-source: `scan-project.md`, `identify-units.md`, `recommend.md`

### Bug fixes folded in (Broken items, all 1-line)

- skf-quick-skill SKILL.md: `{forger_root}` was an undefined variable (no module declares it), silently breaking headless detection via `preferences.yaml`. Replaced with `{sidecar_path}`.
- Bare `_bmad/skf/` references in `health-check.md` frontmatter comments now correctly prefixed with `{project-root}/` (4 skills).
- skf-test-skill `references/report.md`: bare `_bmad/agents/` and `.claude/skills/` in the `--discovery-catalog=all` prose now prefixed.

### Workflow Rules tightened (A5/A6/A7)

- Dropped defensive "main-thread fallback" bullets covered by per-step fallback wording (AN, AS, TS).
- Dropped LLM-native procedural rules ("read each step file completely", "follow the mandatory sequence exactly") from QS Workflow Rules.

### Skill-specific polish

- skf-create-stack-skill: description trigger expanded to 3 quoted phrases; "Ferris Architect mode" persona clause dropped; Invocation Contract gains Exit codes row.
- skf-audit-skill: Invocation Contract Inputs row enumerates the optional inputs the workflow actually consumes (`skill_path`, `tier_override`, `degraded`, `upstream_drift_choice`, `dirty_worktree_choice`, `force`) rather than just `skill_name`.

### Validator updates (directly motivated by surfaces added in this PR)

- `tools/validate-file-refs.js`: skip `.analysis/` analysis dirs (gitignored runtime state); add `agents/` to `INSTALL_ONLY_PATHS` so the test-skill `--discovery-catalog=all` reference to `{project-root}/_bmad/agents/` resolves cleanly.

## Deferred (substantive items — separate Tightening PR + tracking issue)

- 4 Broken items needing design judgment: SS cross-dir `./references/{library}.md` template bug, QS ecosystem-check exit-envelopes, AN orphaned "Pattern N" references, AN nonexistent `knowledge/tool-resolution.md`
- Reference-file boilerplate cleanup in test-skill (~150-line delete across 7 step files)
- Low-severity sweep across all 5 skills (slug-list dedup, no-op directives, etc.)

## Deferred (tracking issue — out of elevation scope)

- New shared scripts: `scan-manifests.py`, `pair-intersect.py`, `compare-file-hashes.py`, `scan-skill-md-structure.py`, `disqualify-candidates.py`, `enumerate-skills.py`, `load-provenance.py`
- compute-score.py unit tests + argparse migration
- Decision-Log Workspace resume protocols for stack/audit
- New optional headless modes (`--no-discovery`, `--no-health-check`, `--tier=`)
- Carving Batch Mode out of skf-quick-skill SKILL.md
- Subagent fan-out for AN map-and-detect per-unit
- `{document_output_language}` second-pass threading where deferred

## Test plan

- [x] `node tools/validate-skills.js` — 0 findings (15 skills scanned)
- [x] `node tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict` — 0 broken refs, 0 abs-path leaks (189 files / 124 refs)
- [x] `node test/test-installation-components.js` — 179 passed
- [x] `node test/test-workflow-state.js` — 38 passed
- [x] `node test/test-cli-integration.js` — 83 passed
- [x] `node test/test-knowledge-base.js` — 95 passed
- [x] `npm run test:python` — 1076 passed
- [x] `npm run lint`, `lint:md`, `format:check` — all clean
- [x] pre-commit hook full suite — all green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.content:
{"body":"## Background

PR #306 shipped the **mechanical/parity** elevation work for the three remaining Core workflow skills (customize.toml × 3, language headers × 27, U4 conventions doc, B6 cancel-line). This issue tracks the **deferred substantial work** identified in the same quality-analysis pass that informed PR #306.

Source quality-analysis reports (local, gitignored):
- `skills/reports/skf-brief-skill/quality-analysis/20260515T115954/quality-report.md` (graded Excellent)
- `skills/reports/skf-create-skill/quality-analysis/20260515T120003/quality-report.md` (graded Good — primary lift target)
- `skills/reports/skf-update-skill/quality-analysis/20260515T120017/quality-report.md` (graded Excellent)

## Workstream A — 9 shared script extractions

Each extraction follows the pattern established by `skf-detect-tools.py` / `skf-merge-ccc-exclusions.py` in PR #305 (`49937672`): PEP 723 inline metadata, JSON I/O contract, paired test in `test/test-skf-*.py`, `uv run` invocation from stage prompts.

### A1. `skf-description-guard.py` (S3 — cross-skill, highest value)

Currently duplicated as ~30 lines of identical 4-phase protocol prose in:
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/validate.md` §0 "Description Guard Protocol" (line 31) and §8 "Description Guard" evidence-report block (line 232)
- `src/skf-update-skill/references/write.md` §0 "Description Guard Protocol" (line 22) and §7 evidence-report block (line 151)

**Proposed subcommands:**
- `capture <skill-md>` → emits `{ guarded_description, schema_hash }` JSON; LLM stashes in context before any SKILL.md-modifying tool call
- `verify-restore <skill-md> --captured <description>` → re-reads on-disk, compares, restores if divergent, emits `{ restored: bool, tool: str?, diff: str? }`

**Companion file:** `src/shared/references/description-guard-protocol.md` — single source of truth for the 4-phase prose; both skills' stage files load it via frontmatter and stop restating the protocol inline.

### A2–A6. update-skill deterministic ops (U1)

Per quality-report A1 "Lift Deterministic Operations into Scripts" (HIGH):

| Script | Extract from | Subcommand sketch |
|---|---|---|
| `skf-build-change-manifest.py` | `skf-update-skill/references/detect-changes.md` §3 | inputs: provenance map + current source tree → emits change manifest JSON |
| `skf-provenance-gap-dispatch.py` | `references/detect-changes.md` (§1c gap-driven dispatcher) | reads drift report + brief scope → emits add/keep/drop classifications |
| `skf-hash-content.py` | scattered SHA-256 prose | `hash <path>` → emits sha256 hex |
| `skf-check-workspace-drift.py` | `references/remote-source-resolution.md` (git guard) | wraps `git status --porcelain` checks, emits clean/dirty enum |
| `skf-update-active-symlink.py` | `references/write.md` (atomic symlink update) | atomic relink via temp + rename; emits before/after target |

### A7–A9. create-skill deterministic ops (C2)

Per quality-report C2 "Script delegation for deterministic ops" (HIGH):

| Script | Extract from | Subcommand sketch |
|---|---|---|
| `skf-detect-scripts-assets.py` | `skf-create-skill/references/extract.md` §4c | walks source tree → classifies scripts vs assets per skill-sections.md rules |
| `skf-resolve-authoritative-files.py` | `references/extract.md` §2a (authoritative-files protocol, ~95 lines) | resolves brief `scope.include` + canonical-doc heuristics → ranked file list |
| `skf-validate-brief-schema.py` | `references/load-brief.md` §3 | validates loaded brief against `src/shared/scripts/schemas/skill-brief.v1.json` (already exists — script just wraps `jsonschema` invocation with skill-friendly error formatting) |

## Workstream B — structural changes in create-skill

### B1. Carve `extract.md` §2a to its own file (C3, medium)

`src/skf-create-skill/references/extract.md` is 379 lines. §2a "Authoritative Files Protocol" embedded inside it accounts for ~95 lines. Carve to `references/authoritative-files-protocol.md` and load via frontmatter. Same pattern applies to `extraction-patterns.md` (430 lines) and `skill-sections.md` (395 lines) if appetite.

### B2. Parallelize gh fetches (C4, medium — 5–18s wall-clock)

- `references/sub/fetch-temporal.md` §3 — runs 4 independent `gh` fetches sequentially. Convert to `xargs -P 4` or background+wait pattern.
- `references/sub/fetch-docs.md` §3 — runs up to 10 subpage fetches sequentially. Same pattern.

## Workstream C — headless improvements in update-skill (U2, U3)

### C1. `headless_decisions[]` in result contract (U2)

Currently auto-resolutions are logged inline as evidence-report lines but not surfaced as a structured field. Add a top-level `headless_decisions[]` array to `src/shared/scripts/schemas/skf-update-result-envelope.v1.json` (file may not yet exist — model on `skf-setup-result-envelope.v1.json`); each entry is `{ gate, default_action, taken_action, reason }`.

### C2. `--dry-run` and `--detect-only` flags (U2)

- `--dry-run`: run detect-changes + plan, emit envelope, skip write/merge
- `--detect-only`: run detect-changes only, emit change manifest, exit

Mirror the CLI-knob pattern from skf-setup (`--orphan-action`, `--ccc-skip-index`, `--quiet` — see commit `3dd864cd`).

### C3. Concurrency lock (U3)

Two simultaneous update-skill runs against the same `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` can corrupt provenance. Implement via `flock` on a lock file at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/.skf-update.lock`. Pattern already in use in `skf-create-skill/references/source-resolution-protocols.md:87` (workspace concurrency guard) — mirror it. Acquire on entry, release on exit. Headless mode should fail-fast if lock held; interactive should prompt.

## Workstream D — brief-skill wiring (B1)

`customize.toml` ships three asset-path scalars (`description_voice_examples_path`, `scope_templates_path`, `brief_schema_path`). Now wire them in `src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md` activation so non-empty scalars override the bundled defaults. The bundled paths are:

- `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
- `assets/scope-templates.md`
- `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`

## Deferred indefinitely (need design conversation, not in this issue)

These were classified as speculative in the PR #306 scope cut:

- **B2:** "Brief from preset" interactive entry
- **B3:** "Preview create-skill output" hop between confirm and write
- **B4:** Workspace-list `'list'` pagination spec or removal
- **B5:** `skf-count-sentences.py` helper (~150 tok/run — low value)
- **C6 UX polish:** rename-orphans cleanup, version-overwrite diff, Quick-tier upgrade hint
- **C5 (create-skill `[workflow.gate_defaults]`):** Needs first to enumerate the actual gates in `skf-create-skill`'s workflow — done by reading SKILL.md's gate-handling prose, not invented.

## Validation gates (same as PR #306)

- `node tools/validate-skills.js`
- `node tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict`
- `node test/test-installation-components.js`
- `node test/test-workflow-state.js`
- Python test suites for new scripts under `test/test-skf-*.py`

## Effort estimate

~4–5 hours focused work for Workstream A (9 scripts × ~30 min each including tests), ~2 hours for Workstreams B/C/D combined. Recommend tackling A1 (Description Guard) first since it eliminates duplication and unblocks both create-skill and update-skill wiring.

Tracks: PR #306, PR #305","title":"Elevate skf-core skills — PR #2: shared scripts + headless improvements + §2a carve"}

**Proposed subcommands:**
- `capture <skill-md>` → emits `{ guarded_description, schema_hash }` JSON; LLM stashes in context before any SKILL.md-modifying tool call
- `verify-restore <skill-md> --captured <description>` → re-reads on-disk, compares, restores if divergent, emits `{ restored: bool, tool: str?, diff: str? }`

**Companion file:** `src/shared/references/description-guard-protocol.md` — single source of truth for the 4-phase prose; both skills' stage files load it via frontmatter and stop restating the protocol inline.

### A2–A6. update-skill deterministic ops (U1)

Per quality-report A1 "Lift Deterministic Operations into Scripts" (HIGH):

| Script | Extract from | Subcommand sketch |
|---|---|---|
| `skf-build-change-manifest.py` | `skf-update-skill/references/detect-changes.md` §3 | inputs: provenance map + current source tree → emits change manifest JSON |
| `skf-provenance-gap-dispatch.py` | `references/detect-changes.md` (§1c gap-driven dispatcher) | reads drift report + brief scope → emits add/keep/drop classifications |
| `skf-hash-content.py` | scattered SHA-256 prose | `hash <path>` → emits sha256 hex |
| `skf-check-workspace-drift.py` | `references/remote-source-resolution.md` (git guard) | wraps `git status --porcelain` checks, emits clean/dirty enum |
| `skf-update-active-symlink.py` | `references/write.md` (atomic symlink update) | atomic relink via temp + rename; emits before/after target |

### A7–A9. create-skill deterministic ops (C2)

Per quality-report C2 "Script delegation for deterministic ops" (HIGH):

| Script | Extract from | Subcommand sketch |
|---|---|---|
| `skf-detect-scripts-assets.py` | `skf-create-skill/references/extract.md` §4c | walks source tree → classifies scripts vs assets per skill-sections.md rules |
| `skf-resolve-authoritative-files.py` | `references/extract.md` §2a (authoritative-files protocol, ~95 lines) | resolves brief `scope.include` + canonical-doc heuristics → ranked file list |
| `skf-validate-brief-schema.py` | `references/load-brief.md` §3 | validates loaded brief against `src/shared/scripts/schemas/skill-brief.v1.json` (already exists — script just wraps `jsonschema` invocation with skill-friendly error formatting) |

## Workstream B — structural changes in create-skill

### B1. Carve `extract.md` §2a to its own file (C3, medium)

`src/skf-create-skill/references/extract.md` is 379 lines. §2a "Authoritative Files Protocol" embedded inside it accounts for ~95 lines. Carve to `references/authoritative-files-protocol.md` and load via frontmatter. Same pattern applies to `extraction-patterns.md` (430 lines) and `skill-sections.md` (395 lines) if appetite.

### B2. Parallelize gh fetches (C4, medium — 5–18s wall-clock)

- `references/sub/fetch-temporal.md` §3 — runs 4 independent `gh` fetches sequentially. Convert to `xargs -P 4` or background+wait pattern.
- `references/sub/fetch-docs.md` §3 — runs up to 10 subpage fetches sequentially. Same pattern.

## Workstream C — headless improvements in update-skill (U2, U3)

### C1. `headless_decisions[]` in result contract (U2)

Currently auto-resolutions are logged inline as evidence-report lines but not surfaced as a structured field. Add a top-level `headless_decisions[]` array to `src/shared/scripts/schemas/skf-update-result-envelope.v1.json` (file may not yet exist — model on `skf-setup-result-envelope.v1.json`); each entry is `{ gate, default_action, taken_action, reason }`.

### C2. `--dry-run` and `--detect-only` flags (U2)

- `--dry-run`: run detect-changes + plan, emit envelope, skip write/merge
- `--detect-only`: run detect-changes only, emit change manifest, exit

Mirror the CLI-knob pattern from skf-setup (`--orphan-action`, `--ccc-skip-index`, `--quiet` — see commit `3dd864cd`).

### C3. Concurrency lock (U3)

Two simultaneous update-skill runs against the same `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` can corrupt provenance. Implement via `flock` on a lock file at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/.skf-update.lock`. Pattern already in use in `skf-create-skill/references/source-resolution-protocols.md:87` (workspace concurrency guard) — mirror it. Acquire on entry, release on exit. Headless mode should fail-fast if lock held; interactive should prompt.

## Workstream D — brief-skill wiring (B1)

`customize.toml` ships three asset-path scalars (`description_voice_examples_path`, `scope_templates_path`, `brief_schema_path`). Now wire them in `src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md` activation so non-empty scalars override the bundled defaults. The bundled paths are:

- `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
- `assets/scope-templates.md`
- `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`

## Deferred indefinitely (need design conversation, not in this issue)

These were classified as speculative in the PR #306 scope cut:

- **B2:** "Brief from preset" interactive entry
- **B3:** "Preview create-skill output" hop between confirm and write
- **B4:** Workspace-list `'list'` pagination spec or removal
- **B5:** `skf-count-sentences.py` helper (~150 tok/run — low value)
- **C6 UX polish:** rename-orphans cleanup, version-overwrite diff, Quick-tier upgrade hint
- **C5 (create-skill `[workflow.gate_defaults]`):** Needs first to enumerate the actual gates in `skf-create-skill`'s workflow — done by reading SKILL.md's gate-handling prose, not invented.

## Validation gates (same as PR #306)

- `node tools/validate-skills.js`
- `node tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict`
- `node test/test-installation-components.js`
- `node test/test-workflow-state.js`
- Python test suites for new scripts under `test/test-skf-*.py`

## Effort estimate

~4–5 hours focused work for Workstream A (9 scripts × ~30 min each including tests), ~2 hours for Workstreams B/C/D combined. Recommend tackling A1 (Description Guard) first since it eliminates duplication and unblocks both create-skill and update-skill wiring.

Tracks: PR #306, PR #305","title":"Elevate skf-core skills — PR #2: shared scripts + headless improvements + §2a carve"}

input.command:
gh issue create --title "Elevate SKF Feature workflows — substantive deferrals from quality-analysis pass" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Background

PR #324 shipped the **mechanical/parity** elevation work for the 5 SKF Feature workflow skills (skf-quick-skill, skf-create-stack-skill, skf-analyze-source, skf-audit-skill, skf-test-skill): customize.toml × 5, language headers across 50+ reference files, Exit Codes + Result Contract sections, `[X]` cancel-lines on interactive gates, bug fixes, defensive-rule trim. A follow-up Tightening PR will sweep the 4 remaining Broken items + low-severity polish.

This issue tracks the **deferred substantial work** identified in the same quality-analysis pass — items requiring new shared scripts, structural carves, new headless surfaces, or design conversations.

Each elevation workstream maps to a peer-Excellent pattern (skf-brief-skill / skf-create-skill / skf-update-skill); references to skill files use bare-path-from-repo-root form.

---

## Workstream A — New shared scripts (LLM → deterministic)

Pattern from PRs #308–#316: each extraction is PEP 723 inline-metadata, JSON I/O contract, paired test in `test/test-skf-*.py`, wired into `test:python`, invoked from stage prompts via `uv run`.

### A1. `skf-scan-manifests.py` (cross-skill — SS + AN)

Replaces ~150 lines of LLM-orchestrated manifest scanning across:
- `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/detect-manifests.md` §2–§3 (8-ecosystem dependency parser)
- `src/skf-analyze-source/references/scan-project.md` §2 (manifest + entry-point + service-config enumeration across `project_paths[]`)

Subcommands: `scan <root> --ecosystems=auto` → emits `{manifests: [{path, ecosystem, deps: [...]}], total_unique: N, monorepo: bool}`.

### A2. `skf-pair-intersect.py` (SS)

Extract from `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/detect-integrations.md` §1 (file-list intersection prune, currently "MANDATORY first pass" implemented in prose).

Subcommands: `intersect --libraries <json>` → emits `{pairs: [{a, b, intersection_count, files: [...]}]}` sorted by intersection size.

### A3. `skf-enumerate-stack-skills.py` (SS)

Extract compose-mode discovery from `src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/parallel-extract.md` §0 (skill discovery + export-manifest fallback + symlink-fallback + cycle guard + metadata-hash). Currently per-skill loop in parent context — heavy implicit-read.

Subcommands: `enumerate <skills-root>` → emits `{skills: [{name, exports, exports_source, confidence, metadata_hash}], cycles: [], warnings: []}`.

### A4. `skf-compare-file-hashes.py` (AS)

Replaces SHA-256 prefix-strip + per-file hash compute prose at `src/skf-audit-skill/references/structural-diff.md` §4b. LLM has no native SHA-256; per-file shell-out today.

Subcommands: `compare <provenance-map.json> <source-root>` → emits `{added: [], removed: [], changed: [{path, stored_hash, current_hash}]}`. Prefix normalization lives in script.

### A5. `skf-load-provenance.py` (AS)

Centralizes provenance-map walk + canonicalization transforms scattered across `src/skf-audit-skill/references/{init,structural-diff}.md`.

Subcommands: `normalize <map.json>` → emits `{bounded_scan_files: [...], is_stack_skill, legacy_stack_provenance, source_root, baseline_commit, baseline_ref, reexport_map: {...}}`.

### A6. `skf-scan-skill-md-structure.py` (TS)

Replaces ~3 LLM-orchestrated checks at `src/skf-test-skill/references/coherence-check.md` §2.1–§2.6: required-section presence (synonym-aware), markdown-fence balance, language-tag scan, table column drift, exports-in-Usage cross-grep.

Subcommands:
- `scan <skill-md>` → emits `{unbalanced_fences, bare_opening_fences[], table_drift[]}`
- `scan <skill-md> --required-sections` → emits `{description: {satisfied, matched_synonym}, usage: {...}, api_surface: {...}}`

### A7. `skf-disqualify-candidates.py` (AN)

Extract boundary disqualification heuristics from `src/skf-analyze-source/references/identify-units.md` (file count < 3, LoC < 100, `node_modules/` / `vendor/` / `dist/` detection).

Subcommands: `filter --boundaries <json>` → emits `{kept: [...], dropped: [{name, reason}]}`.

## Workstream B — compute-score.py modernization (TS)

### B1. Unit tests + argparse migration

`src/skf-test-skill/scripts/compute-score.py` centralizes floor logic that drives correctness (false-PASS catastrophic per scoring-rules.md). Currently:
- No `scripts/tests/` directory
- Raw `sys.argv[1]` parsing, no `--help`
- M3 fixture matrix at `references/scoring-rules.md:113–141` declares fixtures (`test/fixtures/compute-score-contract.json`) that don't exist

**Fix:**
- Create `test/test-compute-score-contract.py` seeded from the M3 matrix's 4 equivalence classes (A/B/C/D) + INCONCLUSIVE floor cases + threshold cap
- Wire into `package.json` `test:python`
- Replace `sys.argv` block with argparse: `--json-input` (positional or stdin), `--help` showing the input schema

## Workstream C — Determinism: subagent fan-out + return-format discipline

### C1. AN `map-and-detect.md` per-unit subagent fan-out

`src/skf-analyze-source/references/map-and-detect.md` §2 references "subprocess Pattern 2 when available" without defining what that means; per-unit analysis is sequential by default. For projects with 10+ qualifying units, this dominates wall-clock.

**Fix:** make the per-unit analysis an explicit parallel fan-out: spawn one subagent per qualifying unit, each returns ONLY `{unit_name, files_count, exports_count, export_pattern, api_surface, scripts_assets, ccc_signals, strategy_used, confidence}`. Parent does not read unit source files before delegating (implicit-read trap).

Also resolve the orphaned "Pattern N" references — either define them inline or drop the labels.

### C2. TS subagent return-format consistency

`src/skf-test-skill/references/coverage-check.md` §1 has the "ONLY return X" discipline. §1b cross-check and contextual-coherence §3–§4 delegate to subagents without re-asserting it.

**Fix:** audit every subagent invocation in `coverage-check.md` and `coherence-check.md`; add `Return ONLY <schema>. No prose, no commentary, no markdown.` to each.

## Workstream D — Headless surface extensions

### D1. New CLI flags (TS)

`src/skf-test-skill/references/report.md` §4b (Discovery Testing) and §7 (Workflow Health Check dispatch) are mandatory. For a CI matrix running test-skill across 50 skills, both dominate wall-clock.

- `--no-discovery` → skip §4b.1–§4b.3 (Discovery Testing block), log Info note
- `--no-health-check` → skip §7 dispatch, exit at §6b
- `--tier=<Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep>` → bypass `forge-tier.yaml` sidecar requirement (for fresh CI shells where setup hasn't run)

### D2. New CLI flags (AN)

`src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md` Invocation Contract currently has no headless arg surface beyond `--headless`.

- `--project-path <path>`
- `--scope-hint <text>`
- `--intent-hint <text>` (also drives the recommendation ranking)

### D3. Workflow scalars

- `default_threshold = 80` in TS customize.toml — wires to score.md §1 reads `{workflow.default_threshold}` and passes into compute-score JSON; CLI override still wins
- `on_complete = ""` hook scalars in TS, AN, AS customize.toml — invoked with `--result-path=...` after atomic write; downstream pipelines (slack, dashboard, CI) snap on without forking

## Workstream E — Structural carves

### E1. Carve Batch Mode out of skf-quick-skill SKILL.md

`src/skf-quick-skill/SKILL.md` includes ~95 lines of Batch Mode + boundary-event JSON sub-schema at SKILL.md scope. Single-target users (the common case) pay weight for this on every activation.

**Fix:** carve to `references/batch-mode.md` with input format, execution loop, summary contract, headless events, exit code. SKILL.md retains a 3–4-line summary + path pointer. Survives compaction because On Activation step 4 explicitly loads when `--batch` is set.

### E2. Carve SS provenance-map schemas to assets/

`src/skf-create-stack-skill/references/generate-output.md` is 328 lines / 3428 tokens. ~80 lines of inline JSON schema templates (provenance-map.json code-mode + compose-mode) could lift to `assets/provenance-map-schema.md` with `{provenanceMapSchema}` frontmatter routing.

## Workstream F — Decision-Log Workspace protocols

### F1. DLW resume protocol for skf-create-stack-skill

10-step workflow with parallel extraction, atomic-write commit, symlink flip. If context compacts between step 4 and step 6 the user loses state. Currently no `.decision-log.md` workspace; re-running rebuilds from scratch.

**Minimum:** add a "if `{forge_version}/in-progress.json` exists, offer to resume from the last completed step" check at the top of `init.md`.

**Full:** write a `.decision-log.md` to `{forge_version}/` as the load-bearing artifact (peer skills do this for the same reason).

### F2. DLW resume / re-audit hint for skf-audit-skill

Drift reports are timestamped — workflow always creates a new one. There's no detection of an existing recent report for the same skill. A user who re-runs after fixing one finding gets a fresh report with no diff against the prior.

**Fix:** at `init.md` §6, before creating the new report, glob `{forge_version}/drift-report-*.md` and if a recent (< 7 days) report exists for the same `audit_ref`, surface a soft gate: `[F] Fresh audit (default) / [D] Diff against prior report / [R] Resume the prior report`. Headless default = F.

## Workstream G — Polish (low ROI, includes only if high-confidence quick wins)

- AN: `intent-before-ingestion` question in `init.md` (low-effort UX win)
- AN: bulk-confirm shortcut in `recommend.md` (`all-Y` / `all-N` then flip exceptions)
- AS: degraded-mode confidence banner in drift-report-template.md header
- TS: spec-ID glossary (B1/B2/.../M5/S8 inline codes currently lack an anchor)

## Validation gates (same as PR #324)

- `node tools/validate-skills.js`
- `node tools/validate-file-refs.js --strict`
- `node test/test-installation-components.js`
- `node test/test-workflow-state.js`
- `node test/test-cli-integration.js`
- `node test/test-knowledge-base.js`
- `npm run test:python` (new test files must be appended to the `test:python` script enumeration in `package.json`)
- `npm run lint`, `lint:md`, `format:check`

## Effort estimate

- Workstream A: ~6 scripts × ~30 min each including tests + wiring ≈ 3–4 hours
- Workstream B: ~45 min (compute-score test file + argparse)
- Workstream C: ~30 min (fan-out wiring + return-format audit)
- Workstream D: ~90 min (3 flags + 2 hook scalars + wiring)
- Workstream E: ~60 min (Batch Mode carve + provenance schemas)
- Workstream F: ~90 min minimum for the resume-hint flavors; full DLW ~3 hours each

Tracks: PR #324
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

Foundation PR #324 has already merged, so SKILL.md and all references/*.md files already have language headers, resolver step, Exit Codes table, Result Contract — do NOT touch those.

CONTEXT:
- Quality findings file (read for context): src/skf-test-skill/.analysis/2026-05-15T19-39-21Z/findings.md (sections A4, A5, A6, A8)
- Calibration peer: src/skf-brief-skill/references/gather-intent.md — note the lean step-file shape (no heavy `## Rules` / `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` scaffolding, just step goal + work + one-line chain).

SCOPE — execute EXACTLY these tasks, nothing else:

1. **A4 — Drop repeated boilerplate scaffolding across 7 step files** (`init.md`, `detect-mode.md`, `coverage-check.md`, `coherence-check.md`, `external-validators.md`, `score.md`, `report.md`)
   - Each currently opens with `## STEP GOAL`, then a `## Rules` block, then a `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` block with a "CRITICAL" warning.
   - Keep `## STEP GOAL` and the actual numbered work that follows.
   - Drop `## Rules` headers and the bullet content beneath them (the bullets are LLM-native or already in SKILL.md's `## Workflow Rules`).
   - Drop `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headers (the steps are already numbered below).
   - If a bullet under `## Rules` carries genuinely load-bearing institutional knowledge (e.g. "Never override the script's `result` value" in score.md, the four-phase Description Guard discipline somewhere), preserve it — relocate to the relevant numbered step inline.

2. **A5 — Collapse Auto-Proceed scaffolding across same 7 step files**
   - Each step ends with both an "Auto-Proceed" subsection (`#### Menu Handling Logic` + `#### EXECUTION RULES`) AND a `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` footer that restates "update frontmatter, then load `{nextStepFile}`."
   - For each auto-proceed step (i.e. all 7 since none are interactive in test-skill), collapse the entire ending block into one closing line: `Update stepsCompleted, then load and execute {nextStepFile}.`
   - Drop the `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE` header, the `#### Menu Handling Logic` subsection, and the `#### EXECUTION RULES` subsection in each file.

3. **A6 — Shrink `references/health-check.md` to ≤6 lines of content** (excluding frontmatter)
   - Currently a 25-line file for a 2-line probe. The probe order is also documented in `report.md` §7.
   - Keep: frontmatter (preserves nextStepFile chain semantics), the language header, ONE concise sentence saying "Probe `{primaryHealthCheck}` then `{fallbackHealthCheck}`; load and execute the first that exists, else HALT with a diagnostic naming both paths." and the closing `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE`-equivalent which now becomes a single line per A5.
   - Drop `## STEP GOAL`, `## Rules`, `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headers; the step goal IS the one sentence above.

4. **A8 — Drop the inline spec-ID codes** (`B1`, `B2`, `B4`, `H1`, `H4`, `M5`, `S6`, `S8`, `S9`)
   - Findings cite their presence in: `init.md`, `score.md`, `report.md`, `coherence-check.md`, `scoring-rules.md`.
   - GREP for the inline forms like `(B4)`, `per B2`, `(H4)`, `(S6)`, `M5`, `S9`, `H1` — they appear as inline parenthetical or "per X" annotations.
   - Drop the annotation. If the surrounding text was load-bearing because of the cross-ref, ensure the prose still reads cleanly without it.
   - Do NOT create a glossary (deferred to tracking issue).

EXECUTION RULES:
- Use Edit tool with precise old_string/new_string. Read each file once before editing.
- After A4+A5 edits, the per-step file should be approximately 50–70% of its current size.
- Preserve all frontmatter exactly (don't break `nextStepFile`, `stepsCompleted`, asset frontmatter scalars).
- Preserve the language-config HTML comment that PR #324 added.
- Preserve the actual numbered work (§1, §2, §3, etc.) — only drop the scaffolding around it.
- If you're unsure whether a bullet under `## Rules` is institutional vs LLM-native, ERR ON THE SIDE OF KEEPING IT (relocate inline into the relevant numbered step).
- Do NOT change behavior — only delete scaffolding and collapse redundant blocks.
- Do NOT modify any file outside src/skf-test-skill/.
- Do NOT modify SKILL.md (Foundation PR already covered Conventions/Workflow Rules edits).
- Do NOT touch customize.toml.
- Do NOT touch compute-score.py (deferred to tracking).
- Do NOT add new scripts/, new headless modes, new shared assets.

RETURN: A concise bullet list of files touched + approximate line delta per file. If you preserved a bullet under `## Rules` because it carried institutional knowledge, name the bullet briefly so I can audit.

   - Keep: frontmatter (preserves nextStepFile chain semantics), the language header, ONE concise sentence saying "Probe `{primaryHealthCheck}` then `{fallbackHealthCheck}`; load and execute the first that exists, else HALT with a diagnostic naming both paths." and the closing `## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE`-equivalent which now becomes a single line per A5.
   - Drop `## STEP GOAL`, `## Rules`, `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` headers; the step goal IS the one sentence above.

4. **A8 — Drop the inline spec-ID codes** (`B1`, `B2`, `B4`, `H1`, `H4`, `M5`, `S6`, `S8`, `S9`)
   - Findings cite their presence in: `init.md`, `score.md`, `report.md`, `coherence-check.md`, `scoring-rules.md`.
   - GREP for the inline forms like `(B4)`, `per B2`, `(H4)`, `(S6)`, `M5`, `S9`, `H1` — they appear as inline parenthetical or "per X" annotations.
   - Drop the annotation. If the surrounding text was load-bearing because of the cross-ref, ensure the prose still reads cleanly without it.
   - Do NOT create a glossary (deferred to tracking issue).

EXECUTION RULES:
- Use Edit tool with precise old_string/new_string. Read each file once before editing.
- After A4+A5 edits, the per-step file should be approximately 50–70% of its current size.
- Preserve all frontmatter exactly (don't break `nextStepFile`, `stepsCompleted`, asset frontmatter scalars).
- Preserve the language-config HTML comment that PR #324 added.
- Preserve the actual numbered work (§1, §2, §3, etc.) — only drop the scaffolding around it.
- If you're unsure whether a bullet under `## Rules` is institutional vs LLM-native, ERR ON THE SIDE OF KEEPING IT (relocate inline into the relevant numbered step).
- Do NOT change behavior — only delete scaffolding and collapse redundant blocks.
- Do NOT modify any file outside src/skf-test-skill/.
- Do NOT modify SKILL.md (Foundation PR already covered Conventions/Workflow Rules edits).
- Do NOT touch customize.toml.
- Do NOT touch compute-score.py (deferred to tracking).
- Do NOT add new scripts/, new headless modes, new shared assets.

RETURN: A concise bullet list of files touched + approximate line delta per file. If you preserved a bullet under `## Rules` because it carried institutional knowledge, name the bullet briefly so I can audit.

# Step 6: Compile Stack Skill

## STEP GOAL:

Assemble the main SKILL.md by combining per-library extractions with the integration layer, and present for user review before writing output files.

## Rules

- Compile SKILL.md following the stack-skill-template structure — integration patterns go first (primary value)
- Do not write output files (Step 07)
- Present compiled content for user review

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.

### 1. Load Template Structure

Load `{stackSkillTemplate}` and prepare SKILL.md section structure.

### 2. Generate Frontmatter

The SKILL.md MUST begin with YAML frontmatter (agentskills.io compliance):

```yaml
---
name: {project_name}-stack
description: >
  Stack skill for {project_name} — {lib_count} libraries with
  {integration_count} integration patterns. Use when working with
  this project's technology stack. NOT for: individual library usage
  outside this project's conventions.
---
```

**Frontmatter rules:**

- `name`: lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match skill output directory name. **Stack skills MUST end in `-stack`** (e.g., `{project_name}-stack`) — this is how consumers (skf-verify-stack, skf-test-skill) detect stack vs individual skills.
- `description`: non-empty, max 1024 chars, trigger-optimized for agent discovery. MUST use third-person voice ("Processes..." not "I can..." or "You can...").
- No other frontmatter fields — only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` are permitted by spec

### 3. Compile Integration Layer

**This is the core value of the stack skill.** Compile in order:

**Zero-integration guard:** If the integration graph from step 05 has zero edges (no detected integration pairs), skip the integration layer compilation and note: "No integration patterns detected — stack skill will contain library summaries without an integration layer." Proceed directly to section 4 (Per-Library Sections).

**Cross-cutting patterns** (if any):
- Patterns spanning 3+ libraries
- Middleware chains, shared configuration, common architectural patterns

**Library pair integrations:**
- For each detected integration pair from step 05:
  - Type classification
  - Pattern description with file:line citations
  - Key files demonstrating the integration
  - Confidence tier label

**Hub library connections:**
- For each hub library (3+ connections):
  - Role in the stack architecture
  - How it connects to partner libraries

### 4. Compile Per-Library Sections

For each confirmed library (ordered by integration connectivity, then import count — **in compose-mode**, order by integration connectivity, then skill confidence tier since import counts are not available):

- Role in stack (one-line description)
- Key exports used in this project
- Usage patterns from extraction
- Confidence tier label
- Link to reference file: `./references/{library}.md`

### 5. Compile Project Conventions

Extract project-specific conventions from the extractions:
- Common initialization patterns
- Error handling approaches across libraries
- Configuration conventions
- Import organization patterns

### 6. Compile Library Reference Index

Create the reference index table:

| Library | Imports | Key Exports | Confidence | Reference |
|---------|---------|-------------|------------|-----------|
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

(**in compose-mode**: replace the Imports column with Export Count from source skill metadata, since import counts are not available)

### 7. Present Compiled SKILL.md Preview

"**Stack skill compilation complete. Please review:**

---

{Display full compiled SKILL.md content}

---

**Compilation stats:**
- **Libraries:** {count}
- **Integration pairs:** {count}
- **Cross-cutting patterns:** {count}
- **Confidence:** T1: {count}, T1-low: {count}, T2: {count}

**Please review the integration layer and per-library sections.**
- Does the integration layer capture how your libraries connect?
- Are the per-library summaries accurate?
- Any sections to adjust before writing output?"

### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS

Display: **Select:** [C] Continue to Output Generation | [X] Cancel and exit

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting compilation
- **GATE [default: C]** — If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [C] Continue, log: "headless: auto-approve stack compilation"
- ONLY proceed to next step when user approves and selects 'C'

#### Menu Handling Logic:

- IF C: Store skill_content, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
- IF X: Invoke the rollback contract (purge any `{forge_data_folder}/{project_name}-stack/{version}/*-tmp` and `*.skf-tmp` staging artifacts under the forge workspace, leave any existing committed stack package untouched), emit the `SKF_STACK_RESULT_JSON` envelope on stderr with `status: "error"`, `halt_reason: "user-cancelled"`, `exit_code: 6`, and exit with code 6
- IF Any other: Process as feedback, adjust compilation, redisplay preview, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)

- Usage patterns from extraction
- Confidence tier label

# Stack Skill Template

## SKILL.md Section Structure

```markdown
---
name: {project_name}-stack
description: >
  Stack skill for {project_name} — {lib_count} libraries with
  {integration_count} integration patterns. Use when working with
  this project's technology stack.
---

# {project_name} Stack Skill

> {lib_count} libraries | {integration_count} integration patterns | Forge tier: {tier}

## Integration Patterns

### Cross-Cutting Patterns
[Patterns that span 3+ libraries — middleware chains, shared config, etc.]

### Library Pair Integrations
[For each detected integration pair:]
#### {LibraryA} + {LibraryB}
**Type:** {pattern_type}
**Pattern:** {description}
**Key files:** {file_list}
**Confidence:** {T1/T1-low/T2}

## Library Reference Index

| Library | Imports | Key Exports | Confidence | Reference |
|---------|---------|-------------|------------|-----------|
| {name} | {count} | {top_exports} | {tier} | [ref](./references/{name}.md) |

## Per-Library Summaries

### {library_name}
**Role in stack:** {one-line description of what this library does in this project}
**Key exports used:** {comma-separated list}
**Usage pattern:** {brief pattern description}
**Confidence:** {T1/T1-low/T2}

## Conventions

[Project-specific conventions for library usage:]
- {convention_1}
- {convention_2}
```

## context-snippet.md Format (Vercel-Aligned)

Indexed format targeting ~80-120 tokens per stack:

```markdown
[{project}-stack v{version}]|root: skills/{project}-stack/
|IMPORTANT: {project}-stack — read SKILL.md before writing integration code. Do NOT rely on training data.
|stack: {dep-1}@{v1}, {dep-2}@{v2}, {dep-3}@{v3}
|integrations: {pattern-1}, {pattern-2}
|gotchas: {1-2 most critical integration pitfalls}
```

## metadata.json Structure

```json
{
  "skill_type": "stack",
  "name": "{project}-stack",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "generation_date": "{ISO-8601}",
  "forge_tier": "{Quick|Forge|Forge+|Deep}",
  "confidence_tier": "{T1|T1-low|T2|T3}",
  "spec_version": "1.3",
  "source_authority": "{official|community|internal}",
  "generated_by": "create-stack-skill",
  "exports": [],
  "library_count": 0,
  "integration_count": 0,
  "libraries": ["lib1", "lib2"],
  "integration_pairs": [["lib1", "lib2"]],
  "language": "{primary language or list of languages from constituent skills}",
  "ast_node_count": "{number-or-omitted-if-no-ast}",
  "confidence_distribution": {
    "t1": 0,
    "t1_low": 0,
    "t2": 0,
    "t3": 0
  },
  "tool_versions": {
    "ast_grep": "{version-or-null}",
    "qmd": "{version-or-null}",
    "skf": "{skf_version}"
  },
  "stats": {
    "exports_documented": 0,
    "exports_public_api": 0,
    "exports_internal": 0,
    "exports_total": 0,
    "public_api_coverage": 0.0,
    "total_coverage": 0.0,
    "scripts_count": 0,
    "assets_count": 0
  },
  "dependencies": [],
  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
}
```

## references/{library}.md Structure

```markdown
# {library_name} Reference

**Version:** {version_from_manifest}
**Import count:** {count} files *(compose-mode: replace with **Export count:** {count} exports)*
**Confidence:** {T1/T1-low/T2}

## Key Exports
[Top exports used in this project with signatures]

## Usage Patterns
[How this library is typically used in this codebase]

## Common Imports
[Most frequent import statements]
```

## references/integrations/{pair}.md Structure

```markdown
# {LibraryA} + {LibraryB} Integration

**Type:** {pattern_type}
**Co-import files:** {count}
**Confidence:** {T1/T1-low/T2 [composed]}

## Integration Pattern
[Detailed description of how these libraries connect]

## Key Files
[Files demonstrating the integration with line references]

## Usage Convention
[How this integration is typically structured in the project]
```

+| {name} | {count} | {top_exports} | {tier} | [ref](references/{name}.md) |
## Per-Library Summaries

# Step 2: Ecosystem Check

## STEP GOAL:

To query the agentskills.io ecosystem for an existing official skill matching the resolved target, preventing unnecessary duplication. This is an advisory gate — it never blocks the workflow on failure.

## Rules

- This check is advisory — never block the workflow on failure
- 5-second timeout on ecosystem queries; tool unavailability is a silent skip, not an error
- Do not begin extraction or compilation

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Query Ecosystem

Search for an existing official skill matching `{repo_name}` in the agentskills.io ecosystem.

**Query methods (try in order):**
1. Search agentskills.io registry for `{repo_name}`
2. Web search: `"agentskills.io" "{repo_name}" skill`

**Apply 5-second timeout.** If query takes longer, treat as no-match.

### 2. Evaluate Result

**If tool unavailable or timeout:**
- Set `ecosystem_status: skip`
- Proceed silently to step 3 (auto-proceed, no message to user)

**If no match found:**
- Set `ecosystem_status: no-match`
- Auto-proceed silently to step 3. Do not display any message — absence of a match is the expected case.

**If match found:**
- Set `ecosystem_status: match`
- Display match details and present conditional menu:

"**Existing official skill found for {repo_name}.**

**Skill:** {matched_skill_name}
**Source:** agentskills.io
**Authority:** official

An official skill already exists. You can:

**[P] Proceed** — Compile a custom community skill anyway (different scope or customization)
**[I] Install** — Install the existing official skill instead (exits this workflow)
**[A] Abort** — Cancel compilation"

### 3. Handle Match Menu (ONLY if match found)

#### Menu Handling Logic:

- IF P: Set `ecosystem_status: match-proceed`, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
- IF I: Display install instructions for the official skill, then end workflow
- IF A: Display "Compilation cancelled." and end workflow
- IF Any other: help user, then redisplay the match menu

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- ONLY display this menu when ecosystem_status is match
- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input when match is found
- **GATE [default: P]** — If `{headless_mode}` and match found: auto-proceed with [P] Proceed (compile custom skill anyway), log: "headless: ecosystem match found, auto-proceeding with custom compilation"
- For no-match and skip cases, auto-proceed without menu

### 4. Auto-Proceed (No Match or Skip)

#### Menu Handling Logic:

- After no-match or skip determination, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- This is an auto-proceed path — no user interaction needed
- Proceed directly to extraction step

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN ecosystem check completes (match with user choice, no-match, or skip) will you load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to proceed to source extraction.

Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable, documented code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:

| Code | Meaning                | Raised by                                                   |
| ---- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0    | success                | step 7 (terminal)                                          |
| 3    | resolution-failure     | step 1 §2c (prose input), step 1 §3 (registry chain failed) |
| 4    | write-failure          | step 5 §2 (deliverable write failed)                       |
| 5    | overwrite-cancelled    | step 5 §1 (user selected [N])                              |
| 6    | user-cancelled         | step 1 §1 ([X] Cancel and exit, or cancel-line affordance); step 4 §6 (user selected [Q]) — originally `compile-cancelled`, generalised to cover any interactive gate |
| 7    | finalize-blocked       | step 6 §1 (active-pointer flip refused — non-link in place) |

Reserved: `validator-missing` may be promoted from advisory log to fatal exit code in a future revision; consumers should not assume code 8+ is unused.

## Result Contract on HARD HALT

In addition to the success-variant result contract written by step 6 §3, every HARD HALT must surface an **error variant** so headless automators don't silently break when `quick-skill-result-latest.json` is missing on failed runs.

**Always (every HARD HALT, regardless of phase)** — emit a single line on **stderr**:

```
SKF_QUICK_SKILL_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","exit_code":<N>,"phase":"<slug>","error":{"code":"<class>","message":"<short>"},"outputs":{},"summary":{},"skill_package":"<path-or-null>"}
```

One line, no pretty-print. Matches the prefix-and-envelope convention used by `skf-emit-result-envelope.py`.

# Step 4: Map Exports and Detect Integrations

## STEP GOAL:

To analyze each qualifying unit's export surface and import graph, detect cross-unit integration points, and flag potential stack skill candidates — completing the analysis foundation needed for recommendations.

## Rules

- Use subprocess Pattern 2 (per-unit deep analysis) when available
- For each qualifying unit, perform thorough export surface analysis — do not shortcut
- Do not make recommendations (Step 05)
- Tier-aware depth: Quick (file-level), Forge (AST), Deep (AST + semantic)

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.

### 1. Load Context

Read {outputFile} to obtain:
- Qualifying units from Identified Units section (names, paths, scope types, languages)
- `forge_tier` from frontmatter

Load {heuristicsFile} for stack skill candidate detection rules.

### 2. Map Export Surfaces Per Unit

DO NOT BE LAZY — For EACH qualifying unit, launch a subprocess (or analyze in main thread) that:

**Size-aware strategy selection:**
- **< 50 files:** Full export scan — analyze every file for exports
- **50-200 files:** Targeted scan — entry points (`__init__.py`, `index.ts`, `lib.rs`) + public modules + barrel exports only
- **200+ files:** Entry-point strategy — analyze top-level entry point for public API surface, list submodule entry points, analyze each submodule entry point only. Report coverage confidence based on percentage of files analyzed.

**Per-unit analysis (scaled by strategy above):**

1. Scans the unit's directory for export/public interface files
2. Counts and categorizes exports based on forge tier:
   - **Quick tier:** Count files by type, identify index/barrel files, list directory structure
   - **Forge tier:** Parse export statements, identify public API surface, count exported functions/classes/types
   - **Forge+ tier:** All Forge analysis plus:
     - If `tools.ccc` is true: run `ccc_bridge.search("{unit_name} exports public API", top_k=15)` — **Tool resolution:** `/ccc` skill search (Claude Code), ccc MCP server (Cursor), or `ccc search` CLI; see `knowledge/tool-resolution.md` — to discover semantically relevant files beyond directory scan
     - Merge CCC-discovered files with scoped file list — files from CCC that are within the unit's directory are added to the analysis queue
     - Record CCC signals in per-unit findings: top 3 CCC-ranked file names (or "—" if no ccc results)
   - **Deep tier:** All Forge analysis plus:
     - ast-grep structural export extraction: `ast-grep -p 'export $$$' --lang typescript` or equivalent per language to build a verified export inventory
     - ast-grep type/interface mapping: `ast-grep -p 'interface $NAME' --lang typescript` or `ast-grep -p 'class $NAME($$$)' --lang python`
     - If QMD available: query for temporal evolution of identified exports (deprecation signals, recent additions, refactoring patterns)
     - Record semantic relationships between exports (which exports reference/depend on each other)
3. Returns structured findings to parent:
   - Export count (files, functions, types, classes)
   - Primary export patterns (barrel exports, direct exports, re-exports)
   - Public API surface size estimate
   - Key entry points
   - Script/asset presence: check for `scripts/`, `bin/`, `assets/`, `templates/` directories and files matching detection signals in `{heuristicsFile}`. Record counts in per-unit findings.
   - Analysis strategy used and coverage confidence

**Per-unit export summary:**

| Unit | Files | Exports | Export Pattern | API Surface | Scripts/Assets | CCC Signals |
|------|-------|---------|----------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|
| {name} | {count} | {count} | {pattern} | {small/medium/large} | {N scripts, M assets or --} | {CCC signals or --} |

### 3. Map Import Graph

For each qualifying unit, analyze inbound and outbound imports:

**Outbound imports (this unit imports from):**
- Which other qualifying units does it depend on?
- Which external dependencies does it use?

**Inbound imports (other units import from this):**
- Which other qualifying units consume this unit's exports?
- How many import sites exist?

**Build cross-reference matrix:**

| Unit | Imports From | Imported By | External Deps |
|------|-------------|-------------|---------------|
| {name} | {list units} | {list units} | {count} |

### 4. Detect Integration Points

Identify cross-unit integration patterns:

**Direct integrations:**
- Unit A imports from Unit B → document the interface boundary
- Shared type definitions across units
- Cross-unit function calls

**For each integration point document:**
- Source unit → Target unit
- Integration type (import, shared types, API call, message passing, shared state)
- Files involved (with paths)
- Coupling strength (tight / loose / indirect)

### 5. Flag Stack Skill Candidates

Using rules from {heuristicsFile}, check for stack skill indicators:

1. **Co-import frequency:** Two or more units imported together in 3+ files
2. **Integration adapter:** A unit exists primarily to bridge two other units
3. **Shared state:** Multiple units read/write to the same data store
4. **Orchestration layer:** A unit coordinates calls across multiple other units

**For each candidate, document:**
- Units involved
- Detection signal
- Recommended stack skill grouping
- Evidence (specific files/lines)

### 6. Present Findings

"**Export Mapping and Integration Detection Complete**

**Export Map Summary:**
{Per-unit export summary table}

**Cross-Reference Matrix:**
{Import graph matrix}

**Integration Points:** {count}
{List each integration with source → target, type, coupling}

**Stack Skill Candidates:** {count}
{List each candidate with units involved and detection signal}

**Observations:**
- {Key architectural patterns observed}
- {Tightly coupled areas}
- {Loosely coupled areas ideal for independent skills}

Does this analysis look complete? Any integration patterns I should investigate further?"

Wait for user feedback. Adjust analysis based on user input.

### 7. Append to Report

Append the complete "## Export Map" section to {outputFile}:
Replace `[Appended by map-and-detect]` under Export Map with:
- Per-unit export summary table
- Export pattern analysis

Append the complete "## Integration Points" section to {outputFile}:
Replace `[Appended by map-and-detect]` under Integration Points with:
- Cross-reference matrix
- Integration point details
- Stack skill candidate flags

Update {outputFile} frontmatter:
```yaml
stepsCompleted: [append 'map-and-detect' to existing array]
lastStep: 'map-and-detect'
stack_skill_candidates: [{list flagged candidate groupings}]
```

### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS

Display: "**Select:** [C] Continue to Recommendations | [D] Discover Additional Source"

#### Menu Handling Logic:

- IF C: Save findings to {outputFile}, update frontmatter, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
- IF D: Accept a new repo path/URL from the user. Run a lightweight scan (directory structure + manifest detection from step 02) and classify (unit identification from step 03) for the new source only. Merge results into the existing report — append new units to the unit list, update `project_paths[]` in frontmatter. Then redisplay this step's export mapping for the new units before returning to the menu.
- IF Any other: help user, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
- ONLY proceed to next step when user selects 'C'

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN both the Export Map and Integration Points sections have been appended to {outputFile} with complete findings, and frontmatter stepsCompleted and stack_skill_candidates have been updated, will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin recommendations.
